Defines the scope of credentialing activities this committee type is authorized to review — initial credentialing, recredentialing, both, sanctions/disciplinary actions, or appeals of adverse decisions.

The voting methodology used by this committee type to reach credentialing decisions — simple majority, supermajority (two-thirds), unanimous, or consensus-based.

Unique identifier for the access standard record. Primary key.
Reference to the access_standard_id of the newer standard that replaces this one, if status is superseded. Enables version tracking and historical compliance analysis.

Current lifecycle status of the access standard: active (in force and monitored), inactive (no longer enforced), draft (under development), pending_approval (awaiting regulatory or executive approval), superseded (replaced by a newer version).

Indicates whether providers must offer after-hours (evening, weekend, or holiday) appointment availability to meet this access standard. True for urgent care, behavioral health crisis, and certain primary care standards; false for routine specialty care.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards, CMS Medicare Advantage Network Adequacy

description: Defines whether this access standard applies to new patient appointments, established patient follow-ups, specialist consultations, or all appointment types. New patient access is typically more stringent than follow-up access.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards

description: The method used to measure and audit compliance with this access standard: appointment_log_audit (review of provider scheduling systems), member_survey (CAHPS or custom surveys), secret_shopper (test calls to provider offices), provider_attestation (self-reported compliance), claims_analysis (time between referral and service date in claims data).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications, CMS CAHPS Survey Protocols, State DOI Market Conduct Examination Standards

description: The date and time when this access standard record was first created in the system.
The date on which this access standard becomes enforceable. Aligns with regulatory effective dates, contract year start, or accreditation cycle.
reference: CMS Regulatory Effective Dates, NCQA Accreditation Cycle, State DOI Rule Adoption Dates

description: Conditions under which this access standard may be waived or modified (e.g., rural service area with documented provider shortage, member preference for delayed appointment, specialist unavailability due to leave). Used for compliance reporting and grievance resolution.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Exception Process, State DOI Rural Health Exemptions

description: Free-text field for operational notes, implementation guidance, or context for network management and compliance teams. Not disclosed to members or regulators.
The user ID or system account that last modified this access standard record.
The date and time when this access standard record was most recently modified.

Comma-separated list of lines of business to which this access standard applies (e.g., Commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, Exchange/QHP). Some standards are federally mandated for Medicare/Medicaid; others are state-specific or voluntary for commercial plans.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage and Medicaid Managed Care Regulations, State DOI Commercial Market Rules, ACA QHP Network Adequacy Standards

description: The maximum number of calendar days allowed between the member's request for an appointment and the scheduled appointment date. For urgent care, this is typically 1-2 days; for routine care, 7-30 days depending on specialty and regulatory requirements.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards, State DOI Network Adequacy Rules, CMS Medicare Advantage and Medicaid Managed Care Access Standards

description: For emergent or crisis access standards, the maximum number of hours allowed for appointment availability (e.g., behavioral health crisis must be seen within 6 hours). Null for standards measured in days.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Behavioral Health Access Standards, State Mental Health Parity Requirements

description: The member age cohort to which this access standard applies: pediatric (under 18), adult (18-64), geriatric (65+), or all. Pediatric and geriatric populations often have stricter access requirements.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage and Medicaid Managed Care Access Standards, NCQA HEDIS Age Stratification

description: How often compliance with this access standard is measured and reported: monthly (for high-risk or CMS-mandated standards), quarterly (typical for HEDIS and accreditation), semi_annual, annual (for internal benchmarks), or continuous (real-time monitoring via appointment systems).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Reporting Schedule, CMS Quality Reporting Timelines, State DOI Annual Filing Requirements

description: Indicates whether meeting this access standard contributes to network adequacy compliance calculations for regulatory filings. True for standards tied to CMS or state DOI network adequacy requirements; false for internal quality benchmarks.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Requirements 42 CFR 438.68, State DOI Network Adequacy Filing Requirements

description: Description of the consequences for failing to meet this access standard: regulatory sanctions, accreditation downgrade, member grievance escalation, provider contract breach, financial penalties, or corrective action plan requirements.
reference: CMS Compliance and Enforcement, NCQA Accreditation Standards, State DOI Market Conduct Rules

description: Specific regulatory or accreditation standard citation (e.g., 42 CFR 438.206, NCQA HEDIS AAP measure, State Insurance Code Section 1367.03). Provides traceability to the authoritative source.
reference: CMS Code of Federal Regulations, NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications, State Insurance Codes

description: The governing body or accreditation organization that mandates or recommends this access standard: NCQA (National Committee for Quality Assurance), URAC (health care accreditation), CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services), state_doi (State Department of Insurance), aca (Affordable Care Act QHP standards), internal (health plan's own standards exceeding regulatory minimums), joint_commission (for facility-based care). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ncqa|urac|cms|state_doi|aca|internal|joint_commission — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NCQA HEDIS, URAC Health Plan Accreditation, CMS Network Adequacy Requirements, State DOI Regulations, ACA Section 1311

description: The provider specialty or service category to which this access standard applies (e.g., Primary Care, Cardiology, Orthopedics, Behavioral Health, Obstetrics). May be null for standards that apply across all specialties.
reference: AMA Physician Specialty Codes, CMS Specialty Taxonomy

description: Business identifier code for the access standard, used for external reference and reporting (e.g., ROUTINE_PCP, URGENT_SPEC, BH_CRISIS).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards

description: Human-readable name of the access standard (e.g., Routine Primary Care Appointment, Urgent Specialty Care, Behavioral Health Crisis Access).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards

description: Classification of the access standard by care urgency and category: routine (non-urgent scheduled care), urgent (same-day or next-day need), emergent (immediate life-threatening), preventive (wellness and screening), behavioral_health (mental health and substance use), specialty (specialist consultation).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Access and Availability Standards, CMS Network Adequacy Requirements

description: Indicates whether telehealth (virtual) appointments satisfy this access standard in lieu of in-person visits. True for most routine and behavioral health standards post-COVID; may be false for certain procedures requiring physical examination.
reference: CMS Telehealth Policy, State Telehealth Parity Laws, NCQA Telehealth Accreditation Standards

description: The date on which this access standard is no longer in effect, either due to regulatory change, standard revision, or plan policy update. Null for currently active standards.
reference: CMS Regulatory Sunset Provisions, NCQA Standard Revisions

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Adequacy assessments are performed to meet a specific regulatory obligation for network adequacy compliance.

Indicates whether providers in the assessed specialty or facility type are accepting new patients. True if accepting, False otherwise.

reference: NCQA HEDIS Getting Care Quickly Measure

description: Date on which the network adequacy assessment was conducted or completed.
Business identifier for the adequacy assessment, used for external reference and regulatory submissions.
End date of the evaluation period covered by this assessment.
Start date of the evaluation period covered by this assessment.

Type of network compliance evaluation performed: adequacy (provider count and ratio), access-survey (member experience survey), mystery-shopper (secret shopper call study), gap-analysis (deficiency identification), time-distance (geographic access compliance), or appointment-availability (wait time measurement).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, NCQA HEDIS Access Measures

description: Name of the individual or team responsible for conducting the network adequacy assessment.
Organization or department that conducted the assessment (e.g., internal network management team, third-party consultant, regulatory auditor).
Additional notes, observations, or context related to the network adequacy assessment, including qualitative findings or special circumstances.

Overall compliance result of the network adequacy assessment: compliant (meets all standards), non-compliant (fails standards), partially-compliant (meets some but not all standards), under-review (pending final determination), or remediation-required (corrective action needed).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Regulations

description: Timestamp when the adequacy assessment record was first created in the system.
Type of healthcare facility evaluated in this assessment (e.g., hospital, urgent care, skilled nursing facility (SNF), ambulatory surgery center, imaging center).
reference: CMS Provider Taxonomy, NAIC Facility Classification

description: Indicates whether network gaps or deficiencies were identified during the assessment. True if gaps exist, False otherwise.
Severity level of identified network gaps: critical (immediate member access risk), high (significant access limitation), moderate (minor access concern), or low (minimal impact).
reference: Internal Risk Assessment Framework, CMS Compliance Standards

description: Calculated ratio of enrolled members to available providers in the assessed specialty or facility type, expressed as members per provider.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, NCQA HEDIS

description: Number of in-network providers or facilities available in the assessed specialty or facility type within the service area.
Minimum number of providers or facilities required by regulatory standards to meet network adequacy thresholds for the assessed specialty or facility type.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Requirements

description: Type of regulatory body or framework for which this assessment is being conducted: CMS Medicare, CMS Marketplace, State Department of Insurance (DOI), NCQA accreditation, internal monitoring, or ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) certification.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Requirements, State DOI Regulations, NCQA Standards, ACA Section 1311

description: Description of corrective actions taken to address identified network gaps, such as provider recruitment, contract expansion, telehealth deployment, or member outreach.
Target date by which remediation actions must be completed to resolve identified network gaps and achieve compliance.
reference: CMS Corrective Action Plan Requirements, State DOI Regulations

description: Current status of remediation efforts: not-started (action plan pending), in-progress (corrective actions underway), completed (actions finished), verified (compliance confirmed), or overdue (past due date).
Medical specialty or provider type being evaluated in this assessment (e.g., primary care, cardiology, orthopedics, behavioral health, pediatrics).
reference: AMA Physician Specialty Codes, CMS Provider Taxonomy

description: Date on which the network adequacy assessment was submitted to the regulatory body (CMS, State DOI, NCQA).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Filing Deadlines, State DOI Reporting Requirements

description: Status of the regulatory submission: draft (not yet submitted), submitted (under review), accepted (approved by regulator), rejected (not approved), or resubmission-required (corrections needed).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Filing Process, State DOI Submission Standards

description: Methodology used to conduct the assessment: member survey (CAHPS or custom), secret shopper call (mystery shopper study), provider survey (direct provider inquiry), administrative data (claims and enrollment analysis), or geospatial analysis (GIS mapping).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications, CAHPS Survey Protocol

description: Indicates whether telehealth or virtual care options were offered as an alternative during the assessment. True if telehealth was available, False otherwise.
reference: CMS Telehealth Expansion Guidelines, ACA Section 1311

description: Percentage of members in the service area who have access to the assessed provider specialty or facility type within the required time and distance standards.
Maximum distance in miles that members should travel to access the assessed provider specialty or facility type, as defined by regulatory standards.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Time and Distance Standards

description: Maximum travel time in minutes that members should travel to access the assessed provider specialty or facility type, as defined by regulatory standards.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Time and Distance Standards

description: Timestamp when the adequacy assessment record was last modified in the system.
Average number of days members wait for a routine appointment with the assessed provider specialty, measured through survey or mystery shopper methodology.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Getting Care Quickly Measure, CAHPS Survey

description: Average number of days members wait for an urgent appointment with the assessed provider specialty, measured through survey or mystery shopper methodology.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Getting Care Quickly Measure, CAHPS Survey

description: Unique identifier for the network adequacy gap record. Primary key.
Reference to the parent network adequacy assessment that identified this gap.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Identified adequacy gaps are addressed within the context of the specific provider contract; required for remediation tracking.
Reference to the health plan affected by this network adequacy gap.
Reference to the geographic service area where this adequacy gap exists.
Reference to the provider network in which this adequacy gap exists.
Actual date on which this adequacy gap was resolved and the network met the applicable adequacy standard. Null if gap remains unresolved.
Estimated number of plan members residing in the affected geographic area who are impacted by this adequacy gap.
Number of providers or facilities of the specified type currently available in the network in this geographic area. Null if standard is not count-based.
Timestamp when this adequacy gap record was first created in the system.
Date on which the regulatory exception or access plan waiver was approved by CMS or the state DOI. Null if no exception has been granted.
Date on which the granted regulatory exception or access plan waiver expires and the network must meet the full adequacy standard. Null if no exception has been granted or if exception is permanent.
Indicates whether a regulatory exception or access plan waiver has been granted by CMS or the state DOI for this adequacy gap.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Requirements

description: Business rationale and supporting documentation for the regulatory exception or access plan waiver request (e.g., rural area with limited provider availability, specialty shortage area designation, alternative access arrangements).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Requirements

description: Date on which a regulatory exception or access plan waiver was formally requested for this adequacy gap. Null if no exception has been requested.
Indicates whether a regulatory exception or access plan waiver has been requested from CMS or the state DOI for this adequacy gap.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Requirements

description: Numeric difference between required and available provider count (required minus available). Positive values indicate shortfall. Null if standard is not count-based.
Business identifier for the adequacy gap, used for tracking and reporting purposes.
Severity classification of the adequacy gap based on magnitude of shortfall, number of affected members, and criticality of the specialty or service type. Used to prioritize remediation efforts.
Current lifecycle status of the adequacy gap indicating whether it has been identified, is being remediated, has been resolved, or has received a regulatory exception or waiver.

Classification of the adequacy gap by the dimension of network adequacy that fails to meet standards (specialty provider shortage, facility type shortage, geographic coverage gap, time and distance standard violation, appointment wait time standard violation, or essential community provider requirement shortfall).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, NAIC Model Act #74

description: Code identifying the specific geographic area affected by this adequacy gap (e.g., FIPS county code, ZIP code, CBSA code).
reference: U.S. Census Bureau FIPS Codes, USPS ZIP Codes

description: Human-readable name of the geographic area affected by this adequacy gap (e.g., county name, city name, region name).
Type of geographic unit used to define the area affected by this adequacy gap (county, ZIP code, Core-Based Statistical Area, service area, or custom region).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, U.S. Census Bureau Geographic Standards

description: Date on which this network adequacy gap was first identified during an adequacy assessment.

Accepting indicates open to new patients, not accepting indicates closed panel, accepting existing only indicates existing patient family members only, waitlist indicates accepting with delay, unknown indicates status could not be determined.

Indicates whether the provider accepting new patients status was verified during this verification event. True if status was confirmed or updated, false if not verified or unable to verify. Critical for member access and network adequacy reporting.

Indicates whether the provider practice address was verified during this verification event. True if address was confirmed or updated, false if not verified or unable to verify.
Free-text summary of changes identified during verification. Describes what directory fields were updated and the nature of the changes. Nullable if no changes were identified.
Indicates whether any changes to directory information were identified during this verification event. True if updates were needed, false if all information was confirmed as accurate.

Calendar year for which this verification counts toward annual directory accuracy compliance requirements. Used for regulatory reporting and multi-year trend analysis.
Number of contact attempts made before achieving verification outcome. Tracks effort required to reach provider and supports operational efficiency analysis.
Date and time when this directory verification record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.
Date and time when the provider directory was updated based on verification findings. Nullable if no directory update was required. Used to track compliance with CMS 90-day update requirement.

Indicates whether the provider directory was updated as a result of this verification. True if changes were applied to the directory, false if no updates were made. Tracks compliance with 90-day directory update mandate.
Date of the first contact attempt for this verification cycle. Used to measure time-to-verification and operational efficiency.
Date of the most recent contact attempt for this verification cycle. Used to track verification completion timeline.
Calculated date by which the next directory verification must be completed to maintain CMS quarterly verification compliance. Typically 90 days from current verification date.

Indicates whether the provider National Provider Identifier was verified during this verification event. True if NPI was confirmed, false if not verified. NPI is the unique provider identifier required by HIPAA.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Filing Identifier (ID)
description: The CMS-assigned identifier for the network adequacy filing or Plan Benefit Package (PBP) submission that includes this plan-network association. Required for Medicare Advantage and Marketplace plans.
Reference to the provider network being associated with the health plan product. Links to the network domain's network configuration entity.

description: Indicates whether this plan-network association includes participation in an Accountable Care Organization arrangement. True means ACO providers are included in this network for this plan; false means no ACO participation.
The date when this plan-network association was certified as meeting network adequacy requirements by the regulatory authority or internal compliance review.
The date of the most recent network adequacy review or assessment for this plan-network association. Used to track compliance monitoring cycles.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Network Adequacy Standards

description: Business identifier code for this plan-to-network association, used in eligibility verification (270/271 EDI transactions) and claims adjudication routing.
Human-readable name for this plan-to-network association, typically combining plan name and network name for operational reference.

Classification of the network's role for this plan. Primary indicates the main network for member access; supplemental provides additional coverage; specialty covers specific service categories; out-of-area supports members outside primary service area; reciprocal represents shared network arrangements; tertiary provides fallback coverage.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Network Adequacy Standards

description: Indicates whether members can be automatically assigned to providers in this network under this plan. Relevant for Medicaid and some Medicare programs that use auto-assignment for members who do not actively select a provider. True means eligible for auto-assignment; false means member must actively select.
reference: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Auto-Assignment Rules

description: Reference to the master contract or agreement document that governs this plan-network association. Links to contract management systems for terms, rates, and legal provisions.
The date and time when this plan-network association record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether providers in this network for this plan should be published in member-facing provider directories and online search tools. True means include in directory; false means exclude (e.g., for internal or specialty networks).
The date when this plan-to-network association becomes active and members can begin accessing providers in this network under this plan.

The line of business this plan-network association serves. Commercial covers employer-sponsored and individual plans; Medicare includes Medicare Advantage; Medicaid covers state programs; Marketplace represents ACA exchange plans; dual eligible serves Medicare-Medicaid beneficiaries; CHIP covers Children's Health Insurance Program.

The market segment this plan-network association targets. Individual covers direct-to-consumer; small group typically 2-50 employees; large group 51-999 employees; jumbo group 1000+ employees; government includes federal and state employee plans; student covers university and college plans.
The number of members currently enrolled in this plan who have access to this network. Used for network adequacy calculations and capacity planning.

Indicates whether this plan-network association meets regulatory network adequacy standards for provider access, time-and-distance requirements, and specialty coverage. Compliant meets all standards; non-compliant fails requirements; under review is being evaluated; exempt has regulatory waiver; conditional meets standards with restrictions.

The tier designation of this network within the plan's tiered network structure. Tier 1 typically offers lowest cost-sharing; tier 2 moderate; tier 3 highest. Preferred, standard, basic, and premium are alternative tier naming conventions used by some plans. Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this plan-network association. Used for operational notes that do not fit structured fields.

Indicates whether this plan provides any coverage for out-of-network services when this network is the member's assigned network. True means out-of-network benefits exist (typical for PPO); false means no out-of-network coverage (typical for HMO and EPO).
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Network Plan Definitions

business_glossary_term: Primary Care Provider (PCP) Selection Required Flag
description: Indicates whether members enrolled in this plan with this network must select a primary care provider. Typically true for HMO and POS plans; false for PPO and EPO plans.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Managed Care Plan Requirements

description: Current lifecycle status of the plan-network association. Active indicates operational; inactive means temporarily disabled; pending awaits regulatory approval or contract execution; suspended indicates temporary hold; terminated means permanently ended.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Network Filing Requirements

description: Indicates whether services rendered by providers in this network under this plan require prior authorization. True means PA is required; false means no PA requirement. This is a network-level default that may be overridden by specific service or provider rules.

Numeric ranking indicating the priority order of this network when multiple networks are associated with the same plan. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used in claims adjudication routing when a member has access to multiple networks.
The number of unique providers (individual practitioners and facilities) participating in this network for this plan. Used for network adequacy reporting and member communications.
Indicates whether members must obtain a referral from their primary care provider to access specialists in this network under this plan. Typically true for HMO plans; false for PPO and EPO plans.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Managed Care Plan Requirements

description: The date when the regulatory authority (CMS or state DOI) approved this plan-network association for use. Required before the association can become effective.
reference: NAIC Model Laws - Plan Filing Requirements

description: The name of the operational system of record that originated this plan-network association data. Typically the Core Administration Platform (Facets, QNXT) or Provider Management System (Cactus, ProviderSource).

Indicates whether provider performance in this network impacts the plan's CMS Star Ratings. Applicable primarily to Medicare Advantage plans. True means network performance affects Star metrics; false means no Star impact.

The date when this plan-to-network association ends. Null indicates an open-ended association. After this date, members can no longer access providers in this network under this plan for new services.
The user identifier or system account that last modified this plan-network association record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this plan-network association record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Indicates whether this plan-network association operates under a value-based care payment model. True means providers in this network are reimbursed based on quality and outcomes; false means traditional fee-for-service.
The user identifier or system account that created this plan-network association record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Unique identifier for the provider network. Primary key for the provider network entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Needed to post network‑level financial transactions (fees, reimbursements) to a dedicated general ledger for audit and reporting.

Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Network Management Vendor Assignment report requires linking each provider network to its contracted vendor for credentialing and data services.
Indicates whether this network includes participation in ACO arrangements. True if ACO providers are part of the network, False otherwise.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Network Identifier (ID)
description: The unique identifier assigned by CMS for Medicare Advantage and Part D networks. Required for all Medicare networks. Format: one letter followed by five digits.
The timestamp when this provider network record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The date when this provider network becomes active and available for member enrollment and provider participation. Start of the network's operational lifecycle.
The number of hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, and other institutional providers in this network. Used for facility access adequacy assessments.
Comma-separated list of states, counties, or service areas where this network operates. Defines the geographic scope of provider availability and member eligibility.
reference: CMS Service Area Filing, State DOI Requirements

description: The date of the most recent internal or regulatory network adequacy review. Used to track compliance with annual review requirements.

The primary business segment this network serves. Commercial (employer-sponsored), Medicare (federal beneficiaries), Medicaid (state beneficiaries), Exchange (ACA marketplace), ASO (Administrative Services Only self-funded).
The current number of members enrolled in plans associated with this network. Used for provider-to-member ratio calculations and capacity planning.

business_glossary_term: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Accreditation Date
description: The date when NCQA accreditation was granted or last renewed for this network. Null if not accredited.

business_glossary_term: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Accreditation Status
description: The current NCQA accreditation status for this network. Accredited (full accreditation), Provisional (conditional approval), Denied (failed accreditation), Not Applicable (not seeking accreditation), Pending (under review).

business_glossary_term: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Accreditation Expiration Date
description: The date when the current NCQA accreditation expires and renewal is required. Null if not accredited.
The date when the most recent network adequacy filing was submitted to CMS or state regulators. Required annually for Medicare Advantage and Exchange plans.
reference: CMS HPMS Network Adequacy Filing Requirements

description: Regulatory assessment of whether the network meets time-and-distance and provider-to-member ratio standards. Adequate (meets all standards), Deficient (fails standards), Conditional (approved with corrective action plan), Pending Review (under regulatory evaluation).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Requirements

description: Short alphanumeric code used to identify the network in operational systems, claims processing, and provider directories. Externally-known business identifier.
Detailed narrative description of the network's design, provider composition, geographic coverage, and unique features. Used in member materials and regulatory filings.
reference: CMS Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) Requirements

business_glossary_term: Network Provider Directory Uniform Resource Locator (URL)
description: The web URL where members and providers can access the online provider directory for this network. Required for ACA and Medicare Advantage compliance.
reference: CMS Provider Directory Requirements, ACA Section 1311

description: The official business name of the provider network (e.g., 'HMO Narrow Network', 'PPO Broad Network', 'Medicare Advantage Network'). Human-readable identifier used in member communications and plan documents.

Current operational status of the provider network. Active (accepting enrollments and claims), Inactive (closed to new enrollments), Suspended (temporary hold), Pending (awaiting regulatory approval), Terminated (permanently closed).

The structural classification of the provider network. HMO (Health Maintenance Organization) requires PCP and referrals; PPO (Preferred Provider Organization) allows out-of-network access; EPO (Exclusive Provider Organization) no out-of-network except emergency; POS (Point of Service) hybrid model; HDHP (High Deductible Health Plan) network; ACO (Accountable Care Organization) value-based network.
reference: NAIC Model Laws, CMS Network Standards

description: The scheduled date for the next network adequacy review. Typically annual for Medicare Advantage and Exchange networks.
Indicates whether the network allows members to access out-of-network providers with cost-sharing. True for PPO and some POS networks, False for HMO and EPO.
reference: Network Design Standards, ACA Essential Health Benefits

description: The number of primary care physicians in this network. Key metric for network adequacy and member-to-PCP ratio calculations.
Indicates whether members enrolled in this network are required to select a primary care physician. True for HMO and POS networks, False for PPO and EPO.
The total number of unique providers (physicians, facilities, ancillary) participating in this network. Used for network adequacy calculations and member communications.
Indicates whether specialist visits require a referral from the PCP. True for HMO networks, False for PPO, EPO, and most POS networks.
The date when the network received regulatory approval from CMS or state Department of Insurance to begin operations.
reference: State DOI Licensing Requirements, CMS HPMS Approval Process

description: The geographic granularity at which this network is defined. Statewide (entire state), Regional (multi-county region), County (county-level), ZIP (ZIP code level), National (multi-state or nationwide).
The number of specialist physicians in this network. Used for specialty access adequacy assessments.

The CMS Star Rating for Medicare Advantage networks, ranging from 1.0 to 5.0 stars. Based on quality measures including network access, member satisfaction, and clinical outcomes. Null for non-Medicare networks.
The date when this provider network ceases operations and is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for open-ended networks. Existing members may have run-out periods.
reference: CMS Network Filing Requirements, State DOI Regulations

description: The tiering structure of the network for cost-sharing purposes. Tier 1 (lowest cost-sharing, narrow network), Tier 2 (moderate cost-sharing), Tier 3 (highest cost-sharing, broadest access), Single Tier (no tiering), Tiered (multi-tier structure).
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this network record. Used for audit and accountability.
The timestamp when this provider network record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether this network includes value-based care arrangements with providers (e.g., shared savings, bundled payments, capitation). True if VBC arrangements exist, False otherwise.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this network record. Used for audit and accountability.
Unique identifier for the provider network termination record. Primary key for the termination entity.

Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Termination Appeal Process allows providers or members to appeal a termination; the termination record must reference the related appeal case.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Termination of network participation is tied to the underlying provider contract; needed for settlement and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocates termination settlement and related financial impacts to a cost center for accurate financial statements.
Party that initiated the termination: provider (voluntary), health plan (involuntary), mutual (both parties agreed), or regulatory authority (mandated by state DOI or CMS).
Reference to the provider being terminated from the network.
Reference to the provider network from which the provider is being terminated.

Number of members currently in active treatment episodes (e.g., ongoing therapy, pregnancy, post-surgical care) with the terminated provider. These members may be eligible for extended continuity-of-care benefits.
Result of the provider appeal: upheld (termination stands), overturned (termination reversed), modified (terms changed), withdrawn (provider withdrew appeal), or pending (not yet resolved).
Date on which any provider appeal or grievance was resolved. Null if no appeal was filed or if appeal is still pending.
Indicates whether the provider filed an appeal or grievance contesting the termination. True = appeal filed; False = no appeal filed.
Final date through which claims for pre-termination services will be accepted at in-network rates. Calculated as termination effective date plus claims runout period days.

Number of days after termination effective date during which claims for services rendered prior to termination will be accepted and processed at in-network rates. Typically 90-180 days per contract terms.
reference: Claims Processing Standards, Network Contract Terms

description: Date through which existing patients may continue to receive in-network benefits from the terminated provider under continuity-of-care provisions. Required under No Surprises Act and state regulations.
reference: No Surprises Act, State DOI Continuity of Care Requirements

description: Final date of the underlying provider contract. May differ from termination effective date if contract allows for early termination or extension provisions.
Timestamp when the termination record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for compliance and operational tracking.

Indicates whether the termination was due to credentialing issues such as license revocation, malpractice history, sanctions, or failure to meet credentialing standards. True = credentialing-related; False = other reason.
Date on which the provider termination becomes effective and the provider is no longer considered in-network for new services. Critical for claims adjudication and member communication.
reference: Network Contract Standards, CMS Network Adequacy Requirements

description: Date on which final financial settlement was completed between the health plan and terminated provider.

Indicates whether a financial settlement or reconciliation is required between the health plan and provider upon termination. True = settlement required; False = no settlement required. Common for capitated or risk-sharing arrangements.
Indicates whether the termination was for-cause due to contract breach, quality issues, fraud, or other serious violations. True = for-cause termination; False = without-cause termination.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated the termination record. Part of audit trail.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the termination record. Part of audit trail for compliance and operational tracking.
Number of active members assigned to or receiving care from the terminated provider at the time of termination. Used to assess continuity-of-care obligations and member notification requirements.
Date on which affected members were notified of the provider termination. Required for regulatory compliance tracking.
Method used to notify affected members of the termination: mail (postal letter), email, member portal, phone call, or multiple channels.

Indicates whether member notification is required by regulation or contract. True = notification required; False = no notification required. CMS and state DOI regulations mandate notification for certain termination types.
reference: CMS Member Communication Requirements, State DOI Regulations

description: Indicates whether the termination impacts network adequacy standards for time-and-distance or provider-to-member ratios. True = adequacy impact; False = no adequacy impact. Triggers network adequacy re-assessment.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State DOI Network Requirements

description: Free-text field for additional context, internal notes, or special handling instructions related to the termination. May include details of negotiations, legal considerations, or operational impacts.
Date on which formal written notice of termination was provided to the provider or plan, as required by contract terms and regulatory requirements.
Ten-digit unique identifier assigned by CMS to healthcare providers. Denormalized for operational efficiency and regulatory reporting.
Date on which the provider was removed from online and printed provider directories. CMS requires directory updates within specified timeframes.

Standardized code indicating the specific reason for termination. Examples include quality concerns, credentialing failure, contract breach, provider relocation, retirement, network restructuring, or regulatory action.
Detailed narrative explanation of the termination reason, providing context beyond the reason code. May include specific incidents, performance issues, or business rationale.
Date on which the termination was reported to the applicable regulatory authority (state DOI, CMS, NCQA). Required for compliance audit trail.
Reference number or confirmation code from the regulatory authority acknowledging receipt of the termination report.

Indicates whether the termination must be reported to regulatory authorities such as state DOI or CMS. True = reporting required; False = no reporting required. For-cause and credentialing-related terminations typically require reporting.
reference: State DOI Reporting Requirements, CMS Oversight Standards

description: Indicates whether the health plan must recruit a replacement provider to maintain network adequacy. True = replacement required; False = no replacement required.
Business-assigned unique reference number for the termination event, used for tracking and correspondence.

Current lifecycle status of the termination record: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized but not yet effective), effective (termination is active), completed (all post-termination obligations fulfilled), cancelled (termination rescinded).

Classification of the termination event indicating whether it was voluntary (provider-initiated), involuntary (plan-initiated), for-cause (breach or quality issue), contract non-renewal, credentialing revocation, or mutual agreement.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created the termination record. Part of audit trail.
Unique identifier for the network tier. Primary key.
User identifier or system account that created this network tier record.

Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Network tier definitions are scoped to a specific provider network; adding provider_network_id FK establishes the parent relationship.
Percentage coinsurance differential for this tier. Used when cost_share_differential_type is coinsurance or hybrid. Null if not applicable.
Fixed dollar copay differential for this tier compared to baseline tier. Used when cost_share_differential_type is copay or hybrid. Null if not applicable.
Type of cost-sharing differential applied to this tier (copay, coinsurance, deductible, hybrid, or none). Determines how member out-of-pocket (OOP) costs are calculated.
Timestamp when this network tier record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the plan deductible applies to services rendered by providers in this tier before cost-sharing begins.
Member-facing display label for the tier shown in EOB, member portal, and Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC). May differ from internal tier name for clarity.
reference: ACA Summary of Benefits and Coverage Requirements

description: Date when this network tier configuration becomes active and applicable to member cost-sharing and provider network assignments.

Internal operational notes or comments about this tier configuration. Not visible to members or external parties.
User identifier or system account that last modified this network tier record.
Timestamp when this network tier record was last modified.
Description of any member steerage incentives or rewards offered for using providers in this tier (e.g., reduced cost-sharing, waived deductible, wellness credits).
Indicates whether providers in this tier count toward CMS network adequacy standards and state Department of Insurance (DOI) access requirements.
Indicates whether member cost-sharing for this tier counts toward the plan Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) limit.
Indicates whether services from providers in this tier require Prior Authorization (PA) or Utilization Management (UM) review.
Indicates whether this tier is designated as a quality tier based on HEDIS, STAR ratings, or other quality performance metrics.
Indicates whether members must obtain a referral from their Primary Care Provider (PCP) to access providers in this tier. Typically applies to HMO and POS plans.
Reference number or identifier for the state Department of Insurance (DOI) or CMS filing that approved this tier structure. Used for compliance audit trails.

business_glossary_term: Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) Disclosure Text
description: Standardized disclosure text for this tier that appears in the Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) document provided to members under ACA requirements.
reference: ACA Summary of Benefits and Coverage Requirements

description: Defines which provider specialties this tier applies to (all, primary care only, specialist only, facility, ancillary, behavioral health, or custom list). Used to segment tier assignment rules by provider type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|primary_care|specialist|facility|ancillary|behavioral_health|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date when this network tier configuration is terminated and no longer applicable. Null if currently active.
Business identifier code for the network tier (e.g., TIER1, TIER2, TIER3, OON). Used in system integrations and member communications.
Official name of the network tier as defined in plan documents and contracts.

Numeric ranking of the tier indicating preference and cost-share level. Lower rank indicates preferred tier with lower member cost-sharing (e.g., 1=Tier 1 Preferred, 2=Tier 2 Standard, 3=Tier 3 Specialty, 99=Out-of-Network).
Current lifecycle status of the network tier configuration (active, inactive, pending approval, suspended, or retired).
Classification of the tier indicating its strategic purpose within the network design (preferred, standard, specialty, out-of-network, value-based, narrow network).
Indicates whether this tier is eligible for Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) performance incentives and shared savings arrangements.
Unique identifier for the ACO provider association record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns ACO provider‑related expenses and shared‑savings allocations to a cost center for proper accounting.
Reference to the primary service location or facility where this provider delivers care under the ACO arrangement. Used for geographic attribution and network adequacy.
Reference to the parent ACO arrangement that this provider participates in. Links to the ACO contract or arrangement entity.
The number of members currently attributed to this provider for ACO performance measurement and shared savings calculations.
The method used to assign members to this provider for ACO performance measurement and shared savings calculations.
The tier level assigned to this provider for care coordination responsibilities and support services within the ACO. Higher tiers indicate greater care management obligations.
The cost efficiency or resource utilization score for this provider within the ACO arrangement. Used for shared savings calculations. Expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).
The date and time when this ACO provider participation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.
The credentialing status of this provider for ACO participation. Providers must maintain active credentialing to remain eligible for attribution and shared savings.
The date when this provider's participation in the ACO arrangement becomes active and member attribution begins.
Indicates whether this provider's EHR system is integrated with the ACO's health information exchange for care coordination and quality reporting. True if integrated.
The date when the provider formally enrolled or was onboarded into the ACO arrangement. May differ from effective_date if there is a waiting period.
Free-text field for internal comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this provider's ACO participation. Not shared externally.
The date when the provider's credentials were last verified for ACO participation. Used to track recredentialing cycles.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that most recently modified this ACO provider participation record. Audit trail field.
The date and time when this ACO provider participation record was most recently modified. Audit trail field.
The date when the provider's credentials are due for renewal or recredentialing for continued ACO participation.
The maximum number of members that can be attributed to this provider under the ACO arrangement. Null if no limit is defined.
The functional role this provider plays within the ACO structure. Determines attribution rules and quality measure accountability.
Current lifecycle status of the provider's participation in the ACO arrangement. Determines whether the provider is eligible for attribution and shared savings.
The level of financial risk and participation commitment for this provider within the ACO arrangement.
The calendar year for which this provider participation record applies. Used for year-over-year performance tracking and historical analysis.
Indicates whether this provider serves as a primary care physician (PCP) for attribution purposes within the ACO. True if the provider is eligible to be an attribution anchor.
The 10-digit National Provider Identifier for the individual provider participating in the ACO. Used when the participant is an individual practitioner.
The 9-digit Tax Identification Number for the provider group or organization participating in the ACO. Used when the participant is a group practice or facility.
Indicates whether this provider is required to submit quality measure data as part of ACO performance reporting. True if the provider must report HEDIS or CMS Star measures.
The composite quality performance score for this provider within the ACO arrangement. Used for performance-based incentive calculations. Expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00).
The percentage of financial risk (upside and downside) that this provider assumes under the ACO arrangement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 for 25%).
Indicates whether this provider is eligible to receive shared savings distributions based on ACO performance. True if the provider participates in financial upside.
The primary specialty or taxonomy code for this provider within the ACO context. Used for role-based performance measurement and network adequacy assessment.
The date when this provider's participation in the ACO arrangement ends. Null if participation is ongoing.
The reason code indicating why the provider's participation in the ACO was terminated. Null if participation is active or pending.
The specific CMS MSSP track or value-based care program track that this provider participates in under the ACO arrangement. Determines risk level and savings methodology.
reference: CMS Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) Tracks

description: The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this ACO provider participation record. Audit trail field.
Primary key for vbc_arrangement
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tracks VBC shared‑savings and risk‑adjustment costs against a specific cost center for financial consolidation.
Foreign key linking to network.vbc_program. Business justification: A VBC arrangement implements a defined VBC program; adding vbc_program_id FK ties arrangement to its program definition.
Human-readable name of the VBC arrangement or ACO program (e.g., 'Metro Health ACO MSSP Track 1', 'Regional P4P Quality Incentive Program').
Business identifier or contract number assigned to this VBC arrangement. Externally-known reference used in communications with providers and CMS.
Current lifecycle status of the VBC arrangement. Tracks progression from draft through active participation to termination or completion.

Classification of the VBC arrangement model. Distinguishes between Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) tracks, Next Generation ACO, Realizing Equity Access and Community Health (REACH) ACO, commercial pay-for-performance (P4P), episode-based payment, capitation, bundled payment, or other VBC models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mssp_track_1|mssp_track_2|mssp_track_3|next_gen_aco|reach_aco|commercial_p4p|episode_based_payment|capitation|bundled_payment|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Innovation Center VBC Model Taxonomy

description: Number of health plan members or Medicare beneficiaries attributed to this ACO or VBC arrangement for the performance period. Used for PMPM calculations and risk adjustment.

Method used to assign members to the ACO or provider group. Prospective assigns at year start; retrospective assigns at year end based on utilization; voluntary alignment allows member choice; claims-based uses historical claims patterns.
Target cost or quality benchmark value established for the performance period. Expressed in dollars for cost benchmarks or as a quality score for quality benchmarks.

Method used to establish the cost or quality benchmark against which performance is measured. Options include historical baseline (prior year costs), regional average, national trend, risk-adjusted PMPM, or episode target price.

Indicates whether the VBC arrangement requires formal care coordination programs (care management, transitional care, chronic disease management). True if required; False if optional or not applicable.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) ACO ID
description: CMS-assigned identifier for Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) or other federally-recognized ACO arrangements. Null for commercial VBC programs.
Indicates whether this arrangement requires formal reporting to Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS). True for all Medicare ACOs; False for commercial-only VBC programs.
Timestamp when this VBC arrangement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement exists between the health plan and the ACO or provider group for claims, clinical, and quality data exchange. True if agreement is in place; False otherwise.
Date when the VBC arrangement ends or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended arrangements. Aligns with CMS performance year end for Medicare ACOs.
Date when the VBC arrangement becomes active and performance measurement begins. Aligns with CMS performance year start for Medicare ACOs.

business_glossary_term: Health Information Technology (IT) Certification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether participating providers must use certified Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems and meet health IT standards. True if required; False if not required.
Timestamp when this VBC arrangement record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Minimum percentage of cost overruns before shared losses are assessed. Provides a buffer zone for two-sided risk arrangements.
reference: CMS MSSP Minimum Loss Rate Standards

description: Minimum percentage of cost savings required before shared savings are paid. Acts as a threshold to ensure savings are statistically significant.
reference: CMS MSSP Minimum Savings Rate Standards

description: Legal name of the Accountable Care Organization or provider group participating in this VBC arrangement. Null for program template records.
Number of individual providers (physicians, specialists, facilities) participating in this ACO or VBC arrangement. Used for network adequacy and capacity planning.
Tax Identification Number of the participating ACO or provider organization. Used for financial settlement and IRS reporting.
Dollar amount of financial guarantee or bond required from the ACO or provider group to participate in two-sided risk arrangements. Ensures ability to repay shared losses.
Calendar year for which performance is measured and financial reconciliation is calculated. Aligns with CMS performance year for Medicare ACOs.
Number of primary care physicians participating in this ACO or VBC arrangement. Critical for attribution and care coordination requirements.
Name or identifier of the quality measure set used to evaluate clinical performance (e.g., 'CMS ACO Quality Measures', 'HEDIS Comprehensive', 'Custom Commercial P4P Measures').
reference: CMS ACO Quality Reporting Standards, NCQA HEDIS

description: Minimum quality performance score required to earn shared savings or avoid penalties. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 70.00 for 70th percentile).

Frequency at which financial performance is reconciled and shared savings or losses are calculated and settled. Most Medicare ACOs reconcile annually; commercial arrangements may reconcile more frequently.
Distinguishes whether this record represents a program definition template (reusable VBC program design) or a specific participation instance (actual arrangement with a provider group or ACO).

Financial risk-sharing model of the arrangement. One-sided risk allows shared savings only; two-sided risk includes shared savings and losses; upside-only is gain-sharing; downside risk is penalty-only; full capitation transfers all risk.
Geographic description of the service area covered by this VBC arrangement (e.g., 'Statewide California', 'Metro Atlanta Region', 'National Medicare Population').
Percentage of cost overruns above benchmark that the ACO or provider group is responsible for under two-sided risk models. Null for one-sided risk arrangements.
Percentage of cost savings below benchmark that the ACO or provider group earns as shared savings. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).
Name of the operational system from which this VBC arrangement record originated (e.g., 'Cactus Provider Management', 'Custom VBC Platform', 'CMS ACO Portal').
Number of specialist physicians participating in this ACO or VBC arrangement. Used for network adequacy and specialty care access analysis.
Name of the organization sponsoring or administering the VBC program (e.g., health plan name, CMS for federal programs, employer group for commercial arrangements).
Maximum financial loss amount the ACO or provider group is responsible for under two-sided risk arrangements. Provides downside risk protection. Null for one-sided risk or full capitation models.
Actual date the VBC arrangement was terminated or withdrawn. Null for active or completed arrangements. Distinct from effective_end_date which is the scheduled end.

Business reason for early termination of the VBC arrangement (e.g., 'Poor Financial Performance', 'Provider Withdrawal', 'Regulatory Non-Compliance', 'Mutual Agreement'). Null for arrangements that completed their term.
Unique identifier for the value-based care program. Primary key.
The method used to assign members or patients to providers or ACOs for performance measurement and accountability.
The method used to calculate the cost or quality benchmark against which performance is measured. PMPM = Per Member Per Month.
The base year or period used to establish the performance benchmark for this program.
Indicates whether participating providers must implement formal care coordination programs as a condition of participation.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Program Identifier
description: The official CMS-assigned identifier for this VBC program, used for regulatory reporting and tracking. CMS = Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
Primary email address for inquiries and communications regarding this VBC program.
The date and time when this VBC program record was first created in the system.
The date when this VBC program design becomes active and available for provider participation.
Comma-separated list of provider types eligible to participate in this VBC program (e.g., PCP, Specialist, Hospital, SNF). PCP = Primary Care Provider, SNF = Skilled Nursing Facility.
Description of health IT capabilities required for participation (e.g., EHR certification, HIE connectivity, data reporting). EHR = Electronic Health Record, HIE = Health Information Exchange.
Free-text field for internal operational notes, special considerations, or program-specific guidance.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this record.
The date and time when this VBC program record was most recently modified.
The insurance product line or market segment to which this VBC program applies. LOB = Line of Business.
The minimum percentage loss threshold that must be exceeded before shared losses are assessed. MLR = Minimum Loss Rate. Distinct from Medical Loss Ratio.
The minimum number of attributed members required for a provider or ACO to participate in or qualify for this VBC program.
The minimum percentage savings threshold that must be achieved before shared savings are paid. MSR = Minimum Savings Rate.
The date when the performance measurement period for this VBC program ends.
The date when the performance measurement period for this VBC program begins.

description: Broad category of value-based arrangement that this program represents. ACO = Accountable Care Organization.
Unique business identifier code for the VBC program used across systems and external reporting.
Detailed narrative description of the VBC program objectives, structure, and key features.
Full business name of the value-based care program.
The internal business unit or department responsible for managing and administering this VBC program.
Current operational status of the VBC program.
Classification of the VBC program model. MSSP = Medicare Shared Savings Program, REACH = Realizing Equity Access and Community Health, NextGen = Next Generation ACO, P4P = Pay for Performance.
Web address for the program information page, provider portal, or documentation site.

How often financial reconciliation and shared savings/loss settlements are calculated and paid.
The percentage of losses above benchmark that is shared with participating providers, expressed as a percentage. Null for upside-only models.
The percentage of savings below benchmark that is shared with participating providers, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).
The organization or regulatory body that sponsors and governs this VBC program. CMS = Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
The date when this VBC program design is terminated or discontinued. Null for active programs.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this record.
Primary key for benefit_accumulator
Identifier for the employer group or sponsor associated with the member's pharmacy benefit. Relevant for group-level accumulator reporting and ASO/TPA arrangements.
Identifier for the health benefit plan under which the member is enrolled, determining the applicable deductible, OOP maximum, and Part D benefit phase thresholds.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156; CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP)

description: Unique identifier for the health plan member whose pharmacy benefit accumulators are being tracked. Links to the member master record in the enrollment system (Facets/QNXT).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; CMS Medicare Managed Care Manual

description: Identifier of the most recent pharmacy claim that triggered an update to this accumulator record. Supports audit trail and real-time adjudication reconciliation.
Date on which the accumulator balances were last reset to zero, typically at the start of a new benefit year. Supports annual reconciliation and audit of accumulator lifecycle.
reference: CMS Part D Annual Reconciliation; ACA Benefit Year Rules

description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated or last updated this accumulator record (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM, Facets, QNXT). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312

description: Current lifecycle state of the accumulator record. 'active' indicates in-use for real-time adjudication; 'suspended' indicates member eligibility gap; 'closed' indicates benefit period ended; 'pending_reset' indicates awaiting annual reset; 'reconciled' indicates CMS or plan reconciliation completed.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; HIPAA EDI 835

description: Optimistic locking version counter incremented with each update to the accumulator record. Supports concurrent adjudication event handling and prevents race conditions in real-time point-of-sale updates.
reference: Internal Data Architecture Standard; NCPDP Real-Time Adjudication Guidelines

description: Last date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Defines the boundary for annual accumulator reset.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §147.126; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters

description: First date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Accumulators reset at the start of each new benefit period.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §147.126; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters

description: Cumulative drug cost amounts applied toward the Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold. Tracks member spend after exiting the coverage gap and entering catastrophic coverage.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; 42 CFR §423.104

description: The Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold amount for the current benefit year. Once TrOOP reaches this level, the member qualifies for catastrophic cost-sharing rates. Published annually by CMS.
reference: CMS Part D Benefit Parameters Annual Update; 42 CFR §423.104

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Reconciliation Status
description: Status of the CMS annual Part D reconciliation process for this accumulator record. Tracks whether the accumulator data has been submitted to and accepted by CMS via RAPS/EDPS for risk adjustment and subsidy reconciliation.
reference: CMS Part D RAPS/EDPS Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.329

description: Indicates the coordination of benefits (COB) role of this plan for the member's pharmacy benefit. Determines how accumulator amounts are applied when multiple payers are involved.
reference: NCPDP COB Standard; HIPAA COB Rules; CMS Part D COB Guidelines

description: Cumulative manufacturer discount amounts applied to brand-name drugs while the member is in the Medicare Part D coverage gap (donut hole). These discounts count toward TrOOP under the ACA Coverage Gap Discount Program.
reference: ACA §3301; CMS Coverage Gap Discount Program; 42 CFR §423.2305

description: Date and time when this accumulator record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: HIPAA Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Cumulative dollar amount the member has applied toward their pharmacy deductible in the current benefit period. Updated with each pharmacy claim adjudication event. Used at point of sale to determine member cost-sharing responsibility.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: Total pharmacy deductible amount the member must satisfy before plan cost-sharing begins, as defined in the benefit plan design for the current benefit period.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP)

description: Indicates whether an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) has been generated for the member reflecting the current accumulator balances. Supports member communication and regulatory EOB delivery requirements.

Cumulative dollar amount applied toward the family-level pharmacy deductible for plans with embedded or aggregate family deductible structures. Applicable for commercial group plans with family coverage tiers.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; IRS Notice 2004-50 (HSA family deductible rules)

description: Cumulative out-of-pocket spend applied toward the family-level MOOP for plans with family OOP limits. Tracks combined member cost-sharing across all family members enrolled under the same plan.

The formulary drug tier level most recently applied during adjudication for this member's accumulator. Indicates the cost-sharing tier structure in effect, which affects deductible and OOP accumulation rates.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

business_glossary_term: Health Savings Account (HSA) Eligible Applied Amount
description: Cumulative amount of pharmacy cost-sharing that qualifies as HSA-eligible expenses under IRS rules for members enrolled in a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). Supports member HSA contribution and tax reporting.

Cumulative total drug costs (plan paid plus member cost-sharing) applied toward the Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL). When this amount reaches the ICL threshold, the member enters the coverage gap (donut hole).
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; 42 CFR §423.100

description: The Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL) threshold amount for the current benefit year, as published annually by CMS. Defines the boundary between the initial coverage phase and the coverage gap (donut hole).
reference: CMS Part D Benefit Parameters Annual Update; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Indicates whether the member has fully satisfied their pharmacy deductible for the current benefit period. When true, plan cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) applies rather than full deductible responsibility.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters

description: Indicates whether the member has reached their maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit for the current benefit period. When true, the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters

description: Date and time of the most recent pharmacy claim adjudication event that updated this accumulator record. Critical for real-time point-of-sale cost-sharing calculation and concurrency control.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard D.0; CMS Part D Real-Time Adjudication Requirements

description: The line of business (LOB) under which the member's pharmacy benefit is administered. Determines applicable regulatory framework, benefit phase rules, and accumulator logic (e.g., Part D coverage gap applies only to Medicare Part D).
reference: CMS Medicare Part D; ACA Exchange Rules; FEHB Program Handbook

description: The applicable LIS copay tier for the member, determining the fixed copay amounts for generic, preferred brand, non-preferred, and specialty drugs under the Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) program.
reference: CMS Part D LIS Copay Schedule; 42 CFR §423.782

description: Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) eligibility level for Medicare Part D members, determining reduced cost-sharing amounts. 'full' indicates 100% subsidy; 'partial_1/2/3' indicate graduated subsidy tiers; 'none' indicates no LIS. Affects TrOOP accumulation rules.
reference: CMS Part D LIS Program; 42 CFR §423.780; SSA §1860D-14

description: Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied for drugs dispensed through mail-order pharmacy channels in the current benefit period. Supports mail-order utilization reporting and member EOB generation.
reference: CMS Part D Mail Order Requirements; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: The maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) dollar threshold the member must reach before the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period. Set per plan design and ACA/CMS requirements.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters Annual Update

description: Cumulative out-of-pocket (OOP) spend by the member for pharmacy benefits in the current benefit period, including deductible, copays, and coinsurance. Used to track progress toward the maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit.
reference: ACA 45 CFR §156.130; CMS Part D Benefit Parameters

description: Current Medicare Part D benefit phase for the member based on accumulated TrOOP and ICL amounts. Drives cost-sharing calculation at point of sale. 'not_applicable' for non-Part D lines of business.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; 42 CFR §423.100

description: Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used to cross-reference accumulator balances between the health plan and PBM for reconciliation.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Total number of pharmacy claims adjudicated and applied to this accumulator record in the current benefit period. Used for utilization reporting and EOB generation.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D Reporting Requirements

description: Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied specifically for specialty tier drugs in the current benefit period. Tracked separately to support specialty pharmacy reporting, prior authorization analytics, and plan design evaluation.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Tier Rules; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: Cumulative dollar amount paid by third parties (e.g., manufacturer copay assistance programs, other insurance) toward the member's pharmacy cost-sharing. Tracked separately as these amounts may not count toward TrOOP under CMS rules.
reference: CMS Part D TrOOP Rules; 42 CFR §423.100; CMS Manufacturer Coupon Policy

description: Cumulative true out-of-pocket (TrOOP) expenditure for Medicare Part D members, counting only qualifying cost-sharing amounts toward the catastrophic coverage threshold. Excludes amounts paid by third parties not qualifying under CMS TrOOP rules. Required for CMS Part D benefit phase determination.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; 42 CFR §423.100

description: The annual TrOOP threshold amount that, when reached, transitions a Medicare Part D member into catastrophic coverage. Published annually by CMS as part of the Part D benefit parameters.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 5; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Date and time when this accumulator record was most recently modified, reflecting the latest claim adjudication event or manual adjustment. Supports concurrency control and audit trail.
reference: HIPAA Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Primary key for claim_line

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Line‑level pricing and cost allocation depend on the specific PBM contract; the FK supports detailed cost breakdowns for audits and CMS reporting.
Reference to the drug master record for the dispensed drug on this claim line, enabling linkage to formulary, tier, and pricing data.
reference: NDC Drug Master; CMS Part D Drug Data

description: Reference to the formulary under which this drug was adjudicated, determining tier assignment, coverage rules, and cost-sharing obligations.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; PBM Formulary Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry_line. Business justification: Journal Entry Line Creation: each claim line generates a GL journal entry line for detailed expense tracking and audit trails.
Reference to the parent pharmacy claim header to which this line belongs. Establishes the header/detail relationship for multi-line and compound drug claims.
reference: HIPAA EDI 837D Transaction Set; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Reference to the prior authorization record approved for this drug line, required for specialty and non-formulary drugs. Null if no PA was required.
reference: HIPAA EDI 278 Transaction; CMS Part D PA Requirements

description: Date and time when this pharmacy claim line was adjudicated by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Represents the business event timestamp for the adjudication transaction. Used for audit trails and processing SLA measurement.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D PDE Reporting; HIPAA EDI 835

description: Code indicating the pricing benchmark used to determine the ingredient cost for this claim line. Values include Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), Federal Upper Limit (FUL), NADAC, negotiated rate, usual and customary, or other. Critical for PBM audit and CMS Part D compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AWP|WAC|MAC|FUL|NADAC|negotiated|usual_and_customary|other — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates whether this claim line was adjudicated under the catastrophic coverage phase of the Part D benefit, where the plan pays a higher percentage of drug costs after the member's True Out-of-Pocket (TrOOP) threshold is met.
reference: CMS Part D Benefit Phases; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS PDE Reporting
