description: Indicates the payer sequence in a Coordination of Benefits (COB) scenario. 1=primary payer, 2=secondary payer, 3=tertiary payer. Used to correctly apply COB rules and calculate each payer's liability on this claim line.
reference: HIPAA COB Rules; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS COB Requirements

description: Indicates whether this claim line is part of a compounded drug prescription. When true, multiple ingredient lines will exist under the same claim header, each with its own NDC and ingredient cost.
reference: NCPDP Compound Drug Billing Standard; CMS Part D Compound Drug Policy

description: Indicates whether this claim line was adjudicated during the Part D coverage gap (donut hole) phase. Used for manufacturer discount program tracking and CMS Part D benefit phase reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Gap; ACA Coverage Gap Discount Program; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Number of days of medication supply dispensed on this claim line as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for refill-too-soon edits, adherence calculations, and HEDIS medication adherence measures.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; HEDIS Medication Adherence Measures; CMS Part D

description: NCPDP Dispense As Written (DAW) code indicating whether the prescriber or member requested brand-name dispensing when a generic was available. DAW 0=no product selection indicated, DAW 1=substitution not allowed by prescriber, DAW 2=substitution allowed-patient requested brand. Impacts cost-sharing and GDR calculations.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard DAW Codes; CMS Part D DAW Reporting

description: Date the drug was dispensed to the member at the pharmacy. Represents the real-world service event date used for benefit period calculations, days supply tracking, and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D PDE Reporting

description: The fee charged by the dispensing pharmacy for the professional service of dispensing the medication. Negotiated as part of PBM network contracts and reported separately from ingredient cost per CMS Part D requirements.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Network Contract Terms

description: Coverage determination status for this drug on the applicable formulary at time of adjudication. Indicates whether the drug is covered, non-covered, excluded, subject to step therapy, or requires prior authorization (PA).
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Determination; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; ACA Essential Health Benefits

description: NCPDP DUR conflict code identifying the type of drug utilization issue detected during adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, duplicate therapy, high dose). Supports clinical safety monitoring and regulatory DUR reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full NCPDP DUR conflict code set]
reference: NCPDP DUR Standard; CMS Part D DUR Requirements; OBRA 90

description: NCPDP Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code recorded during adjudication, indicating the pharmacist's action taken in response to a DUR alert (e.g., filled as is, filled with prescriber approval, not filled). Supports clinical safety audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full NCPDP DUR outcome code set]
reference: NCPDP DUR Standard; CMS Part D DUR Requirements; OBRA 90

description: Sequential fill number for this prescription across all fills, including original and all refills. Distinct from refill_number in that it counts total fills rather than refill sequence. Used for prescription lifecycle tracking.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; RxClaim/Argus PBM System

description: Drug formulary tier assigned to this drug at the time of adjudication, determining the member's cost-sharing level (e.g., Tier 1 generic, Tier 2 preferred brand, Tier 3 non-preferred brand, Tier 4 specialty). Drives copay/coinsurance calculation.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Formulary Standards; PBM Formulary Design

description: Indicates whether the dispensed drug is a generic equivalent. Used for generic dispensing rate (GDR) reporting, formulary compliance analytics, and PBM performance measurement.
reference: FDA Generic Drug Approval; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; HEDIS GDR Measure

description: Total gross cost of the drug on this claim line, calculated as ingredient cost plus dispensing fee plus sales tax plus incentive amount. Used as the basis for CMS Part D gross drug cost reporting and risk corridor calculations.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Incentive or bonus amount paid to the pharmacy for performance-based or preferred network participation on this claim line. Included in gross drug cost calculations for CMS Part D reporting.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Performance Contract

description: The drug ingredient cost component of the claim line, representing the cost of the drug itself exclusive of dispensing fee, sales tax, and other charges. Key input for MAC/AWP/WAC-based reimbursement calculations and compound drug cost analysis.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; MAC/AWP/WAC Pricing Standards

description: Sequential number identifying the position of this line within the parent pharmacy claim. Used to order compound ingredient lines and COB line splits. Starts at 1.

Current adjudication status of this pharmacy claim line. Drives payment processing, COB resolution, and audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid|denied|reversed|adjusted|pending|void — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; HIPAA EDI 835; CMS Part D

description: Classifies the nature of this claim line: standard single-drug fill, compound ingredient line, COB line split, reversal, or adjustment. Drives compound cost analysis and COB workflows.
reference: NCPDP Compound Drug Billing; HIPAA COB Rules

description: Indicates whether the member received a Low Income Subsidy (LIS) benefit on this claim line, resulting in reduced cost-sharing. Required for CMS Part D PDE reporting and LIS reconciliation.
reference: CMS Part D LIS Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart P; CMS PDE Reporting

description: Amount of manufacturer discount applied to this claim line under the CMS Part D Coverage Gap Discount Program. Counts toward the member's TrOOP and is reported on the PDE record.
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Gap Discount Program; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS PDE Reporting

description: 11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product, strength, and package size dispensed on this claim line. Sourced from FDA NDC Directory. PHI when linked to a member.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; HIPAA EDI 837D; CMS Part D Reporting

description: Amount paid by another payer (e.g., secondary insurer, manufacturer coupon, government program) on this claim line as part of Coordination of Benefits (COB). Used to calculate the plan's net liability.
reference: HIPAA COB Rules; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D COB Requirements

description: Total amount paid by the member/patient at point of sale, including copay, coinsurance, and deductible contributions. Used for Out-of-Pocket (OOP) accumulator tracking and Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) calculations.
reference: ACA OOP Maximum Rules; CMS Part D TrOOP; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: Date this claim line was submitted to CMS as a Prescription Drug Event (PDE) record for Part D reconciliation and risk adjustment. Null for non-Part D claims. Critical for CMS compliance and audit.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS RAPS/EDPS

description: Distribution channel through which the drug was dispensed: retail pharmacy, mail-order pharmacy, specialty pharmacy, long-term care (LTC) pharmacy, or home infusion. Drives network contract application, reimbursement rates, and utilization analytics.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Type Codes; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Network Contract

description: Net amount paid by the health plan on this claim line after applying member cost-sharing, COB adjustments, and other payer amounts. Core financial metric for MLR and PMPM reporting.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; ACA MLR Rules

description: Actual quantity of the drug dispensed (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, grams) as reported by the pharmacy. Used for quantity limit edits, compound ingredient cost allocation, and drug utilization review (DUR).
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D PDE; FDA NDC

description: Indicates whether a quantity limit restriction was applied to this drug claim line during adjudication. When true, the dispensed quantity was subject to plan-defined quantity limit rules.
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Policy; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Formulary Management

description: Refill sequence number for this prescription fill. Zero indicates the original fill; subsequent values indicate refill number. Used for refill-too-soon edits, adherence monitoring, and quantity limit enforcement.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D Refill Rules; State Pharmacy Board Regulations

description: Date and time when this pharmacy claim line was reversed, if applicable. Null for non-reversed lines. Used for financial reconciliation, audit trails, and identifying reversed claims in PMPM and MLR calculations.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D PDE Reversal Reporting; HIPAA EDI 835

description: State or local sales tax applied to this pharmacy claim line where applicable. Reported separately per CMS Part D PDE requirements. Zero or null in states with no drug sales tax.
reference: CMS Part D PDE Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; State Tax Regulations

description: Natural key or transaction identifier for this claim line as assigned by the originating PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for lineage tracing, reconciliation with source system, and EDI transaction matching.
reference: Internal Data Lineage Standard; HIPAA EDI 837D; RxClaim/Argus PBM System

description: Indicates whether the drug dispensed on this line is classified as a specialty drug, typically requiring special handling, storage, or administration and subject to specialty pharmacy channel requirements and PA rules.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Drug Definition; NCQA Specialty Pharmacy Standards; PBM Specialty Drug List

description: Indicates whether this drug claim line was subject to a step therapy protocol requiring the member to try a preferred or lower-cost drug before coverage of this drug was approved.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Rules; NCQA Utilization Management Standards; State Step Therapy Laws

description: Member's True Out-of-Pocket (TrOOP) cost contribution from this claim line, used to track progress toward the Part D catastrophic coverage threshold. Includes patient pay and applicable manufacturer discounts.
reference: CMS Part D TrOOP Rules; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS PDE Reporting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert record generated during pharmacy claim adjudication in the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus).
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements

description: Reference to the pharmacy claim record against which this DUR alert was generated during adjudication. Links the alert to the originating claim transaction.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D; HIPAA EDI 837P

description: Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which the member is enrolled at the time of the DUR alert. Used to apply plan-specific DUR rules, formulary restrictions, and CMS Part D contract reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Contract; HIPAA 45 CFR §162; Facets/QNXT Plan Configuration

description: Unique identifier of the health plan member for whom the DUR alert was triggered. Used to associate the alert with the member's medication history and clinical profile.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; CMS Part D; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 302-C2

description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) to the adjudication transaction that generated this DUR alert. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; RxClaim/Argus PBM System; CMS Part D

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DUR alerts are reviewed by a clinical reviewer employee; audit of DUR decisions needs this link.

Final adjudication outcome of the pharmacy claim after the DUR alert was processed. Indicates whether the claim was paid, rejected due to the alert, overridden by the pharmacist or plan, pending review, or reversed.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D; HIPAA EDI 835

description: Date and time when the DUR alert was generated by the PBM adjudication engine (RxClaim or Argus) during real-time claim processing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D DUR Reporting; RxClaim/Argus PBM System

description: Current lifecycle status of the DUR alert record. 'active'=alert is open and requires action, 'resolved'=alert addressed and closed, 'overridden'=pharmacist or plan overrode the alert, 'suppressed'=alert suppressed per plan rules, 'escalated'=referred for clinical review.
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; CMS Part D DUR Reporting; URAC Pharmacy Benefit Management Standards

description: NCPDP standardized code identifying the category of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert. Common values: DD=Drug-Drug Interaction, DC=Drug-Disease Contraindication, TD=Therapeutic Duplication, PA=Drug-Allergy, LD=Low Dose, HD=High Dose, MN=Drug-Age, CS=Drug-Gender, AD=Additive Toxicity, RF=Early Refill. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DD|DC|TD|PA|LD|HD|MN|CS|AD|RF|LR|DS|PG|MC|ID — promote to reference product]
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; CMS Part D DUR Reporting; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 439-E4

description: Human-readable description of the DUR alert type (e.g., 'Drug-Drug Interaction', 'Therapeutic Duplication', 'High Dose Alert', 'Early Refill'). Complements the alert_type_code for reporting and member communications.
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; CMS Part D DUR Reporting

description: Numeric code from the clinical drug database (First DataBank or Medi-Span) indicating the clinical significance of the drug interaction or contraindication. Ranges from 1 (most severe) to 5 (least severe). Supplements the NCPDP severity_level_code with database-specific grading.
reference: First DataBank (FDB) Drug Interaction Database; Medi-Span Clinical Drug Information; NCPDP DUR Standards

description: Timestamp when the DUR alert record was first created and persisted in the data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Represents the audit trail creation time, distinct from the business event timestamp (alert_generated_timestamp).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; CMS Part D Record Retention; NAIC Data Governance Standards

description: Number of days elapsed since the member's last fill of the same or therapeutically equivalent drug. Key metric for early refill DUR alert generation and medication adherence analysis.
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; CMS Part D DUR Reporting; PBM Early Refill Policy

description: Number of days of medication supply dispensed with the prescription. Used in early refill DUR calculations, quantity limit evaluations, and high-dose alert assessments.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 405-D5; CMS Part D; PBM Formulary Management

description: Date on which the drug was dispensed at the pharmacy. Used to correlate the DUR alert with the dispensing event and for retrospective DUR analysis and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 401-D1; CMS Part D; HIPAA EDI 837P

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacy that dispensed the drug and at which the DUR alert was generated during point-of-sale adjudication.
reference: CMS NPI Registry; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.406; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 201-B1

description: Identifies the clinical drug knowledge database used to generate the DUR alert (e.g., First DataBank (FDB), Medi-Span, Micromedex, Multum, or custom plan rules). Important for audit, reproducibility, and database version tracking.
reference: First DataBank (FDB); Medi-Span; Micromedex; NCPDP DUR Standards; CMS Part D

description: Version or effective date of the clinical drug knowledge database (e.g., FDB or Medi-Span) used at the time the DUR alert was generated. Required for audit trail and regulatory reproducibility of DUR alert decisions.
reference: First DataBank (FDB); Medi-Span; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153

description: Brand or generic name of the primary drug (identified by drug_ndc) that triggered the DUR alert. Denormalized for reporting and pharmacist display without requiring a drug master join.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; First DataBank (FDB); Medi-Span Drug Database

description: 11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) that triggered the DUR alert. Primary drug identifier used in pharmacy adjudication.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 407-D7; CMS Part D

description: Indicates whether the DUR alert was generated as part of a prospective (point-of-sale, real-time), retrospective (post-dispensing, claims analysis), or concurrent (during hospitalization or ongoing therapy) DUR program.
reference: CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153; OBRA '90 DUR Requirements; URAC

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Code Version
description: Version of the ICD coding system used for the icd_diagnosis_code on this DUR alert record (e.g., ICD-10-CM). Required for accurate clinical coding interpretation and regulatory reporting.
reference: WHO ICD Classification; CMS ICD-10 Mandate; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.1002

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Diagnosis Code
description: ICD-10-CM diagnosis code associated with the drug-disease contraindication or drug-condition DUR alert. Identifies the member's condition that conflicts with the dispensed drug. Populated for DC (drug-disease) alert types.
reference: WHO ICD-10-CM; CMS ICD-10 Implementation; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.1002

description: Brand or generic name of the interacting drug involved in a drug-drug interaction or therapeutic duplication alert. Provides human-readable context for pharmacist review and DUR reporting without requiring a drug master lookup.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; First DataBank (FDB); Medi-Span Drug Database

description: 11-digit NDC of the second drug involved in a drug-drug interaction (DDI) or therapeutic duplication DUR alert. Populated when alert_type_code indicates a drug-drug conflict. Null for non-interaction alert types.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; First DataBank (FDB) Drug Interaction Database

business_glossary_term: NCPDP Drug Utilization Review (DUR) Conflict Code
description: Two-character NCPDP standardized DUR conflict code transmitted in the pharmacy claim transaction (Field 439-E4). Identifies the specific clinical conflict detected (e.g., 'DD' for drug-drug interaction). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 439-E4; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153

description: Two-digit NCPDP reject code returned when the DUR alert results in a claim rejection (e.g., '88'=DUR Reject Error). Populated when adjudication_outcome is 'rejected'. Used for claim denial tracking and appeals.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Reject Code List; CMS Part D; HIPAA EDI 835

description: Two-character NCPDP code submitted by the pharmacist to override a DUR alert and allow dispensing (e.g., 'OO'=Other Override, 'OP'=Prescriber Override, 'OR'=Pharmacist Override). Populated when adjudication_outcome is 'overridden'.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Override Segment; CMS Part D DUR Reporting

description: Free-text clinical justification or pharmacist notes entered when overriding a DUR alert. Documents the clinical rationale for proceeding with dispensing despite the alert. Supports audit and retrospective DUR review.
reference: CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153; URAC Pharmacy Standards

description: Reference number of the Prior Authorization (PA) request or approval associated with this DUR alert. Populated when pa_required_flag is True and a PA has been submitted or approved.
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 462-EV; Utilization Management System (InterQual/MCG)

description: Indicates whether the DUR alert outcome requires a Prior Authorization (PA) to be obtained before the drug can be dispensed. True when the alert triggers a PA requirement per plan formulary or step therapy rules.
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization Requirements; NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; URAC Pharmacy Standards

description: Quantity of the drug as written on the original prescription by the prescriber. Compared against quantity_dispensed and plan quantity limits in DUR alert evaluation.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; State Board of Pharmacy Regulations; CMS Part D

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or clinician who ordered the drug associated with the DUR alert. Used for prescriber-level DUR reporting and outreach.
reference: CMS NPI Registry; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.406; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 411-DB

description: Date the prescription was originally written by the prescriber. Used in early refill DUR alerts to calculate days since prescription was issued and validate refill timing.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 414-DE; State Board of Pharmacy Regulations

description: Two-character NCPDP code (Field 440-E5) indicating the professional service performed by the pharmacist in response to the DUR alert (e.g., 'AS'=Patient Assessment, 'CC'=Coordination of Care, 'DE'=Dosing Evaluation, 'PM'=Patient Medication Education). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 440-E5; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153

description: Quantity of the drug dispensed (in units as defined by the drug's billing unit of measure). Used in high-dose and quantity limit DUR alert evaluations.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 442-E7; CMS Part D; FDA Drug Labeling

description: Indicates whether the DUR alert was triggered by a quantity limit edit, where the dispensed or prescribed quantity exceeds the plan's maximum allowable quantity for the drug.
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Requirements; NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; PBM Formulary Management

description: Two-character NCPDP code (Field 439-E4 context) providing the clinical reason for the DUR service intervention. Captures the specific clinical basis for the alert and pharmacist action. Used in prospective DUR programs.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153

description: Sequential refill number for the prescription (0=original fill, 1=first refill, etc.). Used in early refill DUR alert logic to determine if the member is refilling too soon relative to the previous fill's days supply.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 403-D3; State Board of Pharmacy Regulations

description: Two-character NCPDP code (Field 441-E6) indicating the outcome of the pharmacist's intervention in response to the DUR alert (e.g., 'FA'=Filled As Is, 'FN'=Filled with Prescriber Approval, 'FC'=Filled, Calling Prescriber, 'FD'=Filled, Directions Clarified). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard Field 441-E6; CMS Part D DUR Reporting Requirements 42 CFR §423.153

description: Coded severity level of the DUR alert indicating clinical significance. Typically: 1=Contraindicated/Severe, 2=Major, 3=Moderate, 4=Minor, 5=Informational. Drives pharmacist override requirements and adjudication workflow.
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; First DataBank (FDB) Severity Levels; Medi-Span Drug Interaction Severity

description: Plain-language description of the DUR alert severity level (e.g., 'Contraindicated', 'Major', 'Moderate', 'Minor', 'Informational'). Used in pharmacist-facing displays and DUR reporting dashboards.
reference: NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; First DataBank (FDB); Medi-Span

description: Indicates whether the DUR alert is related to a step therapy protocol violation, where the member has not tried a required first-line drug before the prescribed drug. Supports step therapy enforcement and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Requirements; NCPDP DUR/PPS Codes; PBM Formulary Management

description: Timestamp when the DUR alert record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks updates such as status changes, override entries, or adjudication outcome revisions for audit and data lineage purposes.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; CMS Part D Record Retention; NAIC Data Governance Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each CMS Part D regulatory data submission record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.

The CMS-assigned Plan Benefit Package identifier associated with this submission. Distinguishes specific benefit packages within a Part D contract for formulary, cost-sharing, and coverage gap reporting.
reference: CMS Plan Benefit Package Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Part D submissions are a type of regulatory filing; linking to regulatory_submission records submission details and compliance tracking.

The number of records within the submission batch that CMS accepted and processed successfully. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Used to calculate acceptance rate and identify partial rejections.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Externally assigned batch identifier assigned by the CMS submission system (RAPS, EDPS, or CGDP portal) for the submission file. Used to correlate records across the submission lifecycle and match CMS acknowledgment responses.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The four-digit calendar year of the Medicare Part D benefit period to which this submission applies. Drives payment year reconciliation, risk adjustment settlement, and DIR reporting cycles with CMS.
reference: CMS Part D Payment Reconciliation; 42 CFR Part 423

business_glossary_term: Coverage Gap Discount Program (CGDP) Invoice Amount
description: The total dollar amount invoiced under the Coverage Gap Discount Program for this submission, applicable to CGDP submission types. Represents manufacturer discount obligations for brand-name drugs dispensed to Part D beneficiaries in the coverage gap.
reference: CMS CGDP Operational Guidance; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart R; ACA Section 3301

description: The tracking or reference identifier assigned by the EDI clearinghouse (Availity or Change Healthcare) upon receipt of the submission file. Used to trace submission status through the clearinghouse to CMS.
reference: Availity EDI Gateway; Change Healthcare EDI Gateway; HIPAA EDI Standards

description: The CMS-assigned Part D plan contract identifier (e.g., H1234 or S5678) under which this submission is filed. Identifies the health plan's Medicare Part D contract for regulatory reporting and payment purposes.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Contract Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The CMS regional office code associated with the Part D contract under which this submission is filed. Used for regulatory routing and regional compliance reporting.
reference: CMS Regional Office Structure; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The acknowledgment or disposition code returned by CMS upon processing the submission batch. Maps to CMS-defined response code sets (e.g., RAPS response codes, EDPS acknowledgment codes). Used for automated error triage and compliance tracking.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

business_glossary_term: CMS Response Receipt Date and Time
description: The timestamp when the CMS acknowledgment or response file was received by the health plan. Used to calculate CMS processing turnaround time and to trigger downstream reconciliation workflows.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: Human-readable description of the CMS response code returned for this submission. Provides context for the acceptance, rejection, or error condition reported by CMS.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The timestamp when this Part D submission record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the record audit created timestamp for data lineage and ETL traceability.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule; CMS Data Governance Requirements

description: The total Direct and Indirect Remuneration dollar amount reported in this submission, applicable to DIR submission types. Represents post-point-of-sale price concessions, rebates, and other remuneration from manufacturers and pharmacies that reduce net drug costs. Required for CMS Part D DIR reconciliation.
reference: CMS Part D DIR Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.308; CMS DIR Guidance 2023

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Interchange Control Number
description: The ISA13 interchange control number from the EDI transmission envelope, applicable to EDI-formatted submissions. Used to correlate submission files with EDI gateway acknowledgments (997/999) from the clearinghouse or CMS.
reference: HIPAA EDI Standards; ASC X12 837; Availity/Change Healthcare EDI Gateway

description: Structured or free-text description of errors returned by CMS for rejected or partially accepted records. May include CMS error codes, field-level rejection reasons, and record-level identifiers. Supports error remediation and resubmission workflows.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The number of Hierarchical Condition Category diagnosis records included in this submission batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Used to monitor HCC capture completeness and risk score optimization.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates whether this submission is a resubmission of a previously rejected or corrected batch (True) or an original submission (False). Used to track correction cycles and measure first-pass acceptance rates.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates whether the submission was transmitted to CMS on or before the regulatory deadline date (True) or after the deadline (False). Supports CMS compliance reporting and internal SLA monitoring.
reference: CMS Part D Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423; NCQA HEDIS Compliance

description: The Medicare Part D line of business associated with this submission. MAPD = Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug plan; PDP = standalone Prescription Drug Plan; EGWP = Employer Group Waiver Plan; LIS = Low Income Subsidy. Drives regulatory reporting segmentation.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Program Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS MAPD Guidelines

description: Free-text field for operational notes, manual override justifications, or compliance annotations associated with this submission record. Used by compliance and operations teams to document exception handling, CMS communications, or remediation actions.
reference: CMS Part D Compliance Requirements; HIPAA Security Rule

description: For resubmissions, the batch_id of the original submission that this record corrects or replaces. Null for original submissions. Enables lineage tracing across submission correction cycles.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: Identifies the source PBM system from which the submission data was extracted. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Values align to operational PBM platforms (RxClaim, Argus).
reference: CMS Part D Data Submission Requirements; HIPAA EDI Standards

description: The number of Prescription Drug Event records included in this submission batch, applicable to PDE and EDPS submission types. PDE records are the foundational data element for CMS Part D payment and reconciliation.
reference: CMS PDE Submission Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart G; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The number of records within the submission batch that CMS has received but not yet finalized (e.g., held for additional review or in a processing queue). Supports real-time submission status monitoring.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The estimated revenue impact in dollars attributable to the risk adjustment data submitted in this batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Supports risk adjustment revenue integrity analysis and HCC-based payment reconciliation.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Methodology; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart R; CMS RAPS Technical Guidance

description: The date on which the submission was fully reconciled against the CMS response. Null if reconciliation is not yet complete. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.
reference: CMS Part D Payment Reconciliation; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates the reconciliation state of the submission against CMS response data. UNRECONCILED = CMS response not yet matched; IN_PROGRESS = reconciliation underway; RECONCILED = fully matched and closed; EXCEPTION = discrepancy identified requiring manual review.
reference: CMS Part D Payment Reconciliation; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart K

description: The number of records within the submission batch that CMS rejected. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Drives error remediation workflows and resubmission prioritization.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The last calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the data window for the submission batch.
reference: CMS Part D Payment Reconciliation; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The first calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Used to align submission data with the applicable benefit year, payment year, or reconciliation cycle as defined by CMS.
reference: CMS Part D Payment Reconciliation; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Sequential count of resubmission attempts for a given original batch. Value is 0 for original submissions, 1 for first resubmission, 2 for second, etc. Supports escalation workflows and CMS deadline compliance monitoring.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the submission batch was transmitted to CMS. Distinct from the record audit created timestamp. Used for timeliness compliance tracking against CMS filing deadlines.
reference: CMS Part D Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart K

description: The regulatory deadline date by which this submission must be received by CMS. Used to assess timeliness compliance and trigger escalation alerts for at-risk submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423 Subpart K

description: The name of the file transmitted to CMS for this submission batch. Used for audit trail, resubmission tracking, and reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment files.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The electronic data interchange or file format used for the CMS submission. EDI_837 = ANSI X12 837 pharmacy claim transaction; FLAT_FILE = CMS-specified fixed-width flat file; XML = extensible markup language; CSV = comma-separated values.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; HIPAA EDI Standards; ASC X12 837

description: Current workflow state of the Part D submission. PENDING = prepared but not yet transmitted; SUBMITTED = transmitted to CMS; ACCEPTED = CMS confirmed full acceptance; REJECTED = CMS rejected the batch; PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED = CMS accepted a subset of records; RECONCILED = submission reconciled against CMS response and closed.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Classifies the type of CMS Part D regulatory submission. RAPS = Risk Adjustment Processing System pharmacy encounter; EDPS = Encounter Data Processing System; DIR = Direct and Indirect Remuneration report; CGDP = Coverage Gap Discount Program invoice; PDE = Prescription Drug Event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAPS|EDPS|DIR|CGDP|PDE — promote to reference product if additional types are added]
reference: CMS Part D Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The name of the organization or entity submitting the Part D data to CMS. May be the health plan, TPA, or PBM acting on behalf of the plan. Supports audit trail and accountability tracking.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The total number of records (encounters, claims, or line items) included in the submission batch as transmitted to CMS. Used as the control total for reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment.
reference: CMS RAPS Technical Guidance; CMS EDPS Companion Guide

description: The timestamp when this Part D submission record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used to detect record changes, support incremental processing, and maintain audit trail integrity.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule; CMS Data Governance Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacy claim record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the pharmacy_claim data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.
Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: AP Invoice Generation: insurer creates an accounts‑payable invoice to reimburse the dispensing pharmacy for each approved claim.
Foreign key linking to finance.ap_payment. Business justification: AP Payment Recording: payment of the AP invoice for a claim is tracked to close the reimbursement cycle.

Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Required for the 'Pharmacy Claim Appeal' process where each claim can generate an appeal case; enables claim‑level appeal reporting and tracking.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Claim adjudication uses PBM contract rates, step‑therapy and rebate rules; linking claim to provider_contract enables accurate pricing and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑Center Allocation: claim expense is allocated to the appropriate cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.

Surrogate reference to the pharmacy that dispensed the drug. Links to the provider/pharmacy master record. Distinguishes retail, mail-order, and specialty pharmacy channels. Used for network adequacy reporting and pharmacy audit trail requirements.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 201-B1; CMS Part D EDPS

description: Surrogate reference to the NDC-level drug master record in the pharmacy domain. Links to the formulary drug master product which carries NDC, drug name, tier, and pricing (AWP/WAC/MAC). Central to formulary compliance and drug utilization review (DUR).
reference: FDA NDC Directory; NCPDP D.0 Field 407-D7

description: Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to determine member's eligibility span at service date, supporting CMS reporting and accurate cost‑share calculations.

Foreign key linking to claim.header. Business justification: Required for integrated care reporting: links pharmacy claims to the corresponding medical claim header for episode‑of‑care analysis and coordinated benefits calculations.

Surrogate reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Links to the plan and benefit product. Determines formulary, cost-sharing rules, and CMS Part D contract identification.
reference: CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP); ACA QHP Standards

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Required for Claim Adjudication Posting process: each pharmacy claim expense must be posted to a GL ledger for financial reporting and regulatory audit.

Surrogate reference to the health plan member who is the beneficiary of this pharmacy claim. Links to the member master record in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT). Required for eligibility verification, coordination of benefits (COB), and CMS Part D risk adjustment (RAF/HCC).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; CMS Part D EDPS/RAPS

description: Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Risk‑adjusted claim adjudication report requires each claim to reference the member's risk score to apply CMS risk adjustment factors.

Source system transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) at the time of adjudication. Used for claim traceability, PBM reconciliation, EDI 835 remittance matching, and pharmacy audit trail. Enables lineage back to the operational system of record.
reference: RxClaim/Argus PBM System; NCPDP D.0 Transaction Reference; HIPAA EDI 835

description: Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Claim adjudication requires verifying prescriber credentials per CMS regulations; linking claim to credential_record enables automated credential status checks.

Surrogate reference to the prior authorization record that approved dispensing of this drug, if applicable. Null when no PA was required. Links to the utilization management PA product. Required for specialty drug claims and step therapy override tracking.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 462-EV; CMS Part D PA Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Claim Processing Audit Report to track which employee adjudicated each pharmacy claim.

Surrogate reference to the prescribing provider who authored the prescription. Links to the provider master record in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource). Used for prescriber profiling, fraud/waste/abuse (FWA) detection, and HEDIS measure attribution.

Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Provides audit link between each pharmacy claim and the enrollment transaction that established coverage, needed for reconciliation and regulatory audit.

Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Claims are associated with utilization‑management cases for case‑level cost analysis and outcome tracking, required by case management dashboards.

Date and time the pharmacy claim was adjudicated (approved or rejected) by the PBM system. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing event). Used for claims processing SLA measurement, 835 remittance matching, and audit trail compliance.
reference: NCPDP D.0; HIPAA 835 Remittance; CMS Part D EDPS

description: Code indicating the pricing benchmark used to determine the ingredient cost for this claim. AWP = Average Wholesale Price; WAC = Wholesale Acquisition Cost; MAC = Maximum Allowable Cost; usual_and_customary = pharmacy's standard retail price; negotiated = contracted rate; 340B = 340B program pricing; other = alternative basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awp|wac|mac|usual_and_customary|negotiated|340b|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 423-DN; CMS Part D Pricing Rules; 340B Drug Pricing Program (HRSA)

description: Externally-known, human-readable claim reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) at adjudication. Used for member communications, Explanation of Benefits (EOB), and 835 remittance reconciliation. Maps to NCPDP D.0 transaction reference number.
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard D.0; CMS Part D Encounter Data Processing System (EDPS)

description: Current adjudication lifecycle status of the pharmacy claim as determined by the PBM system. 'paid' = claim adjudicated and payment issued; 'rejected' = claim denied with reject code; 'reversed' = previously paid claim reversed; 'adjusted' = claim reprocessed with changed amounts; 'pending' = awaiting adjudication; 'void' = cancelled before processing.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Transaction Response; CMS Part D EDPS Claim Status Codes

description: Indicates whether coordination of benefits (COB) was applied on this claim (True = COB applied, another payer was involved). Triggers COB segment processing and other_payer_amount population. Used for COB reconciliation and CMS Part D TrOOP calculations.
reference: NCPDP D.0 COB Segment; HIPAA COB Rules; CMS Part D TrOOP

description: Timestamp when this pharmacy claim record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and HIPAA record retention requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: NCPDP Dispense As Written (DAW) code indicating whether a brand drug was dispensed when a generic was available, and the reason. 0 = no product selection indicated; 1 = substitution not allowed by prescriber; 2 = substitution allowed, patient requested brand; 3 = substitution allowed, pharmacist selected brand; 4 = substitution allowed, generic not in stock; 5 = substitution allowed, brand dispensed as generic. Used for generic substitution rate analytics and formulary compliance.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 408-D8 (DAW/Product Selection Code); CMS Part D Generic Substitution

description: Number of days of medication supply dispensed on this claim as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for refill-too-soon edits, medication adherence (MPR/PDC) calculations, HEDIS pharmacy measures, and CMS Part D days supply validation.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 405-D5; CMS Part D Days Supply Rules; HEDIS Technical Specifications

description: Dollar amount of this pharmacy claim applied toward the member's pharmacy or combined medical/pharmacy deductible. Relevant for HDHP/HSA plans and CMS Part D deductible phase tracking. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.
reference: NCPDP D.0; ACA Deductible Rules; CMS Part D Benefit Phases

description: Fee charged by the dispensing pharmacy for the act of dispensing the prescription, separate from ingredient cost. Negotiated as part of the PBM network contract. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and total claim cost analysis.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 412-DC; CMS Part D Dispensing Fee Rules

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the dispensing pharmacy as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability and NCPDP D.0 compliance. Used for pharmacy network validation, audit trail, and CMS Part D encounter data.
reference: HIPAA NPI Final Rule 45 CFR §162.406; NCPDP D.0 Field 201-B1

description: NCPDP standard code indicating the outcome of the prospective drug utilization review (DUR) edit performed during adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, duplicate therapy, dose check). Null if no DUR alert was triggered. Critical for pharmacy safety and regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — NCPDP DUR outcome codes exceed 6 values]
reference: NCPDP D.0 DUR/PPS Segment Field 439-E4; CMS Part D DUR Requirements

description: The date the prescription was dispensed by the pharmacy to the member. This is the principal real-world business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for days supply calculations, refill-too-soon edits, and CMS Part D encounter data date of service reporting.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 401-D1 (Date of Service); CMS Part D EDPS

description: Formulary tier assigned to the dispensed drug at the time of adjudication. Determines member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) and plan benefit design. Tier 1 = preferred generic; Tier 2 = generic; Tier 3 = preferred brand; Tier 4 = non-preferred brand; Tier 5 = specialty; non_formulary = not on formulary.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: The cost of the drug ingredient(s) as billed by the dispensing pharmacy, before dispensing fee and other adjustments. Basis may be AWP, WAC, MAC, or negotiated rate. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for pharmacy claim financials. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and MLR calculation.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 409-D9; CMS Part D Direct and Indirect Remuneration (DIR)

description: Indicates whether this pharmacy claim was dispensed under the 340B Drug Pricing Program (True = 340B claim). Used for 340B duplicate discount prevention, HRSA compliance reporting, and separation of 340B and non-340B claims in financial reconciliation.
reference: HRSA 340B Drug Pricing Program; CMS Part D 340B Duplicate Discount Policy

description: Indicates whether this claim is for a compounded drug product containing multiple ingredients (True = compound claim). Compound claims require line-level detail in the pharmacy_claim_line product. Used for compound drug utilization review and cost management.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Compound Segment; CMS Part D Compound Drug Rules

description: Line of business under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Drives regulatory reporting requirements, formulary rules, and cost-sharing structures. 'commercial' = employer-sponsored or individual; 'medicare_part_d' = CMS Medicare Part D; 'medicaid' = state Medicaid; 'exchange' = ACA marketplace QHP; 'cobra' = COBRA continuation coverage.
reference: CMS Part D; ACA QHP Standards; NAIC LOB Reporting; ERISA

description: Percentage-based cost-sharing amount paid by the member for this pharmacy claim, calculated as a percentage of the allowed cost. Applies when the benefit design uses coinsurance rather than fixed copay. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.
reference: NCPDP D.0; ACA Cost-Sharing Rules; CMS Part D Benefit Design

description: Fixed dollar amount paid by the member at point of sale as their cost-sharing obligation for this pharmacy claim. Determined by formulary tier and benefit design. Contributes to member out-of-pocket (OOP) and maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) accumulation.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 506-F6; ACA MOOP Limits; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules

description: 11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product dispensed, including labeler, product, and package segment. Denormalized from the drug master for claim-level auditability and CMS Part D encounter data submission (EDPS). Required for 340B duplicate discount prevention and drug utilization review (DUR).
reference: FDA NDC Directory; NCPDP D.0 Field 407-D7; CMS Part D EDPS

description: Claim number of the original pharmacy claim that this record adjusts or reverses. Populated only for adjusted or reversed claims; null for original claims. Enables claim adjustment chain tracing for financial reconciliation, audit, and CMS Part D encounter data correction submissions.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Claim Adjustment; CMS Part D EDPS Adjustment Processing; HIPAA 835

description: Amount paid by another payer (secondary insurer, Medicare, Medicaid, manufacturer coupon) under coordination of benefits (COB). Used for COB reconciliation, 340B duplicate discount prevention, and CMS Part D TrOOP (True Out-of-Pocket) calculations.
reference: NCPDP D.0 COB Segment; CMS Part D TrOOP; HIPAA COB Rules

description: Channel through which the drug was dispensed. Drives cost-sharing differentials, formulary tier rules, and network adequacy reporting. 'retail' = community pharmacy; 'mail_order' = 90-day mail service; 'specialty' = specialty pharmacy for high-cost/complex drugs; 'long_term_care' = SNF/LTC pharmacy; 'home_infusion' = infusion therapy at home.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Type Codes; NCPDP D.0 Pharmacy Service Type

description: Total amount paid by the health plan for this pharmacy claim after applying member cost-sharing (copay, coinsurance, deductible) and COB adjustments. Net total component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for 835 remittance, MLR reporting, and CMS Part D encounter data.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 505-F5; HIPAA 835 Remittance; CMS Part D EDPS

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing provider as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability, NCPDP D.0 compliance, and CMS Part D encounter data submission. Used for prescriber fraud/waste/abuse (FWA) analytics.
reference: HIPAA NPI Final Rule 45 CFR §162.406; NCPDP D.0 Field 411-DB; CMS NPI Registry

description: Date the prescription was written by the prescriber. Used to validate prescription age, enforce days-supply-from-written-date rules, and support medication adherence analytics. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing date).
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 414-DE (Date Prescription Written); State Pharmacy Board Regulations

description: Actual quantity of the drug dispensed (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, grams) as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for quantity limit (QL) edits, drug utilization review (DUR), and CMS Part D encounter data submission.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 442-E7; CMS Part D EDPS Quantity Dispensed

description: NCPDP standard unit of measure code for the quantity dispensed. EA = each (tablets/capsules); ML = milliliters; GM = grams; UN = units; F2 = international units; ME = milliequivalents. Required for accurate quantity limit (QL) edit enforcement and CMS Part D encounter data.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 600-28 (Unit of Measure); CMS Part D EDPS

description: Refill sequence number for this dispensing event. Zero (0) indicates the original fill; subsequent values indicate refill number. Used for refill-too-soon edits, medication adherence tracking, and prescription lifecycle management.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 403-D3; CMS Part D Refill Rules

description: NCPDP standard reject code returned by the PBM system when a claim is rejected or denied. Null for paid claims. Used for pharmacy operations, member appeals, and drug utilization review (DUR) outcome tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — NCPDP reject codes exceed 6 values]
reference: NCPDP D.0 Reject Code List; CMS Part D Reject Code Requirements

description: Sales tax amount applied to the pharmacy claim where applicable by state law. Included in total claim cost and reported separately for financial reconciliation and statutory accounting (SAP) purposes.
reference: NCPDP D.0 Field 481-HA; State Tax Regulations

description: Indicates whether a step therapy protocol was overridden for this claim (True = override applied). Step therapy requires trying lower-cost drugs before approving higher-cost alternatives. Override requires prior authorization. Used for utilization management (UM) reporting and CMS step therapy compliance.
reference: CMS Step Therapy Rule (42 CFR §422.136); NCQA UM Standards

description: Timestamp when this pharmacy claim record was last modified in the Silver layer, such as after a claim adjustment, reversal, or COB update. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Primary key for prior_authorization

Foreign key linking to appeal.appeal_case. Business justification: Needed for the 'Prior Authorization Appeal' workflow; links a PA request to its appeal case for audit, compliance, and outcome reporting.

Identifier of the clinical reviewer (pharmacist, physician, or nurse) who made the prior authorization decision in the Utilization Management system (InterQual/MCG). Required for audit trail and URAC UM accreditation.
reference: URAC UM Accreditation Standards; NCQA Accreditation; State DOI UM Regulations

description: Identifier of the formulary associated with the member's plan at the time of the prior authorization request. Determines drug tier, coverage rules, and applicable PA criteria.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; PBM Formulary Management (RxClaim/Argus)

description: Identifier of the health plan or benefit plan under which the prior authorization is being evaluated. Determines applicable formulary, tier rules, and PA criteria.
reference: CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package; ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Prior‑authorization workflow uses the member's risk score to assess clinical necessity and cost containment, mandated by internal utilization management policies.
Identifier of the health plan member for whom the prior authorization is requested. Links to the member master record in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR §164; CMS Part D Enrollment

description: Reference to the original prior authorization record when this record represents a renewal, appeal, or modification. Enables tracking of the full PA lifecycle chain. Null for initial requests.
reference: CMS Part D PA Continuity; URAC UM Standards

description: Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_decision. Business justification: Needed for compliance reporting that ties each prior authorization to the final decision, supporting regulatory PA decision audit.

Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: Required for PA audit report linking each prior authorization record to its originating request, enabling traceability of request‑to‑authorization lifecycle.
Indicates whether this prior authorization record is associated with a member or prescriber appeal of a prior denial. True = this PA is an appeal case.
reference: CMS Part D Appeals and Grievances Requirements; URAC UM Standards; State DOI Appeal Regulations

description: Number of days of drug therapy authorized under this prior authorization. Used by the PBM to enforce dispensing limits and by CMS Part D for compliance reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Days Supply Rules; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: Total quantity of the drug authorized for dispensing under this prior authorization (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, units). Enforced at point-of-sale by the PBM adjudication system.
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Rules; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard

description: Number of refills authorized under this prior authorization in addition to the initial fill. Enforced by the PBM adjudication system at point-of-sale.
reference: CMS Part D; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; DEA Controlled Substance Regulations

description: Code identifying the specific clinical criteria set applied during the prior authorization review, sourced from the Utilization Management system (InterQual or MCG). Used for audit, appeals, and regulatory reporting.
reference: InterQual Clinical Criteria; MCG Care Guidelines; URAC UM Accreditation Standards

description: Version of the clinical criteria set (InterQual or MCG) applied at the time of the prior authorization decision. Required for audit trails and appeals to ensure the correct criteria version is referenced.
reference: InterQual Clinical Criteria; MCG Care Guidelines; URAC UM Accreditation

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Reportable Indicator
description: Indicates whether this prior authorization record is subject to CMS Part D PA reporting requirements. True = must be included in CMS Part D PA reporting submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization Reporting Requirements; 42 CFR §423

description: Timestamp when this prior authorization record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer processing compliance.
reference: HIPAA Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312; Enterprise Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Indicates whether the submitted clinical information met the applicable clinical criteria (InterQual/MCG) for the requested drug. True = criteria met (supports approval); False = criteria not met (supports denial).
reference: InterQual Clinical Criteria; MCG Care Guidelines; CMS Part D PA Requirements

description: Exact date and time the prior authorization decision (approval, denial, or pend) was rendered by the clinical reviewer or automated adjudication engine. Used to calculate turnaround time against CMS and URAC standards.
reference: CMS Part D PA Timeliness Standards; URAC UM Accreditation; NCQA Accreditation

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for denial of the prior authorization request (e.g., not medically necessary, step therapy not completed, quantity limit exceeded, non-covered drug). Populated only when pa_status = denied. Used for member appeals and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Denial Reason Codes; NCPDP Reject Codes; URAC UM Standards

description: Free-text description of the denial reason, providing clinical or administrative context beyond the denial reason code. Included in member and prescriber denial notices per CMS and state DOI requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Denial Notice Requirements; State DOI Adverse Determination Notice Rules; URAC UM Standards

description: Pharmacy dispensing channel authorized for this prior authorization (retail, mail-order, specialty pharmacy, long-term care, or home infusion). Determines which pharmacy network is eligible to dispense.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Network Requirements; NCPDP Pharmacy Type Codes; PBM Network Management

description: 11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) identifying the specific drug product, strength, and package size for which prior authorization is requested. Sourced from the PBM drug master (RxClaim/Argus).
reference: FDA NDC Directory; CMS Part D Drug Data; NCPDP Standard

description: Formulary tier of the requested drug at the time of the prior authorization request. Determines cost-sharing obligations and PA criteria applied.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Tier Requirements; PBM Formulary Management (RxClaim/Argus)

description: NCPDP-standardized Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code resulting from prospective DUR screening performed during PA adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, duplicate therapy). Sourced from PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).
reference: NCPDP DUR Standard; CMS Part D DUR Requirements; OBRA 1990 DUR Mandate

description: Date on which the prior authorization expires and is no longer valid for dispensing. After this date, a new PA request is required. Nullable for open-ended authorizations.
reference: CMS Part D; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Adjudication Rules

description: Date from which the approved prior authorization is valid for dispensing. Pharmacies and PBM adjudication systems use this date to confirm dispensing eligibility.
reference: CMS Part D; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; PBM Adjudication Rules

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Primary Diagnosis Code
description: Primary ICD-10-CM diagnosis code submitted by the prescriber to support the clinical necessity of the requested drug. Required for clinical criteria evaluation and CMS Part D PA reporting. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).
reference: WHO ICD-10-CM; CMS Part D Clinical Criteria; HIPAA 45 CFR §164 PHI

description: Line of Business (LOB) under which the member's coverage falls, determining applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and PA criteria (e.g., Commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace).
reference: CMS Part D; ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP); NAIC Model Laws; State DOI Regulations

description: Free-text clinical notes entered by the reviewing clinician during the prior authorization evaluation, capturing clinical rationale, additional context, or instructions for the prescriber or pharmacy. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR §164; CMS Part D PA Documentation Requirements; URAC UM Standards

description: Externally-known, human-readable prior authorization reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) or Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG). Used on Explanation of Benefits (EOB) and communicated to prescribers and pharmacies.
reference: CMS Part D; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; HIPAA 278 Transaction

description: Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request. Drives dispensing eligibility at the pharmacy and CMS Part D PA reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|denied|pending|cancelled|expired|appealed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization; NCPDP Telecommunication Standard; HIPAA 278 Transaction

description: Category of prior authorization criteria applied, indicating the clinical or formulary reason the drug requires PA review (e.g., specialty drug, non-preferred tier, step therapy requirement, quantity limit, age restriction, off-label use). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty|non_preferred|step_therapy|quantity_limit|age_restriction|off_label — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Formulary and PA Requirements; PBM Formulary Management

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or authorized prescriber requesting the prior authorization. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource) and validated against NPPES.
reference: CMS NPI Final Rule 45 CFR §162.406; NPPES Registry

description: Indicates whether a quantity limit restriction was a factor in the prior authorization evaluation. True = quantity limit rules were applied during adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Requirements; PBM Formulary Management (RxClaim/Argus)

description: Exact date and time the prior authorization request was received by the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG) or PBM system (RxClaim/Argus). Used for precise turnaround time calculation and regulatory compliance.
reference: CMS Part D PA Timeliness; URAC UM Accreditation Standards

description: Calendar date on which the prior authorization request was submitted by the prescriber or pharmacy. Used for turnaround time measurement and CMS Part D compliance reporting.
reference: CMS Part D PA Timeliness Standards; URAC Drug Therapy Management Standards

description: Classification of the prior authorization request indicating whether it is a new request, renewal of an existing authorization, appeal of a denial, expedited urgent request, or retrospective review.
reference: CMS Part D PA Requirements; URAC UM Standards; NCQA Accreditation

description: Level of clinical review applied to the prior authorization request, indicating whether it was reviewed by a pharmacist, physician, peer-to-peer consultation, or medical director. Escalation level affects turnaround time standards.
reference: URAC UM Accreditation Standards; NCQA Accreditation; CMS Part D PA Requirements

business_glossary_term: Secondary International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Diagnosis Codes
description: Pipe-delimited list of secondary ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes submitted to support clinical necessity. Stored as a delimited string per Silver Layer conventions (no ARRAY type). Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).
reference: WHO ICD-10-CM; CMS Part D Clinical Criteria; HIPAA 45 CFR §164 PHI

description: Operational system of record from which this prior authorization record originated (PBM system RxClaim/Argus or UM system InterQual/MCG or Core Admin Platform Facets/QNXT). Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; HIPAA Data Provenance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Native prior authorization identifier from the originating operational system (RxClaim, Argus, InterQual, or MCG). Preserved for reconciliation, audit, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Indicates whether the requested drug is classified as a specialty drug requiring specialty pharmacy dispensing and enhanced clinical review. Specialty drugs typically have higher cost-sharing and stricter PA criteria.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Tier Definition; NCPDP Specialty Drug Classification; PBM Formulary Management

description: Indicates whether the member has documented evidence of completing the required step therapy (tried and failed prerequisite drugs) as required by the formulary PA criteria. Populated when step_therapy_required = True.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Rules; NCQA Formulary Management Standards

description: Indicates whether the prior authorization requires the member to have tried and failed one or more preferred or lower-tier drugs (step therapy) before the requested drug is authorized. True = step therapy applies.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Rules; NCQA Formulary Management Standards; State DOI Step Therapy Laws

description: Timestamp when this prior authorization record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform, reflecting status changes, decision updates, or data corrections. Used for change data capture and audit.
reference: HIPAA Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312; Enterprise Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each drug master record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins across formulary, claims, DUR, rebate contracting, and PBM adjudication.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Data governance requires tracking which employee entered or updated drug master records.

Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for drug rebate and pricing management: linking each drug to its manufacturer/vendor enables accurate rebate calculations, pricing negotiations, and CMS reporting.

The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code at the 5th level, identifying the drug's anatomical main group, therapeutic subgroup, pharmacological subgroup, chemical subgroup, and chemical substance. Used for therapeutic class reporting, HEDIS pharmacy measures, population health analytics, and international drug classification alignment.
reference: WHO ATC/DDD Index; WHO Collaborating Centre for Drug Statistics Methodology; NCQA HEDIS Pharmacy Measures

description: The date on which the current AWP unit price became effective. Used to apply the correct AWP for historical claims repricing, audit reconciliation, and retroactive rebate calculations.
reference: First Databank AWP Update Schedule; Medi-Span AWP Update Schedule; CMS Part D Pricing

description: The Average Wholesale Price (AWP) per unit of the drug as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. AWP is the benchmark pricing reference used in pharmacy reimbursement calculations, MAC pricing comparisons, and rebate contract negotiations. Expressed in USD per unit (tablet, mL, gram, etc.).
reference: First Databank AWP; Medi-Span AWP; CMS Part D Pricing Benchmarks; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Transparency Act

description: The proprietary trade name under which the drug is marketed by the manufacturer (e.g., Lipitor, Humira). Used in formulary tier assignment, rebate contracting, member communications, and brand vs. generic substitution logic in PBM adjudication.
reference: FDA Drug Labeling Requirements; First Databank Drug Database

description: The DEA controlled substance schedule classification (I through V) indicating the drug's potential for abuse and accepted medical use. Schedule II-V drugs require enhanced DUR edits, quantity limits, early refill restrictions, and special handling in PBM adjudication. Not_scheduled indicates no DEA restriction.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act (21 USC 812); DEA Schedule of Controlled Substances; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: The timestamp when this drug master record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and change data capture tracking per HIPAA Security Rule requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164.312); SOC 2 Audit Trail Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether a valid DEA registration number is required on the prescription for this drug due to its controlled substance classification. Drives prescription validation edits in PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing workflows.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act (21 USC 829); DEA Practitioner Registration Requirements

description: The physical form in which the drug is dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution, injection, patch, inhaler). Critical for formulary configuration, quantity limit rules, step therapy protocols, and DUR screening in RxClaim/Argus. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution|injection|patch|inhaler|cream|ointment|suspension|powder — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Dosage Form Terminology; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; First Databank Drug Database

description: The classification code for the drug class within the GPI or ETC hierarchy, representing a more granular grouping below therapeutic subclass (e.g., specific statin subtype). Used in formulary step therapy rules, DUR clinical edits, and rebate tier segmentation in PBM systems.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Classification

description: The human-readable name of the drug class corresponding to the drug_class_code. Provides the most granular named classification level in the therapeutic hierarchy for formulary configuration and clinical reporting.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Classification

description: The full proprietary or established name of the drug as it appears on the FDA-approved label and in the First Databank or Medi-Span drug database feed. Used as the primary human-readable identity label across formulary displays, EOB communications, and member-facing materials.
reference: FDA Drug Labeling Requirements; First Databank Drug Database; Medi-Span Drug Database

description: Current lifecycle status of the drug record in the master file. Active drugs are eligible for formulary placement and PBM adjudication. Discontinued or recalled drugs trigger claim edits and member/provider notifications. Drives formulary maintenance workflows in RxClaim/Argus.
reference: FDA Drug Approval and Market Status; First Databank Drug Database; CMS Part D Formulary Requirements

description: Classification of the drug as brand-name, generic, biosimilar, over-the-counter (OTC), compounded, or repackaged. Drives formulary tier assignment, cost-sharing rules, generic substitution logic, and rebate eligibility in PBM adjudication and formulary management.
reference: FDA Orange Book; FDA Purple Book (Biosimilars); CMS Part D; NCQA HEDIS Pharmacy Measures

description: The date on which this drug master record became effective and eligible for use in formulary configuration, PBM adjudication, and claims processing. Used for temporal validity enforcement in adjudication date-of-service lookups and formulary effective date management.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; Internal Formulary Management Policy

description: The current FDA regulatory approval status of the drug product. Approved drugs are eligible for formulary coverage. Tentatively approved generics may be covered under specific plan designs. Withdrawn or not-approved drugs are excluded from formulary and trigger claim denials. Required for CMS Part D formulary compliance.
reference: FDA Orange Book; FDA Purple Book; CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423

description: The International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or United States Adopted Name (USAN) for the active ingredient(s) of the drug (e.g., atorvastatin, adalimumab). Used for generic substitution logic, DUR screening, therapeutic equivalence grouping, and HEDIS pharmacy measure reporting.
reference: WHO INN Program; USAN Council; First Databank Drug Database

description: The 14-digit Medi-Span Generic Product Identifier (GPI) code providing a hierarchical drug classification from drug group (2 digits) through drug class, subclass, name, name extension, dosage form, and strength. Used as the primary therapeutic classification key in RxClaim/Argus PBM systems for formulary management, DUR screening, and rebate contracting.
reference: Medi-Span GPI Classification System; Wolters Kluwer Health Drug Database

description: Indicates whether this drug is clinically associated with one or more CMS Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs) for risk adjustment purposes. HCC-relevant drugs are used in RAF score modeling, RAPS/EDPS encounter data submissions, and population health stratification for Medicare Advantage plans.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS/EDPS Submission Requirements; 42 CFR Part 422

description: Indicates whether this drug is an FDA-approved biosimilar to a reference biological product. Biosimilars have distinct formulary placement rules, interchangeability designations, and rebate contracting considerations under CMS Part D and commercial plan designs.
reference: FDA Purple Book; Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act (BPCIA); CMS Part D Biosimilar Policy

description: Indicates whether an FDA-approved generic equivalent is available in the market for this brand-name drug. Used in formulary tier assignment, member cost-sharing communications, and generic substitution prompts during PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing.
reference: FDA Orange Book; CMS Part D Generic Substitution Policy; NCQA HEDIS PDC Measures

description: Indicates whether this drug is classified as a maintenance medication intended for long-term chronic condition management (e.g., antihypertensives, statins, diabetes medications). Maintenance drugs are eligible for 90-day supply dispensing at mail-order pharmacies and are used in HEDIS medication adherence measures (PDC).
reference: NCQA HEDIS Medication Adherence Measures; CMS Part D Medication Therapy Management (MTM) Criteria

description: Indicates whether this drug qualifies as a preventive medication under ACA Section 2713 or HDHP/HSA preventive care rules, making it eligible for $0 cost-sharing before the deductible is met. Used in benefit configuration and claims adjudication cost-sharing logic.
reference: ACA Section 2713; IRS Notice 2004-23 (HSA Preventive Care); USPSTF Preventive Drug List
