description: Indicates whether this drug is classified as a specialty pharmaceutical requiring special handling, storage, administration, or monitoring (e.g., biologics, oncology agents, hepatitis C treatments). Specialty drugs are subject to mandatory specialty pharmacy channel requirements, prior authorization, and enhanced DUR in PBM adjudication. Critical for CMS Part D specialty tier placement.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Tier Definition; NCPDP Specialty Drug Classification; PBM Specialty Drug List

description: The 5-digit FDA-assigned labeler code (first segment of the NDC) identifying the firm that manufactures, repackages, relabels, or distributes the drug. Used for manufacturer-level rebate aggregation and PBM contract management.
reference: FDA NDC Labeler Code Registry; CMS Part D

description: The FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=originator brand with no generic, M=multi-source (generic available), N=no current market (discontinued), Y=single-source generic. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary tier assignment, and MAC pricing eligibility determination.
reference: FDA Orange Book Multi-Source Code Definitions; CMS Part D Generic Substitution

description: The 11-digit National Drug Code assigned by the FDA uniquely identifying the labeler, product, and package size of a drug. This is the primary external business identifier for the drug and is used in pharmacy claims (NCPDP/837P), formulary management, PBM adjudication (RxClaim/Argus), and CMS Part D reporting. Stored in 5-4-2 format without hyphens.
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; CMS Part D Reporting Requirements

description: The NDC formatted in the standard 5-4-2 hyphenated display format (e.g., 12345-6789-01) as required for human-readable labeling, SBC documentation, and regulatory submissions to CMS and state DOIs.
reference: FDA NDC Labeling Requirements; CMS Part D Reporting

description: The quantity of drug units contained in the dispensed package as defined by the NDC (e.g., 30 tablets, 100 mL, 1 vial). Used in unit-of-use pricing calculations, days supply verification, quantity limit enforcement, and MAC pricing per-unit derivation.
reference: FDA NDC Package Size Definition; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; First Databank Drug Database

description: The unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., EA for each/tablet/capsule, ML for milliliter, GM for gram). Used in conjunction with package_size for accurate dispensing quantity calculations, days supply verification, and unit-cost pricing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH|KIT — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; FDA NDC Package Size Terminology; First Databank Drug Database

description: The pharmacological mechanism-of-action category of the drug (e.g., ACE Inhibitor, Beta Blocker, SSRI, Statin, Monoclonal Antibody). Used for DUR drug-drug interaction screening, therapeutic duplication edits, clinical program targeting in care management, and HEDIS measure attribution.
reference: First Databank Drug Database; Medi-Span Drug Database; NCQA HEDIS Pharmacy Measures

description: The NDC of the FDA Reference Listed Drug (RLD) or reference biological product to which this generic or biosimilar is therapeutically equivalent. Enables generic substitution relationship mapping, step therapy configuration, and biosimilar interchangeability tracking in the PBM system.
reference: FDA Orange Book; FDA Purple Book; 21 CFR Part 314.3

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program
description: The name of the FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program associated with this drug, if applicable (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, TIRF REMS for fentanyl products). REMS drugs require special dispensing controls, patient enrollment, and prescriber certification that must be validated during PBM adjudication and prior authorization.
reference: FDA REMS Program Requirements (21 USC 355-1); FDA REMS Database

description: The path by which the drug is administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Drives formulary benefit category assignment (medical vs. pharmacy benefit), prior authorization requirements, and specialty pharmacy routing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|ophthalmic|otic|rectal — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Route of Administration Terminology; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; First Databank Drug Database

description: The native record identifier from the originating source system (First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim, or Argus) for this drug master record. Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and upstream change tracking in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164)

description: Identifies the operational source system from which this drug master record was ingested (e.g., FDB=First Databank, MEDISPAN=Medi-Span, RXCLAIM=RxClaim PBM, ARGUS=Argus PBM, INTERNAL=internally maintained). Used for data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during ETL, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164); SOC 2 Data Lineage Requirements

description: The concentration or potency of the active ingredient(s) expressed as a numeric value with unit of measure (e.g., 10 mg, 500 mg/5 mL, 0.25%). Used in DUR duplicate therapy screening, quantity limit enforcement, and clinical appropriateness review in UM/PA workflows.
reference: FDA Drug Labeling Requirements; First Databank Drug Database; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: The date on which this drug master record was terminated or superseded, indicating the drug is no longer active for formulary placement or new claim adjudication. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for historical claims repricing and formulary audit trails.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; Internal Formulary Management Policy

description: The standardized code identifying the broad therapeutic class of the drug (e.g., cardiovascular agents, anti-infectives, antineoplastics). Used for formulary tier grouping, rebate contract aggregation, utilization reporting, and disease management program enrollment triggers.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Therapeutic Classification

description: The human-readable name of the therapeutic class corresponding to the therapeutic_class_code. Used in formulary displays, member-facing SBC documents, provider communications, and regulatory reporting to CMS and state DOIs.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Therapeutic Classification

description: The FDA Orange Book therapeutic equivalence evaluation code (e.g., AB, AA, BC) indicating whether a generic drug is therapeutically equivalent to the reference listed drug. AB-rated generics are substitutable at the pharmacy. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary step therapy, and DAW (Dispense As Written) override processing in PBM adjudication.
reference: FDA Orange Book (Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations); 21 CFR Part 314

description: The standardized code identifying the therapeutic subclass within the broader therapeutic class (e.g., HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitors within cardiovascular agents). Enables granular formulary tier differentiation, step therapy protocol design, and rebate contract segmentation.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Therapeutic Classification

description: The human-readable name of the therapeutic subclass corresponding to the therapeutic_subclass_code. Used in formulary management workflows, clinical program configuration, and detailed utilization analytics.
reference: First Databank Enhanced Therapeutic Classification (ETC); Medi-Span GPI Therapeutic Classification

description: The timestamp when this drug master record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental data refresh, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164.312); SOC 2 Audit Trail Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit as reported by the manufacturer, representing the list price to wholesalers before discounts and rebates. Used as a pricing benchmark for specialty drug reimbursement, rebate baseline calculations, and CMS Part D coverage gap discount program calculations.
reference: 42 USC 1395w-3a (WAC Definition); CMS Part D Coverage Gap Discount Program; Medi-Span WAC

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each NDC-level drug pricing record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the drug_pricing data product.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Drug pricing tables are derived from PBM contract agreements; linking ensures price source traceability for regulatory filings and rebate calculations.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: drug_pricing records are defined per NDC; linking to drug_master provides the authoritative drug attributes and eliminates duplicated columns.
FK to pharmacy.mac_list

Percentage discount applied to AWP to derive the reimbursement rate under AWP-based contracts (e.g., AWP minus 15%). Expressed as a positive decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Used for PBM contract compliance validation and reimbursement audits.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Average Wholesale Price per unit as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. Commonly used as the benchmark for pharmacy reimbursement calculations and discount negotiations. Expressed in USD.
reference: First Databank (FDB); Medi-Span Price Rx; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Timestamp when this drug pricing record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring of pricing file processing.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary price fields in this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for Puerto Rico and other US territories. Defaults to USD for domestic plans.
Number of days of drug supply for which this pricing record applies (e.g., 30-day retail, 90-day mail order). Used to differentiate pricing tiers by supply duration per PBM contract terms.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: DEA controlled substance schedule classification (CI through CV) or NON-CONTROLLED. Used for DUR edits, quantity limit enforcement, prior authorization requirements, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act (21 USC 812); 21 CFR Part 1308

description: Pharmacy dispensing channel for which this pricing record applies: retail (community pharmacy), mail_order (90-day supply mail service), specialty (specialty pharmacy), or long_term_care (LTC facility pharmacy). Pricing may differ by channel per PBM contract terms.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Fixed dispensing fee in USD paid to the pharmacy per prescription fill, separate from the ingredient cost. Defined in PBM network contracts and applied during claim adjudication to calculate total pharmacy reimbursement.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Date on which this pricing record becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication. Used to select the correct price for a claim based on the date of service. Aligns with PBM pricing file effective dates.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; PBM Contract Terms

description: Date on which this pricing record expires and is no longer applicable for new claim adjudication. Null indicates an open-ended pricing record with no defined end date. Used with effective_date to determine the active pricing window.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; PBM Contract Terms

description: Formulary tier assignment for this drug under the applicable plan benefit design (e.g., tier_1=preferred generic, tier_2=generic, tier_3=preferred brand, tier_4=non-preferred brand, tier_5=specialty). Drives member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) during claim adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Guidelines; NCQA Standards; ACA Essential Health Benefits

description: Version identifier of the MAC list, used to track updates and revisions to the MAC pricing schedule. Supports audit trails for MAC list changes and pharmacy reimbursement disputes. Null when price_type is not MAC.
reference: CMS Part D MAC Policy; PBM Contract Terms

description: Describes the methodology used to derive the MAC price (e.g., MARKET_BASKET=based on market survey of acquisition costs, NADAC_BASED=derived from CMS NADAC data, AWP_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from AWP, WAC_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from WAC, CUSTOM=plan-specific methodology). Required for PBM contract compliance and regulatory transparency. Null when price_type is not MAC.
reference: CMS Part D MAC Policy; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Law

description: Maximum Allowable Cost per unit set by the PBM or health plan for generic and multi-source drugs. Represents the upper reimbursement limit for a drug under the applicable MAC list. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not MAC.
reference: CMS Part D MAC Policy; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Law; PBM Contract Terms

description: FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=Originator brand (no generic), M=Multi-source (generic available), N=Non-multi-source, Y=Generic product. Used to determine MAC eligibility and generic substitution applicability.
reference: FDA Orange Book; First Databank (FDB); CMS Part D MAC Policy

description: Quantity of units contained in the standard package as defined by the NDC. Used to convert between package-level and per-unit pricing and to validate dispensed quantity against package size during adjudication.
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory; First Databank (FDB)

description: Unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., TAB for tablets, ML for milliliters). Distinct from unit_of_measure which applies to the pricing unit; this field describes the package-level quantity unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Percentage change in unit price from the prior pricing record to the current record, calculated as ((unit_price - prior_unit_price) / prior_unit_price) * 100. Used for drug price inflation monitoring, CMS price increase reporting, and PBM contract compliance.
reference: CMS Drug Pricing Transparency; Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) Drug Pricing Provisions

description: Classifies the pricing methodology represented by this record: AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), RBP (Reference Based Pricing), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary. Determines how the price is applied during claim adjudication and reimbursement calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AWP|WAC|MAC|RBP|NADAC|USUAL_AND_CUSTOMARY — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Law

description: Publication or release date of the source pricing file from which this record was extracted. Used to track pricing file currency, identify stale pricing data, and support audit and reconciliation processes.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; PBM Contract Terms

description: Name of the source pricing file from which this record was loaded (e.g., 'FDB_PRICE_20240101.txt', 'RXCLAIM_MAC_Q1_2024.csv'). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and troubleshooting pricing discrepancies.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; PBM Contract Terms

description: Identifies the originating source of the pricing data (e.g., First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim PBM feed, Argus PBM feed, CMS published rates, internal custom pricing). Critical for audit trails and PBM contract compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDB|MEDISPAN|RXCLAIM|ARGUS|CMS|INTERNAL|CUSTOM — promote to reference product]
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Current lifecycle status of the pricing record: active (currently in use for adjudication), inactive (no longer applicable), pending (approved but not yet effective), superseded (replaced by a newer pricing record for the same NDC and price type).
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; PBM Contract Terms

description: Previous per-unit price for this NDC and price type before the most recent pricing update. Used for price change analysis, trend reporting, and PBM contract compliance audits.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Reference Based Pricing per unit used as the benchmark reimbursement amount under RBP pharmacy contracts. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not RBP.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; ERISA Plan Design

description: Dispensing unit of measure for which the price applies (e.g., EA=Each, ML=Milliliter, GM=Gram, MG=Milligram, UN=Unit, TAB=Tablet, CAP=Capsule, VIAL=Vial, PATCH=Patch). Required for accurate per-unit cost calculation during adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]

Per-unit drug price expressed in USD for the applicable price type (AWP, WAC, MAC, or RBP). Used as the basis for claim adjudication, cost-sharing calculation, and pharmacy reimbursement. Precision to 6 decimal places to accommodate high-precision specialty drug pricing.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; PBM Contract Terms

description: Timestamp when this drug pricing record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164)

description: Wholesale Acquisition Cost per unit as reported by the manufacturer to the pricing compendia. Represents the list price from manufacturer to wholesaler before discounts. Expressed in USD. Used in WAC-based reimbursement contracts.
reference: 42 USC 1395w-3a; First Databank (FDB); Medi-Span Price Rx

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each drug manufacturer rebate record in the pharmacy benefit management system. Primary key for the drug_rebate data product.
FK to pharmacy.drug_master
Reference to the formulary under which the drug's tier placement drove the rebate calculation.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Reference to the PBM contract under which this rebate was negotiated and invoiced. Links to the governing PBM agreement.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; ERISA

description: Reference to the pharmaceutical manufacturer entity responsible for the rebate obligation.
reference: CMS Part D Rebate Program; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Average Wholesale Price (AWP) per unit for the drug as of the rebate period, used as the price benchmark for rebate rate calculations.
reference: Medi-Span AWP; CMS Part D Pricing; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Rebate amount computed by applying the contracted rebate rate to the utilization units and applicable price benchmark. Represents the expected receivable before invoicing.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606; SAP

description: Negotiated rebate rate expressed as a decimal percentage of Average Wholesale Price (AWP) or Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per the PBM contract terms. Confidential as it reflects proprietary pricing agreements.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; ERISA; OIG Compliance Guidance

description: Timestamp when this drug rebate record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this rebate record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency reporting for plans operating in US territories.
Free-text or coded reason for a rebate dispute when reconciliation_status is 'disputed'. Captures manufacturer objections, utilization data discrepancies, or contract interpretation differences.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; OIG Compliance Guidance

description: Date on which a disputed rebate was formally resolved. Null if no dispute exists or dispute is still open.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; OIG Compliance Guidance

description: Formulary tier at which the drug was placed during the rebate period, which determines the applicable rebate rate. Tier placement is a key driver of rebate negotiations.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Oracle Financials General Ledger account code to which the rebate receivable or cash receipt is posted. Supports financial close and statutory reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SAP; Oracle Financials Chart of Accounts

description: Date on which the rebate invoice was issued to the manufacturer or PBM. Marks the start of the payment due window per contract terms.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: Rebate amount formally invoiced to the manufacturer or PBM for the period. May differ from calculated amount due to adjustments, disputes, or rounding.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606; SAP

description: Line of business under which the drug utilization generating this rebate was incurred: commercial, medicare_part_d, medicaid, exchange (ACA marketplace), or individual. Drives MLR segmentation and CMS reporting.
reference: CMS Part D; ACA MLR Requirements; NAIC Model Regulation; DOI Reporting

description: Legal name of the pharmaceutical manufacturer obligated to pay the rebate. Retained on the transaction for reporting and reconciliation without requiring a join.
reference: CMS Part D Rebate Program; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Drug's market share within its therapeutic class during the rebate period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Used to determine market share rebate tier attainment per contract.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14

description: Classification of the rebate for ACA Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) reporting purposes: medical_cost_reduction (reduces incurred claims), quality_improvement (allocated to QI activities), or excluded (not counted in MLR numerator).
reference: ACA MLR Requirements 45 CFR 158; CMS MLR Annual Reporting Form; HHS

description: 11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) subject to the rebate. Formatted as 5-4-2 per FDA NDC Directory standards.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; CMS Part D NDC Crosswalk; 21 CFR 207

description: Indicates whether this rebate is subject to CMS Part D rebate pass-through requirements. True for Medicare Part D covered drugs; False for commercial or Medicaid lines of business.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; 42 CFR 423.505

description: Portion of the received rebate amount that must be passed through to CMS or plan members per Part D or ACA requirements. Critical for MLR and CMS DIR (Direct and Indirect Remuneration) reporting.
reference: CMS Part D DIR Reporting; ACA MLR Requirements 45 CFR 158; 42 CFR 423.505

description: Contractual date by which the manufacturer or PBM must remit the rebate payment. Used for aging analysis and dispute escalation.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; ERISA

description: Date on which the rebate payment was actually received and posted. Null if payment has not yet been received.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether the contractual performance target (e.g., market share threshold, utilization goal) was met during the rebate period, triggering performance rebate eligibility.
reference: PBM Contract Terms; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14

description: Indicates whether prior authorization was required for the drug during the rebate period. PA requirements can affect utilization volumes and rebate performance thresholds.
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization; NCQA UM Standards; InterQual/MCG

description: Externally-known invoice number assigned by the PBM or manufacturer for this rebate billing cycle. Used for reconciliation with accounts receivable and PBM remittance.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: Last day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period for which this rebate invoice applies.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: First day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period (typically a calendar quarter) for which this rebate invoice applies.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: Price benchmark on which the contracted rebate rate is applied: Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), or net price.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Classification of the rebate by its contractual basis: base (standard formulary placement), performance (quality/utilization targets), market_share (volume threshold), formulary_access (preferred tier), inflation (price increase above CPI), or supplemental (ad hoc negotiated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base|performance|market_share|formulary_access|inflation|supplemental — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: CMS Part D Rebate Program; NAIC Model Regulation; OIG Compliance Guidance

description: Actual cash amount received from the manufacturer or PBM for this rebate invoice. Used for cash reconciliation and MLR reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606; ACA MLR Requirements 45 CFR 158

description: Current lifecycle status of the rebate invoice reconciliation process: pending (not yet invoiced), invoiced (invoice sent), partially_received (partial payment), received (fully paid), disputed (discrepancy under review), written_off (uncollectable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|invoiced|partially_received|received|disputed|written_off — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606; ACA MLR Requirements

description: Identifies the operational source system from which this rebate record originated: RxClaim, Argus (PBM platforms), or manual (spreadsheet/manual entry). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312

description: Indicates whether the drug is classified as a specialty drug, which typically carries higher rebate rates and requires prior authorization. Specialty drugs are subject to distinct PBM contract terms.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Tier Definition; NCQA Pharmacy Standards; PBM Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether step therapy protocols were in effect for this drug during the rebate period. Step therapy requirements influence market share and performance rebate attainment.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy; NCQA UM Standards

description: Pharmacological therapeutic class code (e.g., GPI or ATC code) for the drug, used to aggregate rebates by drug category for formulary strategy and analytics.
reference: WHO ATC Classification; Medi-Span GPI; CMS Part D Drug Data

description: Timestamp when this drug rebate record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Number of pharmacy claims adjudicated for the drug during the rebate period. Used to validate utilization units and support audit of rebate calculations.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; NCQA HEDIS Pharmacy Measures

description: Total dispensed units (e.g., tablets, milliliters, grams) of the drug during the rebate period, as reported to the manufacturer for rebate calculation. Sourced from PBM claims data.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; RxClaim/Argus PBM Utilization Report

description: Difference between the invoiced amount and the received amount. A non-zero variance triggers reconciliation or dispute resolution workflows.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 14; GAAP ASC 606

description: Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit for the drug as of the rebate period. WAC is the manufacturer's list price and an alternative rebate rate basis to AWP.
reference: 42 USC 1395w-3a; CMS Part D Pricing; Medi-Span WAC

description: Primary key for mac_list
Self-referencing FK on mac_list (parent_mac_list_id)

Therapeutic classification of the drug.
Unique surrogate identifier for the formulary record. Primary key for the formulary data product in the pharmacy domain.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; HIPAA Administrative Simplification

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Formulary design must adhere to specific regulatory obligations (ACA, CMS); linking enables reporting of compliance status per formulary.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Formulary management assigns a responsible pharmacist/employee; needed for formulary change approval and governance reporting.
Reference to the immediately preceding version of this formulary. Enables formulary change tracking, member notification workflows, and historical adjudication lookups for appeals and grievances.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Change Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120(b)

description: Date on which member notification was sent for formulary changes. CMS Part D requires 60-day advance notice for non-preferred formulary changes and immediate notification for safety-related removals.
reference: CMS Part D 42 CFR Part 423.120(b)(3); CMS Member Notification Requirements

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Formulary Identifier
description: The CMS-assigned formulary identifier used in Part D formulary file submissions to CMS. Required for Medicare Part D plans to identify the formulary in RAPS and EDPS submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS HPMS Formulary Submission Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this formulary record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance documentation purposes.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; Enterprise Data Governance Audit Requirements

description: Total number of unique drug entries (NDC or drug-tier combinations) included in this formulary at the time of the last update. Used for formulary adequacy assessments and CMS Part D formulary file validation.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Adequacy Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120; CMS Formulary File Submission

description: Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) edits are applied during adjudication under this formulary. DUR programs screen for drug interactions, duplicate therapy, and inappropriate dosing per OBRA '90 requirements.
reference: OBRA '90 DUR Requirements; CMS Part D DUR Program; 42 CFR Part 423.153; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: The date on which this formulary becomes active and applicable to member benefit adjudication. Used by PBM systems (RxClaim/Argus) to determine which formulary version applies at point of dispensing.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120

description: Business classification of the formulary indicating its benefit richness level relative to other formularies offered by the plan. Used in benefit design analytics, member communications, and plan comparison tools.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Design Guidelines; NCQA Accreditation Standards; ACA SBC Requirements

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the formulary across systems, used in EDI transactions, PBM interfaces, and CMS Part D submissions. Corresponds to the formulary ID field in RxClaim/Argus PBM system.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Free-text business description of the formulary, including its intended population, key design features, and any notable coverage inclusions or exclusions. Used in internal documentation and plan benefit configuration.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Documentation Requirements; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Human-readable business name of the formulary (e.g., 'Standard Commercial Formulary 2025', 'Medicare Part D Enhanced Formulary'). Used in member-facing materials, SBC documents, and plan benefit configurations.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the formulary record. Active formularies are used in live adjudication; pending formularies are approved but not yet effective; retired formularies are historical records no longer in use.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Classification of the formulary structure indicating coverage breadth and tier design. Open formularies cover most drugs; closed formularies restrict to listed drugs only; tiered formularies apply differential cost-sharing by tier.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: Policy governing generic drug substitution at point of dispensing. Mandatory substitution requires dispensing of generic equivalents; permissive allows brand if requested; DAW (Dispense As Written) honored respects prescriber instructions.
reference: State Pharmacy Practice Acts; CMS Part D Generic Substitution Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Indicates whether this formulary meets ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) essential health benefit (EHB) drug coverage requirements, including the required drug categories and classes under 45 CFR Part 156.
reference: ACA 45 CFR Part 156; CMS QHP Formulary Requirements; HHS EHB Benchmark Plan

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Indicator
description: Indicates whether this formulary is subject to CMS Medicare Part D regulatory requirements, including annual bid submission, formulary file submission to CMS, and coverage determination obligations.
reference: CMS Part D; 42 CFR Part 423; Medicare Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee last reviewed and approved this formulary. P&T Committee review is required for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation.
reference: CMS Part D P&T Committee Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120(b)(1); NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Line of Business (LOB) associated with this formulary, indicating the regulatory and market segment under which the formulary operates. Drives CMS Part D, Medicaid, or commercial compliance requirements.
reference: CMS Part D 42 CFR Part 423; CMS Medicaid Drug Rebate Program; ACA QHP Requirements

description: Indicates whether this formulary is designated for use by Low Income Subsidy (LIS) eligible members under Medicare Part D. LIS formularies must meet CMS benchmark plan requirements for cost-sharing and drug coverage.
reference: CMS Part D LIS Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.780; CMS Low Income Subsidy Program

description: Indicates whether this formulary supports mail-order pharmacy dispensing. Mail-order programs typically offer 90-day supplies at reduced cost-sharing and are a key component of pharmacy benefit design.
reference: CMS Part D Mail Order Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Benefit Standards; State Mail Order Pharmacy Laws

description: Scheduled date for the next Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee review of this formulary. Supports compliance tracking for CMS Part D requirements mandating regular formulary review cycles.
reference: CMS Part D P&T Committee Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120(b)(1); NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: The formulary identifier as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the health plan's internal formulary record and the PBM's formulary configuration.
reference: RxClaim Formulary Management Module; Argus PBM System

description: The benefit plan year (e.g., 2025) for which this formulary is effective. Used for annual formulary change management, CMS Part D annual bid submissions, and benefit year reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Annual Bid Submission; 45 CFR Part 156 ACA QHP Requirements

description: Indicates whether this formulary requires prior authorization (PA) for any covered drugs. PA requirements are subject to CMS Part D coverage determination timelines and member appeal rights.
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.578; CMS Coverage Determination Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee Approval Date
description: Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee formally approved this formulary or the most recent formulary update. Required documentation for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation audits.
reference: CMS Part D P&T Committee Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120(b)(1); NCQA HP Accreditation Standards

description: Indicates whether this formulary applies quantity limits (QL) on covered drugs, restricting the amount dispensed per fill or per defined time period. Quantity limits are a utilization management tool subject to CMS Part D exception requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.578; CMS Coverage Determination Guidelines

description: The date on which CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance approved this formulary for use. Required for Part D compliance documentation and audit trails.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Approval; 42 CFR Part 423; State DOI Approval Process

description: The date on which the formulary was submitted to CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance for regulatory review and approval.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Submission Deadlines; 42 CFR Part 423; State DOI Filing Requirements

description: Status of the formulary's regulatory filing with CMS or applicable State Department of Insurance (DOI). Tracks whether the formulary has been submitted, approved, or rejected for compliance with Part D or state market requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Submission; 42 CFR Part 423; State DOI Filing Requirements; NAIC Model Laws

description: Identifies the operational source system from which this formulary record originated (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM system, Facets). Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; HIPAA Administrative Simplification

description: Indicates whether this formulary designates a specialty pharmacy network for dispensing high-cost specialty drugs. Specialty pharmacy programs may include limited distribution networks and enhanced clinical support services.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Pharmacy Requirements; URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Indicates whether this formulary includes a specialty drug tier for high-cost specialty medications. Specialty tiers typically apply to drugs with monthly costs exceeding CMS specialty tier threshold (currently $670/month for Part D).
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Tier Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.578; CMS HPMS Formulary Guidance

description: Indicates whether this formulary employs step therapy (also known as fail-first) protocols requiring members to try lower-cost or preferred drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423; State Step Therapy Laws; URAC Utilization Management Standards

description: The date on which this formulary is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended formulary with no defined end date. Used for formulary version management and historical adjudication lookups.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120

description: The total number of cost-sharing tiers defined in this formulary (e.g., 3-tier, 4-tier, 5-tier, 6-tier). Determines the tier structure used in drug tier assignments and member cost-sharing calculations.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: Timestamp when this formulary record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit trail requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; Enterprise Data Governance Audit Requirements

description: Public-facing URL where members can access the formulary drug list. CMS Part D and ACA QHP regulations require health plans to publish formularies online and provide the URL in plan materials and SBC documents.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Transparency Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.128; ACA 45 CFR Part 156.230; SBC Requirements

description: Version number of the formulary, incremented with each approved update or mid-year formulary change. Used to track formulary change history and ensure members receive required 60-day advance notice of non-preferred changes.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Change Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423.120(b); NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each formulary drug tier assignment record. Primary key for this association entity linking a drug (NDC) to a formulary with its tier, cost-sharing, and utilization management (UM) controls.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; HIPAA EDI Standards

description: Reference to the internal drug master record corresponding to this NDC, enabling joins to the drug master for drug name, therapeutic class, and other drug attributes.
reference: PBM Drug Master; RxClaim/Argus Drug File

description: Reference to the formulary to which this drug tier assignment belongs. A formulary is the approved list of covered drugs for a given plan and benefit year.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File; 42 CFR §423.120

description: Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. Links the tier assignment to the specific plan for cost-sharing and benefit configuration.
reference: CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP); 42 CFR §423

description: The plan benefit year (e.g., 2024, 2025) to which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. CMS Part D formulary files are submitted annually by benefit year.
reference: CMS Part D Annual Formulary Submission; 42 CFR §423.265

description: Member coinsurance percentage applied to the drug cost under this formulary tier assignment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20%). Used when cost-sharing is percentage-based rather than fixed copay. Applies to specialty and high-cost tiers.
reference: ACA §2715 SBC Requirements; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed through a mail-order pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Mail-order copays are typically lower than retail to incentivize mail-order utilization.
reference: ACA §2715 SBC Requirements; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Fixed member copay amount for a 30-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. Used in SBC and EOB generation.
reference: ACA §2715 SBC Requirements; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Applicable for maintenance medications at extended-supply retail pharmacies.
reference: ACA §2715 SBC Requirements; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules; 42 CFR §423.104

description: Indicates whether the drug is covered under this formulary assignment. 'covered' = fully covered per tier rules; 'covered_with_restrictions' = covered subject to UM controls (PA, ST, QL); 'not_covered' = not on formulary; 'excluded' = explicitly excluded per CMS or plan rules.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.120(b)

description: Timestamp when this formulary drug tier assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312; CMS Part D Record Retention Requirements

description: Indicates whether the plan deductible must be met before cost-sharing for this drug tier applies. Relevant for High Deductible Health Plans (HDHP) and plans with drug-specific deductibles.
reference: IRS HSA/HDHP Rules; ACA §2715; CMS Part D Cost-Sharing Rules

description: Pharmacy dispensing channel(s) through which this drug may be dispensed under this formulary tier assignment. 'specialty' indicates dispensing is restricted to a specialty pharmacy network. Drives pharmacy routing at point of sale.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File; PBM Contract Terms; RxClaim/Argus Channel Configuration

description: Business reason for a change to the drug's tier assignment (e.g., 'generic available', 'contract renegotiation', 'clinical review', 'CMS requirement', 'formulary refresh'). Supports audit trail and CMS formulary change notification requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Change Notification Requirements; 42 CFR §423.120(b)(3)

description: Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert associated with this formulary drug tier assignment (e.g., 'drug-drug interaction', 'drug-disease contraindication', 'duplicate therapy', 'dose range check'). Drives DUR edits at point of sale in the PBM adjudication system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drug_drug_interaction|drug_disease|duplicate_therapy|dose_range|drug_allergy|drug_age — promote to reference product]
reference: OBRA '90 DUR Requirements; CMS Part D DUR Program; RxClaim/Argus DUR Configuration

description: Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment becomes effective. Used to determine the applicable tier and cost-sharing rules at the time of dispensing. Required for CMS formulary file submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.120

description: CMS-defined formulary status code used in the Part D formulary file submission. Values: 1=Covered, 2=Covered with Restrictions, 3=Not Covered, 4=Covered (Grandfathered), 5=Covered (Transition), 6=Covered (LIS). Required field in CMS formulary extract file.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.120

description: Line of business to which this formulary drug tier assignment applies (e.g., 'commercial', 'medicare_part_d', 'medicaid', 'exchange', 'fehb'). Determines applicable regulatory requirements and cost-sharing rules.
reference: CMS Part D; Medicaid Drug Rebate Program; ACA Exchange Requirements; FEHB Program

business_glossary_term: Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) Pricing Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) pricing applies to this drug under this formulary tier assignment. MAC pricing caps reimbursement for generic drugs and is a key PBM contract term.
reference: MAC Pricing Regulations; PBM Contract Terms; State MAC Transparency Laws

description: 11-digit National Drug Code uniquely identifying the specific drug product, dosage form, and package size assigned to this formulary tier. NDC is the primary drug identifier used in pharmacy claim adjudication and CMS formulary file submissions.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 21 CFR §207

description: Narrative description of the clinical criteria that must be met for prior authorization approval of this drug. Supports PA adjudication, member appeals, and CMS/NCQA compliance documentation.
reference: CMS Part D PA Extract File; NCQA UM Standards; InterQual/MCG Clinical Criteria

description: Type of prior authorization process required for this drug (e.g., 'standard' = standard review timeline; 'expedited' = urgent/expedited review; 'retrospective' = post-service review). Null or 'not_applicable' if prior_auth_required is false.
reference: CMS Part D PA Extract File; 42 CFR §423.568; NCQA UM Standards

description: PBM system-specific formulary identifier used in RxClaim or Argus to reference this formulary configuration. Enables reconciliation between the health plan's internal formulary records and the PBM's adjudication system.
reference: RxClaim/Argus PBM Formulary Configuration; PBM Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether prior authorization (PA) is required before this drug will be covered under this formulary tier assignment. When true, a PA request must be approved before the claim will adjudicate. Reported in the CMS PA extract file.
reference: CMS Part D PA Extract File; 42 CFR §423.578; NCQA UM Standards

description: Clinical rationale or basis for the quantity limit rule (e.g., 'FDA-approved maximum dose', 'clinical guidelines', 'safety limit'). Documents the evidence basis for the QL control as required for CMS and NCQA compliance.
reference: CMS Part D QL Extract File; NCQA UM Standards; FDA Drug Labeling

description: The number of days supply over which the maximum quantity limit applies (e.g., 30 days, 90 days). Defines the time window for the quantity limit rule. Null if quantity_limit_required is false.
reference: CMS Part D QL Extract File; RxClaim/Argus QL Configuration

description: Specifies any dosage form restrictions associated with the quantity limit rule (e.g., 'tablet only', 'extended-release excluded', 'oral solution only'). Captures clinical basis for dosage form-specific QL controls.
reference: CMS Part D QL Extract File; FDA Drug Labeling

description: Maximum quantity of the drug allowed per fill or per defined time period under the quantity limit rule. Expressed in the drug's dispensing unit (e.g., tablets, mL, units). Null if quantity_limit_required is false.
reference: CMS Part D QL Extract File; RxClaim/Argus QL Configuration

description: Indicates whether a quantity limit (QL) applies to this drug under this formulary assignment. When true, the dispensed quantity per fill or per time period is restricted. Reported in the CMS QL extract file.
reference: CMS Part D QL Extract File; 42 CFR §423.578

description: Lifecycle status of this formulary drug tier assignment record. 'active' = currently in effect; 'inactive' = terminated or expired; 'pending' = approved but not yet effective; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer version of the same assignment.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File; RxClaim/Argus Formulary Management

description: Indicates whether this drug is designated as a specialty drug under this formulary. Specialty drugs typically require dispensing through a specialty pharmacy, have higher cost-sharing, and may require PA. Used in specialty pharmacy channel routing and PBM contract management.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File; NCQA Specialty Drug Criteria; PBM Contract Terms

description: Clinical rationale or evidence basis for the step therapy protocol (e.g., 'preferred generic available', 'clinical guidelines recommend first-line therapy', 'cost-effectiveness'). Required for CMS and NCQA UM documentation.
reference: CMS Part D ST Extract File; NCQA UM Standards; InterQual/MCG Clinical Criteria

description: Criteria under which the step therapy requirement may be overridden without completing the required step sequence (e.g., 'contraindication to step drug', 'prior treatment failure documented', 'clinical exception'). Supports PA override adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D ST Extract File; NCQA UM Standards; State Step Therapy Override Laws

business_glossary_term: Step Therapy (ST) Required Step Drug NDC
description: National Drug Code (NDC) of the required step drug that must be tried and failed before coverage of this drug is approved under the step therapy protocol. Null if step_therapy_required is false.
reference: CMS Part D ST Extract File; RxClaim/Argus ST Configuration

description: The sequence number of this drug in the step therapy protocol (e.g., 1 = first-line, 2 = second-line). Defines the order in which step drugs must be tried and failed before coverage of the target drug is approved. Null if step_therapy_required is false.
reference: CMS Part D ST Extract File; RxClaim/Argus ST Configuration

description: Indicates whether step therapy (ST) applies to this drug under this formulary assignment. When true, the member must first try and fail one or more preferred (step) drugs before coverage of this drug is approved. Reported in the CMS ST extract file.
reference: CMS Part D ST Extract File; 42 CFR §423.578; NCQA UM Standards

description: Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment expires or is terminated. Null indicates the assignment is currently active with no defined end date. Supports historical tracking and CMS formulary file change submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.120

description: Human-readable label for the drug tier (e.g., 'Generic', 'Preferred Brand', 'Non-Preferred Brand', 'Specialty', 'Specialty Preferred'). Displayed on member-facing materials including the Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) and Explanation of Benefits (EOB).
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File; SBC Requirements under ACA §2715

description: Numeric tier level assigned to the drug within the formulary (e.g., 1=Generic, 2=Preferred Brand, 3=Non-Preferred Brand, 4=Specialty, 5=Specialty Preferred, 6=Select Care). Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. CMS requires tier number in formulary file submissions.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary File Submission Requirements; 42 CFR §423.578

description: Indicates whether a transition fill is permitted for this drug when a member is new to the plan or the drug's formulary status has changed. CMS Part D requires transition fills for certain drugs during the transition period.
reference: CMS Part D Transition Policy; 42 CFR §423.120(b)(3); CMS Memo on Transition Fills

description: Timestamp when this formulary drug tier assignment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and CMS formulary change notification compliance.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312; CMS Part D Record Retention Requirements

description: Primary key for formulary_exception

Identifier of the associated appeal record if the member or prescriber has filed an appeal against a denied formulary exception. Links to the appeals management entity. Null if no appeal has been filed.
reference: CMS Part D §423.580–423.602; 42 CFR §423.580; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Formulary exception requests are reviewed under regulatory obligations; linking captures which obligation governs each exception.
Identifier of the clinical pharmacist, pharmacy technician, or medical director who reviewed and rendered the decision on the formulary exception request. Links to the workforce/staff entity.
reference: CMS Part D §423.578; NCQA Utilization Management Standards

description: Identifier of the specific formulary against which the exception is being evaluated. A plan may maintain multiple formularies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid). Links to the formulary master in the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements 42 CFR §423.120; USP Drug Classification

description: Identifier of the health benefit plan under which the formulary exception is being requested. Determines the applicable formulary, tier structure, and exception adjudication rules.
reference: CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP); ACA §1301

description: Identifier of the health plan member on whose behalf the formulary exception request is submitted. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).

External transaction identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) to this formulary exception request. Used for reconciliation between the health plan's internal records and the PBM system of record.
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; CMS Part D Encounter Data Reporting

description: Identifier of an associated Prior Authorization (PA) request in the UM system (InterQual/MCG) when the formulary exception is linked to or triggered by a PA requirement. Null if no PA is associated.
reference: CMS Part D §423.566; HIPAA Prior Authorization Standards; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Date on which the appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial. Required for CMS Part D compliance tracking and audit purposes.
reference: CMS Part D §423.572(b); 42 CFR §423.580; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Indicates whether the required appeal rights notification was sent to the member and prescriber following a denial decision, as mandated by CMS Part D regulations. True = notification sent; False = notification not yet sent.
reference: CMS Part D §423.572(b); 42 CFR §423.580; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Free-text clinical rationale provided by the prescriber or member supporting the formulary exception request. May include diagnosis, treatment history, contraindications to formulary alternatives, or medical necessity documentation. Treated as confidential clinical information.
reference: CMS Part D §423.578; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.502; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Coverage Determination Type
description: CMS-defined classification of the coverage determination process type for Part D reporting. Standard: routine 72-hour review; Expedited: 24-hour urgent review; Reopen: plan-initiated reconsideration; Revised: updated determination on a prior decision.
reference: CMS Part D §423.568; §423.570; §423.572; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Timestamp when the formulary exception record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: The formulary tier at which the requested drug is currently placed (or non_formulary if not on the formulary) at the time the exception request is submitted. Used to assess the tier differential and cost-sharing impact of the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|tier_4|tier_5|specialty|non_formulary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements 42 CFR §423.120; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 6

description: Number of days of drug supply requested in the formulary exception, applicable for quantity limit or day supply override exceptions. Standard values are 30, 60, or 90 days.
reference: CMS Part D Day Supply Requirements; NCPDP Day Supply Standards

description: Calendar date on which the final determination (approval or denial) was made on the formulary exception request. Used to calculate turnaround time and assess CMS timeliness compliance.
reference: CMS Part D §423.568; 42 CFR §423.572; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Precise date and time the exception determination was rendered. Enables exact measurement of response time against CMS 72-hour standard and 24-hour expedited decision deadlines.
reference: CMS Part D §423.568; §423.570; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for denial of the formulary exception request (e.g., formulary alternative available, insufficient clinical documentation, drug not medically necessary). Null for approved or pending requests. Used in EOB and appeal rights notifications.
reference: CMS Part D §423.572; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18; NCQA Standards

description: Human-readable explanation of the reason for denial of the formulary exception request. Included in the written notice of denial sent to the member and prescriber as required by CMS Part D regulations.
reference: CMS Part D §423.572; 42 CFR §423.572(b); CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: International Classification of Diseases (ICD-10-CM) diagnosis code supporting the medical necessity of the requested drug. Provided by the prescriber as part of the clinical justification for the exception. Treated as confidential health information.
reference: WHO ICD-10-CM; CMS Part D §423.578; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.1002

description: Brand or generic name of the drug for which the formulary exception is requested, as submitted by the requestor. Stored for human-readable reference alongside the NDC code.
reference: FDA Drug Labeling Requirements 21 CFR §201; USP Drug Classification

description: 11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) of the specific drug product for which the formulary exception is requested. Identifies the labeler, product, and package configuration at the NDC level.
reference: FDA NDC Directory 21 CFR §207.33; CMS Part D §423.120

description: Date through which an approved formulary exception remains valid. After this date, the member must reapply or the standard formulary restriction resumes. Null for open-ended approvals or non-approved requests.
reference: CMS Part D §423.566; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Date from which an approved formulary exception is valid and the member may obtain the drug at the excepted tier or without the formulary restriction. Null for denied or pending requests.
reference: CMS Part D §423.566; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Externally-visible, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the formulary exception request at intake. Used by members, prescribers, and pharmacies to track the status of the request. Generated by the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus) or UM system.
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Determination and Exception Request Requirements 42 CFR §423.566

description: Current lifecycle status of the formulary exception request. Pending: under review; Approved: exception granted; Denied: exception not granted; Withdrawn: requestor cancelled; Expired: approved exception past effective period; Appealed: denial under appeal review.
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Determination and Exception Request Requirements 42 CFR §423.566; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Classification of the formulary exception request indicating the specific restriction being challenged. Values: non_formulary (drug not on formulary), tier_exception (request lower cost-sharing tier), pa_exception (override Prior Authorization requirement), step_therapy_exception (bypass step therapy protocol), quantity_limit_exception (override quantity/day supply limit), coverage_determination (standard CMS Part D coverage determination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_formulary|tier_exception|pa_exception|step_therapy_exception|quantity_limit_exception|coverage_determination — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Coverage Determinations and Exceptions 42 CFR §423.566–423.600; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

business_glossary_term: Formulary Alternative Drug National Drug Code (NDC)
description: 11-digit NDC of the formulary-covered alternative drug suggested by the plan when denying a non-formulary or tier exception request. Required by CMS to be communicated to the member and prescriber in the denial notice.
reference: CMS Part D §423.572; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18; FDA NDC Directory

description: Indicates whether the formulary exception request was submitted and processed under the expedited (24-hour) review pathway, as opposed to the standard (72-hour) pathway. Expedited requests require prescriber attestation that the standard timeframe would seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function.
reference: CMS Part D §423.570; 42 CFR §423.570(b); CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Line of Business (LOB) under which the formulary exception is processed. Determines applicable regulatory framework, formulary rules, and exception adjudication standards (e.g., CMS Part D rules apply only to Medicare Part D LOB).
reference: CMS Part D 42 CFR §423; ACA §1301; NAIC Model Laws

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or authorized prescriber who submitted or supports the formulary exception request. Required for CMS Part D coverage determination documentation.
reference: CMS NPI Final Rule 45 CFR §162.406; CMS Part D §423.566

description: The quantity of the drug (in dispensing units) for which the exception is requested, applicable for quantity limit exception requests. Expressed in the drug's standard dispensing unit (e.g., tablets, milliliters).
reference: CMS Part D Quantity Limit Requirements; NCPDP Quantity Dispensed Standards

description: Channel through which the formulary exception request was submitted. Values: phone (member/prescriber call), fax (fax submission), portal (member or prescriber web portal), edi (electronic data interchange transaction), mail (paper/postal), prescriber_portal (dedicated prescriber e-prescribing or PBM portal).
reference: CMS Part D §423.566; HIPAA EDI Transaction Standards

description: Calendar date on which the formulary exception request was formally submitted by the member, prescriber, or authorized representative. This is the principal business event date used for CMS timeliness compliance tracking (72-hour standard, 24-hour expedited).
Precise date and time the formulary exception request was received and logged in the PBM or UM system. Used for exact timeliness measurement against CMS regulatory response deadlines.
reference: CMS Part D §423.568; 42 CFR §423.570; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: The formulary cost-sharing tier to which the requestor is asking the drug to be assigned for this member (e.g., requesting Tier 2 generic pricing for a Tier 3 preferred brand). Applicable for tier exception requests.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Tier Structure 42 CFR §423.120; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 6

description: Identifies who initiated the formulary exception request. Member: the enrollee themselves; Prescriber: the treating physician or authorized prescriber; Authorized Representative: a legally designated representative acting on behalf of the member; Pharmacy: the dispensing pharmacy acting on behalf of the member.
reference: CMS Part D §423.566(b); 42 CFR §423.566; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Level of clinical review applied to the formulary exception request. Pharmacist: initial review by clinical pharmacist; Medical Director: escalated review by physician medical director; Peer to Peer: prescriber-to-medical-director discussion; Committee: pharmacy and therapeutics committee review.
reference: CMS Part D §423.578; NCQA Utilization Management Standards; URAC Pharmacy Benefit Management Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this formulary exception record originated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer. Values: rxclaim, argus (PBM systems), interqual, mcg (UM systems), facets, qnxt (core admin), manual (manually entered). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rxclaim|argus|interqual|mcg|facets|qnxt|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Comma-separated list of NDCs or drug names of step therapy prerequisite drugs that the member has already tried and failed, as documented in the step therapy exception request. Supports clinical review and CMS step therapy override requirements.
reference: CMS Step Therapy Override Requirements 42 CFR §423.578; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Indicates whether the required clinical supporting documentation (e.g., prescriber letter of medical necessity, lab results, treatment history) has been received from the prescriber or member. True = documentation received; False = documentation pending or not received.
reference: CMS Part D §423.578; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Date on which the required clinical supporting documentation was received from the prescriber or member. Used to calculate the effective review start date and CMS timeliness compliance.
reference: CMS Part D §423.578; CMS Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 18

description: Timestamp when the formulary exception record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks status changes, decision updates, and documentation additions throughout the exception lifecycle.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: Primary key for mtm_service
Reference to the Part D prescription drug plan (PDP) or Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MA-PD) plan under which the member is enrolled and MTM eligibility is determined.
reference: CMS Part D Plan Benefit Package (PBP); 42 CFR § 423.153(d)

description: Reference to the health plan member who received the MTM service. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.514; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Identifier for the specific MTM program offering under which this service was delivered. A plan sponsor may operate multiple MTM programs targeting different chronic condition cohorts.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Number of qualifying chronic conditions the member has at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of chronic conditions (typically 2-3 depending on plan criteria). Used for eligibility validation and population stratification.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Date on which the annual Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) was completed for the member. CMS requires at least one CMR per calendar year for all enrolled MTM beneficiaries. Directly drives the CMS STAR Ratings MTM CMR Completion Rate measure.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d)); CMS STAR Ratings Technical Notes

description: CMS Part D reporting period to which this MTM service record is attributed (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-ANNUAL). Used for CMS Part D MTM program reporting submissions and STAR ratings measurement year alignment.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; CMS STAR Ratings Technical Notes

description: Total number of outreach attempts made to the member to schedule or deliver the MTM service. CMS requires documentation of good-faith efforts to reach enrolled members. Used for compliance audits and outreach effectiveness analytics.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate Measure

description: Timestamp when this MTM service record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.312 (Audit Controls)

description: Date on which the member was disenrolled or opted out of the MTM program. Null if the member remains enrolled. Used to track program retention and calculate MTM completion rates.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Primary category of drug therapy problem identified during the MTM service. Common types include: unnecessary drug therapy, needs additional drug therapy, ineffective drug, dosage too low, dosage too high, adverse drug reaction, non-adherence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNNECESSARY_DRUG|NEEDS_ADDITIONAL_DRUG|INEFFECTIVE_DRUG|DOSE_TOO_LOW|DOSE_TOO_HIGH|ADVERSE_REACTION|NON_ADHERENCE|NONE — promote to reference product]
reference: Cipolle, Strand & Morley Pharmacotherapy Workup; NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards

description: NCPDP-standard Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code documenting the result of the pharmacist's clinical intervention during the MTM service (e.g., 1A = filled as is, 1B = filled with prescriber approval, 1C = not filled). Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system DUR transaction records.
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; NCPDP DUR Outcome Codes; CMS Part D DUR Requirements

description: Date on which the member was determined to be eligible for the MTM program based on CMS Part D criteria (multiple chronic conditions, multiple Part D drugs, likely to incur annual costs above the CMS threshold).
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Date on which the eligible member was enrolled into the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment of all eligible beneficiaries with opt-out capability.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

business_glossary_term: Estimated Annual Part D Drug Cost
description: Estimated annual cost of Part D covered drugs for the member at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS requires members likely to incur annual drug costs above the established threshold to be enrolled. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d)); CMS Annual MTM Cost Threshold

description: Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up contact or service following the MTM encounter. Null if no follow-up is required or scheduled. Used to track closure of open MAP items and clinical recommendations.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards

description: Indicates whether a follow-up contact or service is required after the current MTM encounter based on clinical findings or unresolved MAP items. True = follow-up required; False = no follow-up needed.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards

description: Current status of the follow-up action required after the MTM service. PENDING = follow-up scheduled but not yet completed; COMPLETED = follow-up successfully conducted; DECLINED = member declined follow-up; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted for follow-up; NOT_REQUIRED = no follow-up needed.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards

description: Total number of clinical interventions or recommendations made during the MTM service encounter (e.g., drug therapy problem resolutions, adherence recommendations, prescriber referrals). Used for quality reporting and pharmacist productivity analytics.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards

description: Date of the most recent outreach attempt made to the member for MTM scheduling or delivery. Used alongside contact_attempt_count to document good-faith outreach efforts for CMS compliance.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Number of action items documented in the Medication Action Plan (MAP) provided to the member. Each MAP item represents a specific recommendation or goal agreed upon between the pharmacist and the member.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR

description: Indicates whether a written Medication Action Plan (MAP) was provided to the member following the CMR. CMS requires a standardized MAP to be delivered to the member and their prescriber(s) after each CMR. True = MAP provided; False = MAP not yet provided.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Indicates whether the member provided consent to participate in the MTM service and for sharing of clinical findings with their prescriber(s). True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or pending. Required for HIPAA compliance when sharing PHI with prescribers.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.506; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Indicates whether the member has opted out of the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment with opt-out capability. True = member has opted out; False = member remains enrolled. When True, disenrollment_date should be populated.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Reason code for the member's opt-out or disenrollment from the MTM program. Null if the member has not opted out. Used for program retention analysis and CMS reporting.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Number of Part D covered drugs the member is taking at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of Part D drugs (typically 8 or more). Used for eligibility validation.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) used to cross-reference the MTM record with pharmacy claims and formulary data.
reference: PBM System Integration Standards; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Indicates whether a Personal Medication List (PML) was provided to the member as part of the CMR standardized format. CMS requires both a MAP and a PML to be delivered following each CMR. True = PML provided; False = PML not provided.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacist or clinical pharmacist who delivered the MTM service, including Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) or Targeted Medication Review (TMR). Required for CMS Part D MTM reporting.
reference: CMS NPI Registry; HIPAA 45 CFR § 162.406; CMS Part D MTM Reporting Requirements

description: Free-text clinical notes documenting the pharmacist's recommendations, findings, and interventions during the MTM service encounter. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI) and must be handled in accordance with HIPAA Privacy Rule requirements.
