reference: HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.502; CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR

description: Date on which the prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings and MAP items. Null if notification has not yet been sent. Used for compliance tracking against CMS timelines.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Indicates whether the member's prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings, recommendations, or MAP items. CMS requires that the MAP and relevant clinical findings be shared with the prescriber. True = prescriber notified; False = notification pending or not completed.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Standardized Format for CMR; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Calendar date on which the MTM service (CMR, TMR, or follow-up) was delivered to the member. This is the principal real-world event date used for CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measure calculations.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d)); CMS STAR Ratings

description: Mode through which the MTM service was delivered to the member. CMS requires CMRs to be offered as interactive, person-to-person consultations (in-person or telehealth). Supports channel-level analytics and compliance verification.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; CMS Guidance on MTM CMR Delivery Modes

description: Duration of the MTM service encounter in minutes. Used for pharmacist productivity reporting, billing reconciliation with PBM, and quality analytics. CMR encounters typically range from 30 to 60 minutes.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; PBM MTM Billing Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the MTM service record. SCHEDULED = appointment set; IN_PROGRESS = service initiated; COMPLETED = service fully delivered and documented; DECLINED = member declined participation; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted; CANCELLED = service cancelled prior to delivery.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements; CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate Measure

description: Classification of the MTM service delivered. CMR = Comprehensive Medication Review (annual face-to-face or telehealth); TMR = Targeted Medication Review (quarterly or as needed); FOLLOW_UP = follow-up contact on prior CMR/TMR; REFERRAL = referral to prescriber or specialist; OTHER = other MTM-related service.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d)); CMS MTM Standardized Format

description: Code identifying the operational source system from which this MTM service record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across PBM (RxClaim/Argus) and Care Management (Casenet TruCare/Altruista) platforms.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.312 (Audit Controls)

description: Native record identifier from the originating source system (RxClaim, Argus, Casenet TruCare, or Altruista) for this MTM service record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Conventions

description: Indicates whether this MTM service record is eligible to be counted toward the CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate for Comprehensive Medication Reviews (CMR) quality measure. True = eligible for STAR measure numerator/denominator; False = excluded from STAR measure calculation.
reference: CMS STAR Ratings Technical Notes; CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements

description: Date on which the most recent Targeted Medication Review (TMR) was conducted. TMRs are performed at least quarterly for enrolled members who have not yet received a CMR or as clinically indicated. Null if no TMR has been performed.
reference: CMS Part D MTM Program Requirements (42 CFR § 423.153(d))

description: Timestamp when this MTM service record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; HIPAA 45 CFR § 164.312 (Audit Controls)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the specialty drug program record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Reference to the formulary under which this specialty drug program operates. Links the program to the applicable drug tier structure and coverage rules managed in the PBM system.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Guidance; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this specialty drug program is offered. Links to the plan and benefit domain for coverage determination and member eligibility.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; ACA Section 1302

description: Reference to the PBM contract governing the administration of this specialty drug program, including pricing terms (MAC, AWP, WAC), dispensing fees, and performance guarantees.
reference: ERISA Section 408(b)(2); CMS Part D PBM Oversight Guidance

description: Source system identifier for this program as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational PBM system.
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; Internal PBM Integration Specification

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Specialty drug programs are overseen by a program manager employee; required for program governance and reporting.

Indicates whether the Copay Accumulator Adjustment Program (CAAP) applies to this specialty drug program. When true, manufacturer copay assistance payments do NOT count toward the member's deductible or Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP). Critical for CMS Part D compliance.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); HHS Final Rule CMS-4190-F

description: Contracted discount percentage applied to the Average Wholesale Price (AWP) for specialty drugs dispensed under this program. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1800 = 18% discount). Governs ingredient cost reimbursement to specialty pharmacies.
reference: CMS Part D Pricing Guidance; ERISA Section 408(b)(2); MAC/AWP/WAC Industry Standards

description: Indicates whether drugs in this program require active clinical management, including disease management, case management, or care coordination through the Care Management Platform (Casenet TruCare or Altruista).
reference: NCQA Disease Management Standards; URAC Care Management Standards

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Specialty Tier Flag
description: Indicates whether this program is reportable to CMS under the Part D specialty tier requirements. Drives inclusion in CMS Part D formulary submissions and Star Ratings (STAR) calculations.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 6; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates whether drugs in this program require temperature-controlled cold chain handling and storage during dispensing and shipping. Drives specialty pharmacy network eligibility and logistics requirements.
reference: USP Chapter 1079 Good Storage and Distribution Practices; FDA Drug Supply Chain Security Act

description: Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (copay card) is available for drugs in this specialty program. Triggers copay accumulator adjustment program (CAAP) evaluation logic.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); OIG Advisory Opinion

description: Maximum dollar amount of manufacturer copay assistance benefit available to a member per program year under this specialty drug program. Used in accumulator adjustment calculations and OOP tracking.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); ERISA Section 712

description: Name of the manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (e.g., copay card program) associated with this specialty drug program. Used for affordability program tracking and OIG compliance reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); OIG Advisory Opinion

description: Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements under HIPAA and CMS.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; CMS Data Retention Requirements; NAIC Model Audit Rule

description: Contracted dispensing fee paid to specialty pharmacies per prescription dispensed under this program. Expressed in USD. Component of total pharmacy reimbursement alongside ingredient cost.
reference: CMS Part D Pricing Guidance; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; ERISA Section 408(b)(2)

description: Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) applied to claims under this specialty program. Prospective DUR occurs at point-of-sale; retrospective DUR occurs post-adjudication for quality and safety analysis.
reference: OBRA 1990 DUR Requirements; CMS Part D DUR Program Guidance; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Date on which the specialty drug program becomes active and eligible for member enrollment and claim adjudication. Aligns with plan year or contract effective dates.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; ACA Section 1302

description: Indicates whether independent charitable foundation grants are available to assist members with cost-sharing obligations for drugs in this specialty program. Foundation grants may or may not count toward OOP accumulators depending on CMS guidance.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); OIG Advisory Opinion 06-06

description: Indicates whether members must enroll in a manufacturer-sponsored hub services program to access drugs under this specialty program. Hub services may include patient support, adherence programs, and financial assistance coordination.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; OIG Compliance Guidance

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Condition Codes
description: Comma-delimited list of ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes representing the covered clinical conditions for which drugs in this program are indicated. Used for prior authorization (PA) criteria and medical necessity determination.
reference: WHO ICD-10-CM; CMS ICD-10 Implementation Guide; HIPAA Transaction Standards

description: Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; CMS Data Retention Requirements; NAIC Model Audit Rule

description: Indicates whether the drugs in this program are classified as Limited Distribution Drugs (LDD), available only through a restricted network of specialty pharmacies designated by the manufacturer.
reference: FDA Drug Supply Chain Security Act; CMS Part D Formulary Guidance

description: The insurance line of business (LOB) under which this specialty drug program operates. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and CMS reporting obligations.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NAIC Uniform Coding Standards

description: Indicates whether drugs in this program may be dispensed via mail-order pharmacy channels in addition to retail specialty pharmacies.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Maximum number of days supply permitted per dispensing event for specialty drugs under this program. Typically 30 days for specialty drugs per CMS Part D guidance. Enforced at PBM adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Indicates whether third-party assistance payments (copay cards, foundation grants) applied to this program count toward the member's Out-of-Pocket (OOP) and Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) accumulators. Governs accumulator adjustment compliance.
reference: CMS Part D Copay Accumulator Guidance (CMS-4190-F); ACA Section 1302(c)

description: Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored Patient Assistance Program (PAP) is available for uninsured or underinsured members enrolled in this specialty drug program.
reference: OIG Compliance Guidance; CMS Part D Low Income Subsidy (LIS) Guidance

description: Indicates whether Prior Authorization (PA) is required for all drugs covered under this specialty program before dispensing. Drives integration with the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual or MCG).
reference: CMS Part D Prior Authorization Guidance; URAC Utilization Management Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the specialty drug program within the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used in EDI transactions and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Detailed narrative description of the specialty drug program, including clinical rationale, covered conditions, and program objectives. Used in plan documents, Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC), and member communications.
reference: ACA Section 2715 (SBC Requirements); CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual

description: Human-readable name of the specialty drug program (e.g., 'Oncology Specialty Tier Program', 'Rheumatology Hub Services Program'). Used in member-facing communications and plan documents.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the specialty drug program. Controls whether the program is available for member enrollment and claim adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; Internal PBM Operations Policy

description: Classification of the specialty drug program by its primary operational function. Drives downstream adjudication logic and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty_tier|hub_services|copay_assistance|patient_assistance|rems|cold_chain|limited_distribution — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) restrictions apply to drugs dispensed under this specialty program. Quantity limits are enforced at the PBM adjudication level.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the specialty drugs in this program are subject to an FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS). When true, dispensing requires REMS-certified pharmacy and prescriber enrollment.
reference: FDA REMS Program Requirements (21 CFR Part 208); HIPAA

description: Name of the specialty drug list (SDL) associated with this program, identifying the set of National Drug Codes (NDCs) covered under the program. Managed in the PBM formulary system.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Guidance; NCPDP SCRIPT Standard

description: Name of the specialty pharmacy network designated to dispense drugs under this program. Defines the restricted network of pharmacies eligible to fill specialty prescriptions.
reference: CMS Part D Network Adequacy Standards; URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation

description: Formulary tier designation assigned to specialty drugs in this program. Determines member cost-sharing obligations and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual Chapter 6; ACA Section 1302

description: Indicates whether step therapy protocols are enforced for drugs in this program, requiring members to try and fail on preferred therapies before accessing specialty drugs. Governed by state step therapy laws and CMS guidance.
reference: CMS Part D Step Therapy Guidance; NCQA Pharmacy Standards

description: Date on which the specialty drug program is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended program with no scheduled end. Used for plan year transitions and CMS Part D reporting.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Primary therapeutic or disease category associated with the specialty drugs in this program (e.g., Oncology, Rheumatology, Multiple Sclerosis, HIV/AIDS). Used for clinical reporting, HEDIS measures, and population health analytics.
reference: WHO ATC Classification System; CMS Part D Formulary Guidance; NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications

description: Contracted discount percentage applied to the Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for specialty drugs in this program. Used as an alternative pricing basis to AWP in PBM contract negotiations.
reference: CMS Part D Pricing Guidance; ERISA Section 408(b)(2); MAC/AWP/WAC Industry Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the dispensing pharmacy record within the PBM network master. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to compliance.baa. Business justification: BAA management: each dispensing pharmacy must have a Business Associate Agreement for PHI handling; compliance tracks BAAs per pharmacy.

Internal pharmacy identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) for claim routing and adjudication. May differ from NCPDP ID in multi-PBM environments. Used for system-level reconciliation.
reference: RxClaim/Argus PBM System; NCPDP Claim Adjudication Standard

description: Identifier of the MAC pricing list applied to this pharmacy for generic drug reimbursement. MAC lists define the maximum reimbursable ingredient cost for multi-source generic drugs. References the applicable MAC list version in the PBM system.
reference: CMS Part D MAC Transparency Requirements; State MAC Transparency Laws; NCPDP Claim Adjudication

business_glossary_term: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs (NCPDP) Provider ID
description: The 7-digit NCPDP pharmacy identifier (formerly NABP number) used as the primary pharmacy identifier in PBM claim transactions, formulary lookups, and pharmacy network directories. Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; CMS Part D; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 162

description: Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Network participation reports need each pharmacy's verified credential record; FK provides authoritative license/DEA data for compliance audits.

Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Required for network participation reporting and CMS network adequacy; each pharmacy must be linked to the provider network it belongs to.

Indicates whether the pharmacy is enabled to receive electronic prescriptions (e-prescribing) via NCPDP SCRIPT standard. Required for controlled substance e-prescribing (EPCS) compliance in many states.
reference: NCPDP SCRIPT Standard; DEA EPCS Regulations 21 CFR Part 1311; State E-Prescribing Mandates

description: Primary street address of the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for member pharmacy locator, network directory, and regulatory reporting.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 162

description: Secondary address information (suite, unit, floor) for the pharmacy dispensing location.

The contracted discount percentage applied to the AWP for brand drug ingredient cost reimbursement. For example, AWP minus 15% means awp_discount_percent = 15.00. Applies when ingredient_cost_basis is 'awp_discount'. Commercially sensitive contract term.
reference: CMS Part D Reimbursement Standards; AWP Industry Benchmark; PBM Contract Standards

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy is part of a chain organization (e.g., CVS, Walgreens, Rite Aid) or operates as an independent pharmacy. Affects contract terms, reimbursement rates, and network tier assignment.
reference: NCPDP Pharmacy Chain/Independent Classification; PBM Contract Standards

description: Name of the parent chain organization if the pharmacy is a chain location (e.g., CVS Health, Walgreens Boots Alliance). Null for independent pharmacies. Used for chain-level contract management and performance reporting.
reference: NCPDP Chain Code Standard; PBM Contract Management

description: City of the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for geographic network analysis, member access reporting, and CMS Part D network adequacy compliance.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; CMS Part D Network Adequacy Standards

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy holds cold chain certification for temperature-sensitive drug storage and shipping (e.g., biologics, insulin, vaccines). Required for specialty and mail-order pharmacies dispensing refrigerated or frozen medications.
reference: USP Chapter 1118; URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation; CMS Part D Specialty Drug Standards

description: Date on which the pharmacy's network participation contract becomes effective. Used to validate claim eligibility by service date and for contract lifecycle management.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Contract Requirements; PBM Contract Standards

description: Date on which the pharmacy's network participation contract ends or was terminated. Null for active open-ended contracts. Used for claim eligibility validation and network adequacy gap analysis.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Contract Requirements; PBM Contract Standards

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy reimbursement contract is negotiated directly with the health plan (direct) or administered through the PBM intermediary (PBM-administered). Affects financial settlement, audit rights, and MAC list applicability.
reference: PBM Contract Standards; ERISA; CMS Part D Pharmacy Contract Requirements

description: Timestamp when the dispensing pharmacy record was first created in the PBM network master. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record retention requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; CMS Part D Record Retention Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The contracted per-prescription dispensing fee paid to the pharmacy in addition to ingredient cost reimbursement. Expressed in USD. May vary by pharmacy type, drug type, or days supply. Commercially sensitive contract term.
reference: CMS Part D Dispensing Fee Standards; PBM Contract Standards; NCPDP Claim Adjudication

description: Two-letter USPS state code of the state in which the pharmacy is licensed to dispense. May differ from the physical address state for mail-order pharmacies licensed in multiple states. Used for state-level regulatory compliance and claim adjudication.
reference: State Board of Pharmacy Regulations; NABP; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 162

description: Trade name or DBA name under which the pharmacy operates if different from the legal business name. Used in member-facing directories and communications.
Fax number for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for prior authorization transmissions, prescription transfers, and clinical communications.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: The pricing benchmark used to calculate ingredient cost reimbursement for drug claims. AWP (Average Wholesale Price) discount is most common for brand drugs; MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost) applies to generics; WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost) and NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost) are alternative benchmarks. Sourced from PBM contract terms in RxClaim/Argus.
reference: CMS Part D Reimbursement Standards; NCPDP Claim Adjudication; MAC/AWP/WAC Industry Standards

description: The health insurance line of business for which this pharmacy network participation record applies. A pharmacy may have separate network records per LOB with different contract terms. Drives claim adjudication routing and formulary selection.
reference: CMS Part D; Medicaid Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; ACA Exchange Requirements

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy is certified and operationally capable of fulfilling mail-order prescriptions (90-day supply, home delivery). Drives claim routing and member benefit design enforcement.
reference: CMS Part D Mail Order Pharmacy Requirements; URAC Mail Service Pharmacy Accreditation

business_glossary_term: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs (NCPDP) Chain Code
description: NCPDP-assigned 4-digit chain code identifying the pharmacy chain organization. Used for chain-level contract application and bulk reimbursement rate assignment in the PBM system.

Date on which the pharmacy's current network participation status became effective. Distinct from contract_effective_date; captures the most recent network status change date (e.g., reactivation after suspension).
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Network Requirements; PBM Contract Standards

description: Current status of the pharmacy's participation in the PBM network. Drives real-time claim adjudication eligibility checks in RxClaim/Argus. Active pharmacies are eligible to dispense covered drugs; inactive/terminated pharmacies are rejected at point-of-sale.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Network Requirements; NCQA Pharmacy Accreditation; PBM Contract Standards

description: Date on which the pharmacy's network participation was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null for currently active network participants. Used for claim eligibility validation and network adequacy gap analysis.
reference: CMS Part D Pharmacy Network Requirements; PBM Contract Standards

description: Pharmacy network tier designation that determines member cost-sharing levels and reimbursement rates. Preferred pharmacies offer lower member cost-sharing; specialty tier applies to specialty drug dispensing locations.
reference: CMS Part D Preferred Pharmacy Network Rules; ACA Section 2707; PBM Contract Standards

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy contract includes audit provisions allowing the PBM or health plan to conduct on-site or desk audits of dispensing records, pricing, and compliance. Required for CMS Part D sponsor oversight obligations.
reference: CMS Part D Audit Requirements 42 CFR Part 423; PBM Contract Standards; ERISA

description: Legal business name of the dispensing pharmacy as registered with the state board of pharmacy and NCPDP. Used in member-facing communications, EOB documents, and provider directory.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; State Board of Pharmacy

description: Classification of the pharmacy by dispensing channel and operational model. Drives claim routing, reimbursement logic, and formulary applicability within the PBM system (RxClaim/Argus). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|mail_order|specialty|long_term_care|compounding — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: NCPDP Pharmacy Type Codes; CMS Part D Pharmacy Type Classification; PBM Industry Standard

description: Primary telephone number for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for member contact, prior authorization coordination, and provider directory.

Indicates whether the pharmacy requires patient or authorized representative signature upon delivery for mail-order or specialty drug shipments. Relevant for controlled substances and high-value specialty medications.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; CMS Part D Delivery Requirements; NCPDP Claim Adjudication

description: Identifies the operational source system from which this pharmacy record was ingested (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM, Cactus credentialing system). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Accreditation body certification held by the pharmacy for specialty drug dispensing. URAC, ACHC, and NABP PCAB are the primary specialty pharmacy accreditation bodies recognized by health plans and PBMs. Required for specialty network participation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: urac|achc|jcaho|nabp_pcab|none — promote to reference product if multi-accreditation tracking is needed]
reference: URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation Standards; ACHC Specialty Pharmacy Standards; NABP PCAB; CMS Part D Specialty Pharmacy Requirements

description: Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for state-level licensure validation, network adequacy reporting, and regulatory compliance.
reference: USPS State Abbreviation Standard; NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; State DOI Regulations

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. Used for network adequacy reporting and member access analysis, particularly for CMS Part D access requirements.
reference: CMS Part D Network Adequacy Standards; State DOI Network Adequacy Regulations

description: Timestamp when the dispensing pharmacy record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture, data quality monitoring, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; CMS Part D Record Retention Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the pharmacy dispensing location. Used for geographic network adequacy analysis, member proximity calculations, and CMS Part D access reporting.
reference: USPS ZIP Code Standard; CMS Part D Network Adequacy; NCPDP Provider Identification Standard

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the PBM contract record in the pharmacy domain silver layer. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.
Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory obligations tied to PBM contracts; compliance team records which obligations each contract must satisfy.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract management assigns a manager employee; needed for performance monitoring and audit of PBM contracts.
Reference to the drug formulary associated with this PBM contract. The formulary defines covered drugs, tier assignments, and utilization management rules governed by this contract.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Requirements; 42 CFR Part 423; NCQA

description: Reference identifier for the PBM vendor organization in the health plan's vendor or trading partner master. Supports linking to vendor management records for credentialing and payment.

Date of the most recent amendment or addendum executed against this PBM contract. Null if no amendments have been made since original execution. Used to track contract evolution and ensure current terms are applied in adjudication.
reference: ERISA; NAIC; Internal Contract Management Policy

description: Contractually specified frequency at which the health plan may exercise audit rights over the PBM. Relevant only when audit_rights_flag is True. Drives compliance calendar and vendor oversight scheduling.
reference: ERISA; CMS Part D; NAIC; State DOI Audit Requirements

description: Indicates whether the health plan has contractual audit rights to inspect PBM financial records, rebate calculations, MAC list pricing, and claims adjudication data. True = audit rights granted; False = not included. Critical for compliance and financial oversight.
reference: ERISA; HIPAA; CMS Part D; NAIC; State DOI Audit Requirements

description: Indicates whether the PBM contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless notice of termination is provided. True = auto-renews; False = requires affirmative renewal action.

business_glossary_term: Average Wholesale Price (AWP) Discount Mail Order Percentage
description: Contracted discount percentage off AWP (Average Wholesale Price) applied to drug ingredient cost reimbursement at mail-order pharmacies. Typically deeper than retail AWP discounts due to mail-order volume.
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; AWP Industry Pricing Standards

business_glossary_term: Average Wholesale Price (AWP) Discount Retail Percentage
description: Contracted discount percentage off AWP (Average Wholesale Price) applied to brand and generic drug ingredient cost reimbursement at retail pharmacies. A core financial term in PBM contracts (e.g., AWP minus 18% for generics).
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; AWP Industry Pricing Standards

description: CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services) contract number associated with the Medicare Part D plan under which this PBM contract operates (e.g., H1234 for MA-PD, S1234 for PDP). Required for CMS Part D reporting and RAPS/EDPS submissions. Null for non-Medicare LOBs.
reference: CMS Part D; 42 CFR Part 423; CMS HPMS

description: Descriptive name of the PBM contract (e.g., 'Express Scripts ASO Agreement FY2024'). Used for display in reporting dashboards and contract management workflows.

Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM vendor at execution. Used for correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Maps to the contract identifier in RxClaim or Argus PBM system.
reference: ERISA; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Name of the health plan employee or department responsible for managing this PBM contract relationship. Used for escalation routing, audit coordination, and vendor governance.

Current lifecycle state of the PBM contract. Draft indicates the contract is under negotiation; active indicates it is in force; suspended indicates temporarily halted; terminated indicates early cancellation; expired indicates natural end-of-term; pending_renewal indicates renewal is in progress.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation; Internal Contract Lifecycle Policy

description: Classification of the PBM contract arrangement. ASO (Administrative Services Only) means the health plan retains financial risk; full_risk means the PBM assumes drug spend risk; carve_out means pharmacy benefits are separated from medical; pass_through means drug costs are passed at actual cost; hybrid combines elements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASO|full_risk|carve_out|pass_through|hybrid — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: ERISA; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; NAIC

description: Version identifier for the PBM contract document, incremented with each amendment or renewal (e.g., '1.0', '2.1', 'Amendment_3'). Supports contract lifecycle management and audit trail for regulatory examinations.
reference: ERISA; NAIC; Internal Contract Management Policy

description: Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid per prescription claim fulfilled through mail-order pharmacy channels under this PBM contract. Typically lower than retail due to volume efficiencies.
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; Internal PBM Contract Terms

description: Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid to retail network pharmacies per prescription claim under this PBM contract. Part of the pharmacy reimbursement formula alongside ingredient cost (AWP/WAC/MAC).
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; Internal PBM Contract Terms

description: Contracted dispensing fee in USD paid per specialty drug prescription claim under this PBM contract. Specialty pharmacy dispensing fees are negotiated separately due to the high-cost, high-touch nature of specialty drugs.
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; Internal PBM Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) — including prospective, concurrent, and retrospective DUR — is included as a service under this PBM contract. True = DUR services included; False = not included.
reference: OBRA 1990; CMS Part D; NCQA; URAC

description: Date on which the PBM contract becomes legally binding and operationally active. Governs when PBM services, pricing schedules, and rebate guarantees take effect. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: ERISA; CMS Part D; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: Contractually guaranteed minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) as a percentage of total prescription claims. If the PBM fails to achieve this rate, financial penalties or credits apply. Key cost-management metric in PBM contracts.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA HEDIS; Internal PBM Performance Standards

description: Two-letter US state code of the state whose laws govern this PBM contract for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance purposes. Relevant for state DOI oversight, MAC transparency laws, and PBM regulation.
reference: NAIC; State DOI Regulations; State PBM Transparency Laws

description: The drug pricing benchmark used as the basis for ingredient cost reimbursement under this contract. AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or AAC (Actual Acquisition Cost). Determines the reimbursement formula for drug ingredient costs.
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC; AWP/WAC Industry Standards; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Line of Business (LOB) covered under this PBM contract. Determines which member populations and benefit programs are governed by this agreement. A single contract may cover one or multiple LOBs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_part_d|medicaid|exchange|all — promote to reference product for multi-LOB support]
reference: CMS Part D; ACA; NAIC; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Identifier or name of the MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost) list applied under this PBM contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. MAC lists cap reimbursement for generics below AWP. References the specific MAC list version maintained by the PBM.
reference: CMS Part D; NAIC MAC Transparency Laws; State DOI Regulations

description: Indicates whether mail-order pharmacy dispensing services are included in the scope of this PBM contract. True = mail order included; False = retail-only or carved out.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; Internal PBM Contract Terms

business_glossary_term: Mail Order Penetration Rate Guarantee Percentage
description: Contractually guaranteed minimum mail-order pharmacy utilization rate as a percentage of eligible maintenance drug claims. Drives cost savings through mail-order channel incentives.
reference: CMS Part D; Internal PBM Performance Standards

description: Indicates whether the PBM contract includes financial performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, mail-order penetration, member satisfaction scores). True = performance guarantees included; False = not included.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; URAC; Internal PBM Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether prior authorization (PA) requirements for specialty and non-formulary drugs are administered by the PBM under this contract. True = PBM manages PA; False = health plan manages PA internally.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; URAC; 42 CFR Part 423

description: Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) utilization management rules are in scope under this PBM contract. Quantity limits restrict the amount of a drug dispensed per fill or per time period. True = quantity limits apply; False = not applicable.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; URAC; Internal UM Policy

business_glossary_term: Rebate Guarantee Per Member Per Month (PMPM)
description: Contractually guaranteed minimum rebate amount expressed as Per Member Per Month (PMPM) in USD that the PBM must remit to the health plan. A key financial performance guarantee in PBM contracts. Used for actuarial reserving and financial reconciliation.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Benefit Manual; ERISA; GAAP; SAP

description: Percentage of manufacturer drug rebates that the PBM is contractually obligated to pass through to the health plan (e.g., 100% pass-through vs. spread-based retention). Critical for MLR (Medical Loss Ratio) calculations and CMS Part D compliance.
reference: CMS Part D; ACA MLR Regulations 45 CFR Part 158; ERISA

description: Number of calendar days after the close of a settlement period within which the PBM must provide rebate invoices and supporting data to the health plan. Used for financial close scheduling and IBNR reserve calculations.
reference: CMS Part D; GAAP; SAP; Internal Finance Policy

description: Contractually specified frequency at which the PBM remits drug manufacturer rebates to the health plan. Drives cash flow forecasting, accounts receivable management, and actuarial IBNR (Incurred But Not Reported) rebate estimates.
reference: CMS Part D; GAAP; SAP; Internal Finance Policy

description: Timestamp when this PBM contract record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA record retention requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; NAIC; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Timestamp when this PBM contract record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection, SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) processing, and audit trail requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; NAIC; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Number of calendar days prior to contract termination_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent not to renew. Operationally critical for contract management and vendor transitions.

Identifies the operational source system from which this PBM contract record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer (e.g., RxClaim, Argus, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: Indicates whether specialty pharmacy benefit management (high-cost biologics, specialty injectables) is within the scope of this PBM contract. True = specialty pharmacy included; False = carved out or not applicable.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; URAC; Internal Specialty Pharmacy Policy

description: Indicates whether step therapy protocols are included in the scope of this PBM contract. Step therapy requires members to try lower-cost drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized. True = step therapy applies; False = not applicable.
reference: CMS Part D; NCQA; State Step Therapy Laws; URAC

description: Date on which the PBM contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Distinguishes between scheduled expiration and early termination when combined with contract_status. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: ERISA; CMS Part D; NAIC Model Regulation

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacy contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the dispensing pharmacy or pharmacy chain entity in the pharmacy master. Links this contract to the specific pharmacy party.
reference: NCPDP Provider Identification Standard; CMS NPI Registry

description: Identifier of the MAC pricing list applied under this contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. References the specific MAC schedule version used by the PBM or health plan. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is mac.
reference: CMS Part D MAC Pricing; NAIC Model MAC Transparency Act; State DOI MAC Regulations

business_glossary_term: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs (NCPDP) Provider ID
description: 7-digit NCPDP pharmacy identifier used in pharmacy claim transactions (NCPDP Telecom D.0) and PBM adjudication. Complements the NPI for pharmacy-specific identification.
reference: NCPDP Telecom Standard Version D.0; CMS Part D Prescription Drug Event (PDE) Reporting

description: Reference to the PBM contract under which this pharmacy dispensing agreement is administered, when the contract is PBM-administered rather than direct. Null for direct health-plan contracts.
reference: RxClaim / Argus PBM System — Contract Administration Module

description: Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per network; linking contracts to provider_network enables contract‑level reporting, tiering, and compliance tracking.

Type of accreditation required or held by the pharmacy under this contract. URAC and ACHC are common for specialty pharmacies. PCAB (Pharmacy Compounding Accreditation Board) for compounding pharmacies. Relevant for specialty and mail-order contracts.
reference: URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation; ACHC Pharmacy Accreditation; CMS Part D Specialty Drug Requirements

description: Contractually stipulated frequency at which the health plan or PBM may conduct pharmacy audits. Applicable when audit_rights_indicator is true.
reference: CMS Part D Audit Requirements 42 CFR 423.505; NAIC Market Conduct Examination Standards

description: Indicates whether the health plan or PBM retains the right to conduct on-site or desk audits of the pharmacy's dispensing records under this contract. Required for CMS Part D compliance.
reference: CMS Part D Audit Requirements 42 CFR 423.505; HIPAA Audit Provisions

description: Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the required notice period.
reference: NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act; State DOI Contract Renewal Requirements

description: Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug's Average Wholesale Price (AWP) to determine ingredient cost reimbursement for brand and generic drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage (e.g., 16.50 = AWP minus 16.50%). Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount.
reference: CMS Part D Reimbursement Standards; First DataBank / Medi-Span AWP Reference

description: Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM. Used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Part D Contract Requirements

description: Current lifecycle state of the pharmacy contract. Active contracts are eligible for claim adjudication. Suspended contracts are temporarily ineligible. Terminated/expired contracts are closed. Drives network eligibility checks in RxClaim/Argus.
reference: CMS Part D Network Adequacy; NCQA Pharmacy Credentialing Standards

description: Classification of the contractual relationship: direct (health plan contracts directly with pharmacy), pbm_administered (PBM manages on plan's behalf), chain (multi-location chain agreement), independent (single-location independent pharmacy), mail_order, or specialty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|pbm_administered|chain|independent|mail_order|specialty — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Part D Network Adequacy; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Management Transparency Act

description: Indicates whether the pharmacy must complete and maintain credentialing requirements (state licensure, DEA registration, accreditation) as a condition of this contract.
reference: NCQA Pharmacy Credentialing Standards; CMS Part D Network Adequacy; State Board of Pharmacy Licensure

description: Maximum days supply per mail-order prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 90 days). Drives adjudication edits in RxClaim/Argus.
reference: CMS Part D Days Supply Rules; NCPDP Telecom Standard

description: Maximum days supply per retail prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 30 or 34 days). Claims exceeding this limit may be rejected during adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Days Supply Rules; NCPDP Telecom Standard

description: The dispensing channel(s) authorized under this contract. Retail covers walk-in pharmacies; mail_order covers 90-day supply dispensing; specialty covers high-cost/complex drugs; long_term_care covers SNF/LTC facilities; home_infusion covers IV therapy.
reference: CMS Part D Dispensing Channel Definitions; URAC Specialty Pharmacy Standards

description: Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per mail-order prescription dispensed. Typically lower than retail due to higher volume and automation. Applicable for mail-order and specialty pharmacy contracts.
reference: CMS Part D Dispensing Fee Standards; NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act

description: Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid to the pharmacy per retail prescription dispensed, in addition to ingredient cost reimbursement. A core financial term of the pharmacy contract.
reference: CMS Part D Dispensing Fee Standards; NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act

description: Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per specialty drug prescription dispensed. Specialty drugs require enhanced clinical services, justifying a separate fee schedule.
reference: CMS Part D Specialty Drug Dispensing; URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation Standards

description: Date on which the pharmacy contract becomes binding and the pharmacy is eligible to dispense claims under this agreement. Used for network eligibility validation during adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Contract Requirements; NAIC Model Law on Pharmacy Contracting

description: Date on which the pharmacy contract was fully executed (signed by all parties). May precede the effective_date if there is a future start date.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Part D Contract Execution Requirements

business_glossary_term: Generic Average Wholesale Price (AWP) Discount Percentage
description: Contracted AWP discount percentage specifically for generic drugs, which typically carries a deeper discount than brand drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount and a separate generic rate is negotiated.
reference: CMS Part D Reimbursement Standards; First DataBank / Medi-Span AWP Reference

description: Contracted minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) the pharmacy must achieve as a performance requirement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Supports CMS Part D Star Ratings and cost management objectives.
reference: CMS Part D Star Ratings — Generic Dispensing Rate Measure; NCQA HEDIS Pharmacy Measures

description: The pricing methodology used to calculate ingredient cost reimbursement to the pharmacy. AWP (Average Wholesale Price) discount, MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost) discount, NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary (U&C). Core reimbursement term in the contract.
reference: CMS Part D Prescription Drug Pricing; NAIC Model Pharmacy Benefit Management Transparency Act; AWP/WAC/MAC industry standards

description: Date of the most recent amendment or addendum to this pharmacy contract. Used to track contract evolution and ensure the current fee schedule and terms are applied during adjudication.
reference: CMS Part D Contract Amendment Requirements; NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act

description: The line of business to which this pharmacy contract applies. Determines applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., CMS Part D rules for Medicare, state Medicaid rules). A contract may be scoped to one or multiple LOBs.
reference: CMS Part D Contract Requirements; CMS Medicaid Managed Care Rules; ACA Exchange Requirements

description: Indicates whether this pharmacy is authorized to dispense mail-order (90-day supply) prescriptions under this contract.
reference: CMS Part D Mail Order Pharmacy Requirements; NCPDP Mail Service Pharmacy Standards

description: Network participation tier assigned to the pharmacy under this contract. Preferred pharmacies typically offer lower member cost-sharing. Drives member cost-sharing calculations and formulary benefit design.
reference: CMS Part D Preferred Pharmacy Network Rules; ACA Section 2707 Network Adequacy

description: Indicates whether this pharmacy contract is applicable to Medicare Part D prescription drug benefit administration. When true, CMS Part D network adequacy, pricing, and reporting requirements apply.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D 42 CFR Part 423; CMS Part D Network Adequacy Final Rule

description: Indicates whether this contract includes performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, medication adherence, fill accuracy). When true, performance_guarantee_details apply.
reference: NCQA Pharmacy Quality Standards; CMS Part D Star Ratings Pharmacy Measures

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned to the dispensing pharmacy by CMS. Used for claims adjudication, EDI transactions (NCPDP 5.1/D.0), and network directory publication.
reference: CMS NPI Final Rule 45 CFR Part 162; HIPAA Administrative Simplification

description: Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312; Internal Data Governance Audit Trail Standard

description: Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was most recently updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR 164.312; Internal Data Governance Audit Trail Standard

description: Indicates whether the contract includes provisions allowing the health plan or PBM to recoup overpayments identified through audits or claims reviews.
reference: CMS Part D Overpayment Recovery Rules 42 CFR 423.505(b)(20); NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act

description: Native contract identifier from the originating PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) or provider management system (Cactus/ProviderSource). Used for lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.
reference: RxClaim / Argus PBM System; Cactus / ProviderSource Provider Management System

description: Indicates whether this pharmacy is contracted and credentialed to dispense specialty drugs under this agreement. Specialty pharmacies must meet enhanced accreditation requirements (URAC, ACHC).
reference: URAC Specialty Pharmacy Accreditation; CMS Part D Specialty Drug Requirements

description: Two-letter US state code identifying the primary state jurisdiction governing this pharmacy contract. Determines applicable state DOI regulations, MAC transparency laws, and pharmacy contracting statutes.
reference: NAIC State Insurance Regulation; State DOI Pharmacy Contracting Laws; USPS State Abbreviation Standard

description: Date on which the pharmacy contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Claims with service dates after this date are not eligible for reimbursement under this contract.
reference: CMS Part D Contract Requirements; NAIC Model Law on Pharmacy Contracting

description: Number of calendar days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract without cause. Regulatory minimums vary by state DOI requirements.
reference: State DOI Pharmacy Contracting Regulations; NAIC Model Pharmacy Contracting Act

description: Version identifier of the pharmacy contract document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, Amendment 3). Tracks contract amendments and renegotiations over time for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: Internal Contract Management Standards; CMS Part D Contract Amendment Requirements

description: Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug's Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for ingredient cost reimbursement. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is wac_discount.
reference: CMS Part D Reimbursement Standards; WAC as defined under 42 USC 1395w-3a

description: Primary key for affiliation

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Affiliation coordination is handled by a specific employee who manages contracts, privileges, and network status for each affiliation.

Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: affiliation should reference the medical group (group_practice) it belongs to; the existing column name does not match target PK naming, so a new FK column is added and the generic column removed.
Reference to the individual provider (physician, nurse practitioner, or other healthcare professional) who holds this affiliation. Links to the provider master entity.
Boolean indicator denoting whether the provider is accepting new patients through this affiliation. True indicates accepting new patients; False indicates not accepting new patients.

Boolean indicator denoting whether the provider has admitting privileges at the affiliated hospital or facility. True indicates admitting privileges are granted; False indicates no admitting privileges.
Current lifecycle status of the provider affiliation. Indicates whether the affiliation is active and in effect, inactive, suspended, pending approval, or terminated.

Categorical classification of the affiliation relationship. Indicates whether the provider is affiliated with a medical group, Independent Practice Association (IPA), Accountable Care Organization (ACO), health system, hospital, or other facility type.
The National Provider Identifier (NPI) used for billing purposes in this affiliation context. May represent the organizational NPI (Type 2) of the affiliated entity.
The date and time when this affiliation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The date on which the provider's credentials and affiliation with the organization were last verified. Used to track compliance with credentialing re-verification requirements.

The name of the department, division, or service line within the affiliated organization where the provider practices. Examples include Cardiology, Emergency Medicine, Orthopedic Surgery, Primary Care.

Boolean indicator denoting whether this affiliation should be displayed in the member-facing provider directory. True indicates the affiliation is publicly visible; False indicates it is for internal use only.
The date on which the provider affiliation ended or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended affiliations that are currently active.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated this affiliation record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.
The date and time when this affiliation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

The category of medical staff membership the provider holds at the affiliated hospital or facility. Categories include Active Staff, Courtesy Staff, Consulting Staff, Honorary Staff, Affiliate Staff, and Provisional Staff.

Boolean indicator denoting whether this affiliation makes the provider eligible for participation in the health plan's provider network. True indicates network participation; False indicates non-participating affiliation.
The date by which the next credentialing verification for this affiliation is due. Supports proactive credentialing workflow management.

Free-text field for capturing additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the provider affiliation. May include details about practice restrictions, scheduling notes, or administrative remarks.
Boolean indicator denoting whether this is the provider's primary organizational affiliation. True indicates this is the main affiliation; False indicates a secondary or additional affiliation.
The provider's role or title within the affiliated organization. Examples include Staff Physician, Medical Director, Department Chair, Attending Physician, Consulting Physician.
The unique identifier of this affiliation record in the source system. Used for traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.
The name of the source system from which this affiliation record originated. Examples include Cactus, ProviderSource, credentialing system, or manual entry.
The date on which the provider affiliation became effective. Represents the beginning of the provider-organization relationship.

The Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Employer Identification Number (EIN) under which the provider bills for services rendered through this affiliation. Used for claims routing and payment processing.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this affiliation record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.
Primary key for dea_registration

Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: dea_registration must be linked to the core provider entity to avoid silo; provider_npi is redundant once provider_id is present.
True if the provider is authorized to prescribe controlled substances.
True if this row represents the current active registration for the provider.
Unique DEA registration number assigned to the provider for controlled substance prescribing.
Date the DEA registration expires unless renewed.
Date the DEA registration became effective.
Agency that issued the DEA registration (typically DEA).
Timestamp of the most recent update to the DEA registration record.
Free-text field for any additional comments or remarks about the registration.
Timestamp when the DEA registration record was first created in the system.
Date the record became inactive (e.g., when registration was revoked).

Current lifecycle status of the DEA registration.
Indicates whether the registration is for an individual provider or an organization.
DEA schedule(s) the provider is authorized to prescribe (II, III, IV, V).
Name of the source system that supplied the DEA registration data (e.g., ProviderSource, Cactus).
Unique identifier of the DEA registration record in the source system.
Date the DEA registration information was last verified against the primary source.
Unique identifier for the healthcare facility record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Facility operational costs are tracked against a cost center for capital budgeting and regulatory cost reporting.
Indicates whether the facility is currently accepting new patients. Used in provider directory and network adequacy reporting.

Organization that has accredited the facility. Values: joint_commission (The Joint Commission), dnv (DNV Healthcare), cihq (Center for Improvement in Healthcare Quality), aoa (American Osteopathic Association), aaahc (Accreditation Association for Ambulatory Health Care), achc (Accreditation Commission for Health Care), none (not accredited). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: joint_commission|dnv|cihq|aoa|aaahc|achc|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date when the current accreditation expires. Null if facility is not accredited.
Primary street address of the facility physical location.
Secondary address information such as suite, floor, or building number.
Total number of licensed beds at the facility. Used for network adequacy calculations and capacity planning.
Six-character alphanumeric identifier assigned by CMS to Medicare/Medicaid certified facilities. Also known as Provider Number.
City where the facility is located.
Ten-character identifier issued by CMS for facilities performing laboratory testing. Format: 2-digit state code + 1 letter + 7 digits.
County where the facility is located. Used for network adequacy geographic analysis.
Date when the current credentialing approval became effective.
Date when the current credentialing approval expires and recredentialing is required.
Current status of the facility credentialing process. Indicates whether the facility has passed credentialing review and is approved for network participation.
Indicates whether the facility is designated as a Critical Access Hospital under the Medicare Rural Hospital Flexibility Program.

description: Date when the facility record or current participation status became effective.
Primary email address for facility communications and electronic correspondence.
Indicates whether the facility provides 24/7 emergency department services.
Legal name of the healthcare facility as registered with state and federal authorities.

Classification of the healthcare facility. Values: hospital (acute care hospital), snf (Skilled Nursing Facility), asc (Ambulatory Surgical Center), fqhc (Federally Qualified Health Center), rhc (Rural Health Clinic), behavioral_health (behavioral health facility). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hospital|snf|asc|fqhc|rhc|behavioral_health|home_health|hospice|dialysis_center|dme_supplier|ltac|irf|imaging_center — promote to reference product]
Fax number for the facility, used for prior authorization and medical records transmission.
Indicates whether the facility is certified to participate in the Medicaid program.
Indicates whether the facility is certified to participate in the Medicare program.
Tiering classification of the facility within the provider network. Tier 1 is preferred/lowest cost-sharing. Out_of_network indicates non-participating facility.
Type 2 NPI assigned to the facility by CMS. Ten-digit unique organizational identifier required for HIPAA transactions.
Legal ownership structure of the facility. Values: for_profit (investor-owned), non_profit (tax-exempt), government (federal/state/local), tribal (Indian Health Service or tribal).
Name of the parent health system or organization that owns or operates the facility. Null if facility is independent.
Current status of the facility in the provider network. Indicates whether the facility is actively participating, suspended, or terminated.
Primary contact phone number for the facility.
Five or nine-digit ZIP code of the facility location.
Overall quality rating score for the facility on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Derived from CMS Star Ratings, HEDIS measures, or internal quality assessments.
Timestamp when this facility record was first created in the data platform.
Timestamp when this facility record was last updated in the data platform.
Name of the source system from which this facility record originated (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource).
Unique identifier for this facility in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Two-letter state abbreviation where the facility is located.
Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) assigned by IRS. Used for claims payment and 1099 reporting.
Indicates whether the facility is a teaching hospital affiliated with a medical school and residency programs.
Indicates whether the facility offers telehealth or virtual care services.
Date when the facility participation was terminated or is scheduled to terminate. Null if currently active.
Trauma center designation level assigned by state or American College of Surgeons. Level 1 is highest capability. Not_designated indicates facility is not a trauma center.

description: Public website URL for the facility. Used in provider directory.
Primary key for group_practice
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Group practice financial statements require linking each practice group to its cost center for consolidated reporting.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Group Practice Management requires a designated practice manager employee overseeing operations, staffing, and compliance for the group.
The state-assigned Medicaid provider identifier for the group practice, used for Medicaid claims submission.
The Medicare-assigned provider identifier for the group practice, used for Medicare claims submission and enrollment.
Indicates whether the group practice is currently accepting new patients. Used for provider directory accuracy and member access.
Description of accessibility accommodations available at the group practice (e.g., wheelchair access, hearing loop, accessible exam tables) to comply with ADA requirements.
The name of the ACO in which the group practice participates, if applicable.
Indicates whether the group practice participates in an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) for value-based care arrangements.
The primary street address line 1 of the group practice's main location or headquarters.
The primary street address line 2 (suite, floor, building) of the group practice's main location.
The city where the group practice's primary location is situated.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Certification Number (CCN)
description: The CMS Certification Number (CCN) assigned to FQHC, RHC, or other certified facilities. Required for Medicare/Medicaid billing.
The county in which the group practice's primary location is located. Used for network adequacy and geographic access reporting.
The timestamp when this group practice record was first created in the provider management system.
The date on which the group practice was initially credentialed and approved for network participation.
The current credentialing status of the group practice within the health plan's credentialing process.
The name of the source system from which this group practice record originated (e.g., Cactus, ProviderSource, NPPES).
The trade name or DBA name under which the group practice operates, if different from the legal name.
The date on which the group practice's participation in the health plan network became effective.
The primary email address for administrative and contracting communications with the group practice.
The fax number for the group practice, used for prior authorization and medical records transmission.
The legal business name of the medical group, physician organization, or practice entity as registered with state authorities and CMS.
The 10-digit Type 2 NPI assigned to the group practice organization by CMS. This is the organizational NPI distinct from individual provider NPIs.

description: The total number of individual providers (physicians, nurse practitioners, physician assistants) affiliated with and practicing under this group.

Classification of the group practice organizational structure: solo practice (single physician), single specialty group, multi-specialty group, Independent Practice Association (IPA), Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC), or Rural Health Clinic (RHC).
reference: CMS Provider Taxonomy, HRSA FQHC/RHC Designation

description: The name of the primary hospital or health system with which the group practice is affiliated or has admitting privileges.
Comma-separated list of languages in which the group practice offers clinical services, used for provider directory cultural competency and member access.
reference: CMS Provider Directory Standards, ACA Section 1557

description: The current participation status of the group practice in the health plan's provider network. Active indicates the group is contracted and accepting members.
The NCQA PCMH recognition level achieved by the group practice (Level 1, 2, or 3), with Level 3 being the highest.
Indicates whether the group practice has achieved NCQA Patient-Centered Medical Home (PCMH) recognition, qualifying for enhanced reimbursement and quality incentives.
The primary contact phone number for the group practice.
The primary medical specialty or service focus of the group practice (e.g., Family Medicine, Cardiology, Orthopedic Surgery, Multi-Specialty).
The date by which the group practice must complete recredentialing to maintain network participation. NCQA requires recredentialing at least every 36 months.
The two-letter state abbreviation for the group practice's primary location.

business_glossary_term: Tax Identification Number (TIN) / Employer Identification Number (EIN)
description: The federal tax identification number (TIN/EIN) assigned to the group practice by the IRS. Used for claims payment and 1099 reporting.
The 10-character alphanumeric taxonomy code identifying the group's primary classification, specialty, and area of practice per the NUCC taxonomy.
reference: National Uniform Claim Committee (NUCC) Health Care Provider Taxonomy

description: Indicates whether the group practice offers telehealth or telemedicine services to members.
The date on which the group practice's participation in the health plan network was terminated or is scheduled to terminate. Null for active participants.
The reason for termination of the group practice's network participation (e.g., voluntary withdrawal, quality issues, credentialing failure, contract non-renewal).
The timestamp when this group practice record was last modified in the provider management system.
The public website URL for the group practice, used in provider directory listings.
The 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP code for the group practice's primary location.

Reference to the healthcare provider who holds this license. Links to the provider master entity.
Reference to the credentialing or recredentialing cycle during which this license was verified. Links to credentialing workflow tracking.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: License verification is performed by a credentialing staff employee; linking enables audit trails and compliance reporting.
The date the provider attested to the accuracy and currency of this license information. Used for credentialing and recredentialing cycles.

For DEA and CDS registrations, the controlled substance schedules the provider is authorized to prescribe (Schedule II, III, IV, V). Stored as comma-separated list. Critical for pharmacy benefit management and prior authorization workflows.

description: Comma-separated list of state abbreviations where the provider has practice privileges under the interstate compact. Used for multi-state network adequacy and provider directory management.

description: The date disciplinary action was taken against the license. Used for credentialing review timelines and risk assessment.

description: Detailed description of any disciplinary actions, sanctions, or restrictions imposed by the licensing authority. Includes nature of violation, dates, and resolution. Populated only when disciplinary_action_flag is true.

description: Indicates whether any disciplinary action has been taken against this license by the issuing authority. True if disciplinary action exists; false otherwise. Critical for credentialing risk assessment.

description: The date the license becomes valid and the provider is authorized to practice under this credential. May differ from issue date for renewals or reinstatements.

description: The date the license expires and is no longer valid unless renewed. Critical for credentialing compliance and provider directory accuracy. Null for licenses with no expiration.

description: The date the license or registration was originally issued by the licensing authority. Used to calculate license tenure and verify historical credentialing timelines.

description: The regulatory body or government agency that issued the license or registration. Examples include State Medical Board, State Board of Nursing, Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), State Pharmacy Board, or Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS).

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Reporting Flag
description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this screening event must be included in CMS regulatory reporting submissions or program integrity audits.

The compliance status of this screening event relative to regulatory requirements: compliant (on schedule), overdue (past due date), pending review (awaiting resolution), non-compliant (violation), or waived (exception granted).
The version or publication date of the exclusion list data source used for this screening, ensuring traceability to the specific list snapshot consulted.
The date on which the exclusion became effective, as reported by the exclusion list source. Null if no match found.
The date on which the exclusion is scheduled to end or was lifted, if applicable. Null for indefinite exclusions or no match.

The scope of the exclusion found: Federal (Medicare/Medicaid), State (state-specific Medicaid), Both (federal and state), or None (no exclusion).
Detailed narrative or structured information about the match, including matched data elements, exclusion list entry identifiers, and any discrepancies requiring review.

value_regex: Exact NPI Match|Name and DOB Match|Name and Address Match|Partial Match|No Match
description: The type of match logic that triggered a positive or potential screening result, indicating which data elements matched the exclusion list entry.

The timestamp when this exclusion screening record was last modified or updated.

value_regex: No Action Required|Provider Terminated|Contract Suspended|Manual Review Pending|False Positive Confirmed|Waiver Granted
description: The action taken by the health plan in response to the screening result: no action (clear result), provider termination, contract suspension, pending manual review, false positive confirmation, or waiver granted per policy.
reference: CMS Program Integrity Requirements, Internal Compliance Policy

description: The date on which the resolution action was completed or the screening result was finalized. Null if resolution is still pending.

The full name of the individual or system process that performed the screening, for audit trail purposes.
The date on which the exclusion screening was performed.
