description: Calendar or program year in which this episode definition and target price are applicable. Aligns with CMS performance period designations and annual contract renewal cycles.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Performance Year Definitions; CMMI Program Calendar

description: Number of days following the anchor/trigger event included in the episode window for attribution of post-acute and follow-up services (e.g., 90 days post-discharge). Standard CMS BPCI-A windows are 30, 60, or 90 days.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Window Definition; CMMI Episode Grouper Specifications

description: Number of days prior to the anchor/trigger event included in the episode window for attribution of pre-episode services (e.g., pre-operative workup). Typically 0–30 days.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Window Definition; CMMI Episode Grouper Specifications

description: Identifies the payment program under which this episode is defined. Distinguishes CMS mandatory/voluntary programs from commercial and Medicaid bundled payment arrangements.
reference: CMS BPCI-A; CMS Comprehensive Joint Replacement (CJR) Model; CMMI Innovation Models

description: Indicates whether the provider must meet minimum quality performance thresholds before any gain sharing payments are released. True = quality gate is a prerequisite for gain sharing.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Quality Measure Requirements; NCQA HEDIS Quality Standards

description: Percentage of the target price withheld pending quality performance. Released to the provider upon meeting quality thresholds. Aligns financial incentives with quality outcomes (e.g., 0.0300 = 3% withhold).
reference: CMS BPCI-A Quality Measure Requirements; NCQA HEDIS Quality Standards

description: Multiplicative Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) applied to the baseline target price to normalize for patient acuity. Values above 1.0 indicate higher-risk patient populations; below 1.0 indicate lower-risk.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS; CMS EDPS

description: Indicates whether related inpatient readmissions occurring within the episode window are attributed to and included in the bundled payment. Unrelated readmissions are typically excluded.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Readmission Attribution Rules; CMS Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program

description: Frequency at which actual episode expenditures are reconciled against the target price and gain/loss settlements are calculated and paid.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Reconciliation Methodology; CMMI Payment Reconciliation Specifications

description: Specific reconciliation approach used to calculate net settlement amounts. Net Payment Reconciliation (NPR) adjusts individual claims; Episode Payment issues a single bundled payment; Virtual Reconciliation compares totals without adjusting individual claims.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Net Payment Reconciliation Amount (NPRA) Methodology; CMMI Episode Payment Models

description: Indicates whether the target price is adjusted for patient risk (e.g., HCC-based Risk Adjustment Factor) to account for case mix differences across providers and performance periods.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS); CMS HCC Model; CMS EDPS

description: Indicates whether Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) post-acute care services are included within the episode bundle. SNF inclusion significantly impacts target price and provider risk.
reference: CMS BPCI-A SNF Inclusion Rules; CMS SNF Prospective Payment System

description: Maximum dollar amount of savings the provider can earn per episode. Caps upside gain sharing to prevent windfall payments from outlier low-cost episodes.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Gain Sharing Cap Provisions; CMMI Alternative Payment Model Framework

description: Maximum dollar amount of losses the provider is required to absorb per episode before the health plan assumes full financial responsibility. Protects providers from catastrophic high-cost episodes.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Stop-Loss Provisions; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: The all-inclusive target price for the bundled payment episode in USD. Represents the maximum total reimbursement for all covered services within the episode window. Basis for reconciliation and gain/loss sharing calculations.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Target Price Methodology; CMMI Alternative Payment Model Framework

description: Methodology used to establish the target price. Historical claims-based uses the provider's own prior utilization; regional/national benchmarks use market comparators; negotiated is a contractually agreed fixed amount.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Target Price Methodology; CMMI Innovation Models

description: Date from which the current target price is applicable. Target prices are typically updated annually or per performance period.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Annual Target Price Update; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Date on which the current target price expires and must be renegotiated or updated. Null indicates the target price is open-ended until superseded.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Annual Target Price Update; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Date on which this bundled payment episode definition is terminated and no longer eligible to trigger new episodes. Null for currently active definitions.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS BPCI-A Participation Agreement

description: The specific procedure code (CPT/HCPCS), DRG number, or ICD code that triggers the episode window. For multi-code triggers, stores the primary anchor code; supplemental codes managed in a related reference table.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set; CMS DRG Grouper; WHO ICD-10-CM/ICD-10-PCS

description: Classification system for the trigger code. Indicates whether the trigger_code is a Current Procedural Terminology (CPT), Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS), Diagnosis Related Group (DRG), or ICD-10 code.
reference: AMA CPT; CMS HCPCS; CMS DRG; WHO ICD-10

description: Type of clinical event that initiates the bundled payment episode window. Determines whether the anchor event is identified by a CPT/HCPCS procedure code, DRG, ICD diagnosis code, or inpatient admission.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Trigger Specifications; AMA CPT Code Set; CMS DRG Grouper

description: Timestamp when this bundled payment episode definition record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312; NAIC Data Governance Standards

description: Unique identifier for the performance-linked financial arrangement within a provider contract. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links incentive arrangement approvals to the approving employee; required for incentive payout validation and reporting.
Reference to the parent provider contract under which this incentive arrangement is established.
Reference to the provider or provider group participating in this incentive arrangement.
Boolean flag indicating whether this incentive arrangement is part of an ACO agreement with shared savings and shared loss provisions.
Reference to the contract amendment that introduced or modified this incentive arrangement, if applicable.
Dollar amount of withheld funds forfeited by the provider due to failure to meet performance criteria. Expressed in USD.
Dollar amount of withheld funds released back to the provider upon meeting performance criteria. Expressed in USD.
Date when the incentive arrangement was formally approved by authorized parties within the health plan.
Descriptive name of the incentive arrangement for business identification and reporting purposes.
Business-assigned unique identifier for the incentive arrangement, used for external reference and reporting.
Current lifecycle status of the incentive arrangement indicating whether it is active, pending approval, suspended, terminated, or fully settled.
Classification of the incentive arrangement structure. Defines whether the arrangement is a bonus, withhold, shared savings distribution, performance guarantee, or penalty mechanism.
Indicates whether the provider has earned the incentive or withhold release based on performance evaluation results.
Date when the incentive arrangement becomes active and performance measurement begins.
Code identifying the line of business (Commercial, Medicare, Medicaid, Exchange) to which this incentive arrangement applies.
Maximum financial incentive or bonus amount that can be earned by the provider under this arrangement. Expressed in USD.
Maximum financial penalty that can be assessed against the provider for non-performance under this arrangement. Expressed in USD.
Detailed description of how the target metric is calculated, including data sources, calculation formulas, exclusions, and measurement period definitions.
End date of the performance measurement period during which provider performance is evaluated against target metrics.
Start date of the performance measurement period during which provider performance is evaluated against target metrics.
Frequency at which incentive payments or withhold releases are distributed to the provider.
Tiered performance level definition (e.g., bronze, silver, gold, platinum) that determines incentive payout or withhold release amounts.
Calendar year or contract year during which performance is measured for this incentive arrangement.
Boolean flag indicating whether the provider met the minimum quality threshold required for incentive payout or withhold release.
Identifies the quality measure framework used for performance evaluation (HEDIS, STAR, CAHPS, custom measures).
reference: NCQA HEDIS, CMS Star Ratings, CAHPS Standards

description: Composite quality performance score achieved by the provider during the measurement period, expressed as a percentage or index value.
Timestamp when this incentive arrangement record was first created in the system.
Timestamp when this incentive arrangement record was last modified in the system.
Frequency at which performance reports are generated and shared with the provider for transparency and tracking purposes.
Date when the final financial settlement of the incentive arrangement is completed and all payments or forfeitures are processed.
Percentage of cost overruns that will be shared by the provider under a shared risk arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).
Percentage of cost savings that will be shared with the provider under a shared savings arrangement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).
Specific service level agreement commitments that the provider must meet as part of this incentive arrangement (e.g., appointment availability, response time, member satisfaction).
Specific performance metric or key performance indicator (KPI) that the provider must achieve (e.g., readmission rate, preventive care completion, cost per member per month).
reference: HEDIS, CMS Star Ratings, Custom Quality Metrics

description: Date when the incentive arrangement ends and performance measurement ceases. Nullable for open-ended arrangements.
Total dollar amount withheld from provider reimbursement during the measurement period. Expressed in USD.
Name of the value-based care program under which this incentive arrangement is established (e.g., Medicare Shared Savings Program, Commercial Quality Incentive Program).
Percentage of provider reimbursement withheld for quality, utilization, or risk-sharing purposes. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).
Classification of the withhold pool indicating whether it is for quality performance, utilization management, risk-sharing, or a combined pool.
Business rules and conditions that must be met for withheld amounts to be released to the provider (quality gates, utilization targets, shared risk thresholds).

Unique surrogate identifier for the Value-Based Care (VBC) contract record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for all VBC contract master records including ACO agreements, shared savings, bundled payment, and risk-sharing arrangements.
reference: CMS MSSP Program; BPCI-A Program Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: VBC contracts are a subtype of provider contracts; linking them centralizes contract metadata and removes redundant columns.

Foreign key linking to plan.health_plan. Business justification: Required for Value‑Based Contract performance reporting per plan; VBC contracts are negotiated per health plan and need plan_id to generate plan‑specific shared‑savings reports.
Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: VBC_REPORTING: Value‑based contracts calculate shared savings against a risk pool; required for performance and settlement reports.

CMS-assigned unique identifier for the ACO participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) or other CMS Innovation Center models. Required for RAPS/EDPS submissions and CMS regulatory reporting.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR §425.118; CMS ACO Public Reporting

business_glossary_term: Accountable Care Organization (ACO) / Independent Practice Association (IPA) Entity Name
description: Legal name of the ACO, IPA, or provider organization that is the counterparty to this VBC contract. Used for regulatory reporting to CMS, network directory maintenance, and reconciliation settlement correspondence.
reference: CMS MSSP ACO Participation Agreement; 42 CFR §425; NCQA ACO Accreditation

business_glossary_term: ACO / IPA Entity Tax Identification Number (TIN)
description: Federal Tax Identification Number (TIN/EIN) of the ACO or IPA entity party to this contract. Required for CMS regulatory submissions, 1099 reporting, and settlement payment processing.
reference: IRS Publication 1635; CMS MSSP Reporting Requirements; HIPAA §1173

description: Total actual claims expenditure (in USD) incurred by attributed members or episode participants during the performance period. Compared against the benchmark expenditure target to determine shared savings or shared loss amounts.
reference: CMS MSSP Financial Reconciliation (42 CFR §425.604); BPCI-A Reconciliation Methodology

description: Date on which the most recent contract amendment became effective. Used to determine which contract terms apply to claims and reconciliation calculations for a given service date.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS MSSP Amendment Process

description: Number of health plan members attributed to this VBC contract/ACO entity for the current performance period. Used as the denominator for PMPM calculations, benchmark setting, and population health reporting.
reference: CMS MSSP Attribution Methodology (42 CFR §425.400); CMMI Attribution Rules

description: Method used to assign health plan members to this VBC contract/ACO entity for performance measurement. Prospective: members assigned at start of period based on prior utilization; Retrospective: members assigned at end of period based on actual utilization; Voluntary: member elects assignment.
reference: CMS MSSP Attribution (42 CFR §425.400); CMMI Attribution Methodology; NCQA ACO Accreditation Standards

description: Indicates whether this VBC contract is configured to automatically renew at the end of the current term without requiring a new execution. True = auto-renews; False = requires affirmative renewal action.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS MSSP Participation Agreement Renewal Terms

description: The risk-adjusted, trend-adjusted target expenditure amount (in USD) against which actual expenditures are compared during reconciliation. Calculated using historical claims data, risk scores (HCC/RAF), and applicable trend factors per the contract methodology.
reference: CMS MSSP Benchmark Methodology (42 CFR §425.600); BPCI-A Target Price Methodology; CMMI Benchmark Setting Guidelines

description: Specific CMS Innovation Center program track or model under which this contract operates (e.g., 'MSSP_BASIC_A', 'MSSP_BASIC_E', 'MSSP_ENHANCED', 'BPCI_A', 'CJR', 'PCF'). Null for commercial VBC contracts not affiliated with a CMS program. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSSP_BASIC_A|MSSP_BASIC_B|MSSP_BASIC_C|MSSP_BASIC_D|MSSP_BASIC_E|MSSP_ENHANCED|BPCI_A|CJR|PCF|DCE — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR §425; CMS BPCI-A Program; CMS CJR Model; CMMI Innovation Models

description: Sequential identifier for the most recent amendment to this VBC contract (e.g., 'AMD-003'). Tracks contract modifications including rate changes, term extensions, quality measure updates, and risk arrangement adjustments. Null for original, unamended contracts.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Amendment Standards; CMS MSSP Participation Agreement Amendment Process

description: Timestamp when this VBC contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: HIPAA §164.312(b) Audit Controls; NAIC Data Governance Standards; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: The risk-adjusted target price (in USD) for a single bundled payment episode. Calculated from historical claims data with applicable discounts and trend adjustments. Actual episode expenditures are compared to this target for reconciliation. Null for non-bundled VBC models.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Target Price Methodology; CMS CJR Target Price Calculation; CMMI Bundled Payment Pricing

description: The CPT procedure code or DRG (Diagnosis Related Group) code that initiates a bundled payment episode. For example, DRG 470 triggers a major joint replacement episode under BPCI-A. Null for non-bundled VBC models.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Trigger Definitions; AMA CPT Code Set; CMS DRG Grouper; ICD-10-PCS

description: For bundled payment contracts, the clinical episode category being bundled (e.g., 'joint_replacement', 'cardiac_surgery', 'maternity', 'hip_fracture', 'spinal_fusion'). Null for non-bundled VBC models. Determines the trigger event, episode window, and target price methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: joint_replacement|cardiac_surgery|maternity|hip_fracture|spinal_fusion|pneumonia|copd|stroke — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Definitions; CMS Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) Model; CMMI Bundled Payment Models

description: Total number of days in the bundled payment episode window, measured from the trigger event (e.g., 90 days post-discharge for BPCI-A). Defines the period during which all included services are attributed to the bundle for reconciliation.
reference: CMS BPCI-A Episode Window Definition; CMS CJR Model Episode Definition; CMMI Bundled Payment Design

business_glossary_term: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS) / STAR Measure Targets
description: Pipe-delimited list of HEDIS or CMS STAR measure identifiers and their contractual target thresholds included as quality gate requirements for this VBC contract (e.g., 'CBP:75.0|CDC-HbA1c:80.0|W34:85.0'). Supports quality performance monitoring and NCQA reporting.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS STAR Ratings Methodology; CMS MSSP Quality Measure Set

description: Line of business under which this VBC contract operates. Determines applicable regulatory framework, benchmark methodology, and quality measure set (e.g., HEDIS for commercial, STAR for Medicare Advantage, MSSP for fee-for-service Medicare). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare_advantage|medicaid|mssp|bpci_a|exchange|dual_eligible — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage (42 CFR §422); CMS MSSP (42 CFR §425); ACA §1311; NAIC LOB Classification

description: The maximum percentage of benchmark expenditure (expressed as a decimal) that can be assessed as shared loss against the ACO/provider entity. Caps the provider's downside financial exposure in two-sided risk arrangements.
reference: CMS MSSP Shared Loss Cap (42 CFR §425.606); CMMI Two-Sided Risk Parameters

description: The maximum percentage of benchmark expenditure (expressed as a decimal) that can be distributed as shared savings to the ACO/provider entity, regardless of actual savings achieved. Caps the health plan's financial exposure on the upside.
reference: CMS MSSP Shared Savings Cap (42 CFR §425.604(d)); CMMI Model Design Parameters

description: The minimum percentage threshold (expressed as a decimal) by which actual expenditures must exceed the benchmark before any shared loss is assessed. Applicable to two-sided risk arrangements. Symmetric counterpart to the Minimum Savings Rate (MSR).
reference: CMS MSSP MLR Definition (42 CFR §425.558); CMMI Two-Sided Risk Model

description: The minimum percentage threshold (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.02 = 2%) by which actual expenditures must fall below the benchmark before any shared savings are distributed. Protects against random variation triggering unearned payments. Required field for MSSP Track 1 and Track 1+ contracts.
reference: CMS MSSP MSR Definition (42 CFR §425.556); CMMI MSR Methodology

description: End date of the current performance measurement period. Marks the close of the measurement window after which actual expenditures are compared to the benchmark target for reconciliation and settlement.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR §425.502; BPCI-A Performance Period Definition

description: Start date of the current performance measurement period for this VBC contract. Defines the window during which attributed member expenditures, quality scores, and utilization are measured against the benchmark.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR §425.502; BPCI-A Performance Period Definition

description: Calendar or contract year for which performance is being measured and reconciled under this VBC contract. Used to track year-over-year performance trends, benchmark updates, and quality score progression. Typically a 4-digit year (e.g., 2024).
reference: CMS MSSP Performance Year Definition; BPCI-A Performance Period; CMMI Model Year Framework

description: Indicates whether the ACO/provider entity met the minimum quality performance threshold (quality gate) required to be eligible for shared savings distribution. True = quality gate satisfied; False = quality gate not met, shared savings withheld or reduced.
reference: CMS MSSP Quality Performance Standard (42 CFR §425.502); NCQA HEDIS Quality Gate Requirements

description: Composite quality performance score (expressed as a decimal percentage, e.g., 0.85 = 85%) achieved by the ACO/provider entity during the performance period. Derived from HEDIS measures, STAR ratings, or contract-specific quality metrics. May gate or adjust shared savings distribution.
reference: CMS MSSP Quality Performance Standard (42 CFR §425.502); NCQA HEDIS Measures; CMS STAR Ratings Methodology

description: Approach used to calculate and settle financial performance under this VBC contract. Prospective: target price set in advance; Retrospective: actual costs compared to historical benchmark after the period; Hybrid: combination of prospective target with retrospective quality adjustment.
reference: CMS MSSP Reconciliation (42 CFR §425.604); BPCI-A Reconciliation Methodology; CMMI Model Design

description: Current status of the financial reconciliation process for the performance period. Tracks the lifecycle from initial calculation through dispute resolution to final settlement: pending (not yet started), in_progress (under calculation), completed (finalized), disputed (under appeal), settled (payment processed).
reference: CMS MSSP Reconciliation Process (42 CFR §425.604); BPCI-A Reconciliation Methodology; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Specifies the financial risk-sharing structure between the health plan and the provider/ACO entity. Upside_only: provider shares in savings only; two_sided: provider shares in both savings and losses; full_risk: provider assumes full financial risk (global capitation); partial_risk: provider assumes a defined portion of risk.
reference: CMS MSSP Track 1/1+/2/3 Definitions; CMMI Risk Arrangement Framework; NAIC Risk-Sharing Model Act

description: Lower bound of the risk corridor (expressed as a decimal percentage below benchmark) within which no financial settlement occurs. Protects both parties from small random expenditure variations. Equivalent to the Minimum Savings Rate in MSSP terminology.
reference: CMS MSSP Risk Corridor Definition; CMMI Risk Corridor Framework; ACA §1342 Risk Corridor Program

description: Upper bound of the risk corridor (expressed as a decimal percentage above benchmark) beyond which the health plan absorbs excess losses (stop-loss protection). Limits the provider's maximum financial exposure in two-sided risk arrangements.
reference: CMS MSSP Risk Corridor Definition; CMMI Risk Corridor Framework; Stop-Loss Reinsurance Standards

description: The percentage (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.50 = 50%) of qualifying shared savings that is distributed to the ACO/provider entity. Negotiated contractually and may vary by quality score tier or performance band.
reference: CMS MSSP Sharing Rate (42 CFR §425.604(b)); CMMI Shared Savings Distribution

description: Date on which the shared savings distribution or shared loss recoupment payment was or is scheduled to be processed. Used for financial accrual, IBNR reserve release, and cash flow reporting.
reference: CMS MSSP Settlement Timeline; GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; SAP Statutory Accounting

description: Net shared savings (positive) or shared loss (negative) amount in USD calculated after reconciliation. Represents the financial settlement due to or owed by the ACO/provider entity based on performance against the benchmark. Positive = savings distribution; negative = loss recoupment.
reference: CMS MSSP Shared Savings Calculation (42 CFR §425.604); BPCI-A Reconciliation; CMMI Settlement Methodology

description: Maximum dollar amount of shared loss that the ACO/provider entity is obligated to repay in a given performance period. Provides downside protection and is a key negotiated term in two-sided risk VBC contracts.
reference: NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act; CMS MSSP Loss Sharing Cap; CMMI Risk Mitigation Provisions

description: Timestamp when this VBC contract record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: HIPAA §164.312(b) Audit Controls; NAIC Data Governance Standards; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: Classification of the VBC payment model governing this contract. Determines the financial risk structure and reconciliation methodology: shared_savings (upside only), shared_risk (two-sided), full_capitation (global budget), bundled_payment (episode-based), pay_for_performance (quality incentive). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shared_savings|shared_risk|full_capitation|bundled_payment|pay_for_performance|global_budget — promote to reference product if additional models are needed]
reference: CMS MSSP Track Definitions; BPCI-A Model Design; CMMI Alternative Payment Model Framework

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a VBC performance period record within the contract domain. Primary key for this entity.
reference: CMS MSSP Program Guidance; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Reference to the parent VBC contract under which this performance period is defined. Links the performance period to its governing contractual agreement.
reference: CMS MSSP Program Guidance; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Reference to the Accountable Care Organization (ACO) participating in this performance period. Supports CMS MSSP and commercial ACO reporting.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS ACO Program Guidance

description: Total actual claims expenditure (in USD) incurred for the attributed population during the performance period. Compared against the benchmark to determine savings or losses.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; GAAP; SAP

description: Actual expenditure expressed on a Per Member Per Month (PMPM) basis in USD. Compared against benchmark PMPM to assess cost efficiency of the ACO or provider entity.
reference: CMS MSSP Program Guidance; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Contractual or regulatory deadline by which the ACO or provider entity must file a formal appeal of the reconciliation results for this performance period.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.800; State DOI Regulations

description: Total number of members attributed to the ACO or provider entity for this performance period. Used as the denominator for Per Member Per Month (PMPM) calculations and benchmark setting.
reference: CMS MSSP Attribution Methodology 42 CFR Part 425 §425.400

description: The projected total expenditure benchmark (in USD) established at the start of the performance period, representing the expected cost of care for the attributed population. Basis for shared savings or loss calculations.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS Benchmark Methodology

description: Benchmark expenditure expressed on a Per Member Per Month (PMPM) basis in USD. Normalizes the benchmark for population size and enables cross-period and cross-ACO comparisons.
reference: CMS MSSP Benchmark Methodology; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Methodology used to update or set the expenditure benchmark for this performance period (e.g., historical trend, regional average, blended, prospective). Affects benchmark accuracy and ACO incentive alignment.
reference: CMS MSSP Benchmark Methodology 42 CFR Part 425 §425.601

business_glossary_term: Consumer Assessment of Healthcare Providers and Systems (CAHPS) Composite Score
description: Composite CAHPS survey score reflecting member experience with care during this performance period. Contributes to quality gate assessment and CMS Star Ratings.
reference: CAHPS Survey Standards; CMS MSSP Quality Reporting; NCQA HEDIS

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Program Track
description: CMS MSSP participation track for this performance period (e.g., BASIC levels A-E or ENHANCED). Determines risk model, savings rates, and quality reporting requirements.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.600; CMS MSSP Track Guidance

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Submission Date
description: Date on which performance period data was submitted to CMS for MSSP or other federal VBC program reporting. Required for regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS EDPS Submission Requirements

description: Timestamp when this performance period record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; NAIC Model Audit Rule

description: Last date of the measurement period. Expenditures and quality events occurring on or before this date are included in the performance calculation.
Difference between benchmark expenditure target and actual expenditure (in USD). Positive value indicates savings; negative value indicates losses. Drives shared savings or shared loss determination.
Percentage of expected Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) diagnoses that were documented and coded during the performance period. Impacts RAF score accuracy and benchmark integrity.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS/EDPS

business_glossary_term: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS) Composite Score
description: Composite HEDIS measure score for the attributed population during this performance period. Contributes to the overall quality score and CMS quality reporting requirements.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS MSSP Quality Reporting

business_glossary_term: Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) Reserve Amount
description: Actuarial reserve (in USD) for claims Incurred But Not Reported (IBNR) as of the performance period end date. Included in actual expenditure calculations to ensure completeness of cost measurement.
reference: GAAP ASC 944; SAP SSAP No. 55; Milliman MG-ALFA Reserving Standards

description: Line of Business (LOB) associated with this performance period (e.g., Medicare, Medicaid, Commercial, Marketplace, D-SNP). Determines applicable regulatory requirements and benchmark methodologies.
reference: CMS Program Guidance; NAIC Model Laws; State DOI Regulations

description: The minimum percentage threshold of losses relative to the benchmark that triggers shared loss liability for the ACO under two-sided risk models. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.02 = 2%).

The minimum percentage threshold of savings relative to the benchmark that must be achieved before the ACO qualifies for shared savings. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.02 = 2%). Required by CMS MSSP program rules.

Final net dollar amount payable to or recoverable from the ACO or provider entity after applying shared savings, shared losses, quality adjustments, and any withholds. Positive = payment to provider; negative = recovery from provider.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; GAAP; SAP

description: Free-text field for capturing supplemental context, reconciliation commentary, dispute details, or operational notes related to this performance period.

Reimbursement methodology governing this performance period. Determines how savings, losses, and risk are allocated between the health plan and the ACO or provider entity. reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS Innovation Center VBC Models; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Externally-known business identifier for this performance period, formatted as PP-YYYY-NNN. Used in CMS submissions, ACO reporting, and contract correspondence.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Calendar or contract year (e.g., 2024) for which performance is measured. Used for year-over-year trend analysis and CMS annual reconciliation cycles.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS Innovation Center VBC Models

description: Current lifecycle state of the performance period. Tracks progression from open measurement through reconciliation, settlement, and potential dispute resolution.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Classification of the measurement period (e.g., annual, semi-annual, quarterly, interim, final). Determines the reconciliation cadence and reporting obligations.
reference: CMS MSSP Program Guidance; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Dollar amount of adjustments applied to this performance period resulting from corrections or true-ups from a prior performance period. Positive = additional payment; negative = recovery.
reference: GAAP ASC 944; SAP SSAP No. 55; CMS MSSP Reconciliation Guidance

description: Indicates whether the ACO or provider entity met the minimum quality performance threshold required to qualify for shared savings distribution. True = quality gate satisfied.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.502; NCQA HEDIS

description: Composite quality performance score for the ACO or provider entity during this period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.85 = 85%). Derived from HEDIS, CAHPS, and CMS quality measures. May gate or adjust shared savings eligibility.
reference: CMS MSSP Quality Reporting; NCQA HEDIS; CAHPS Survey Standards

description: Average Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score for the attributed population during this performance period. Reflects the relative health risk of the population and is used to normalize the benchmark expenditure target.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS/EDPS; 42 CFR Part 422

description: Date on which the financial reconciliation calculation was completed and results were finalized for this performance period.
Current status of the financial reconciliation process for this performance period. Tracks progression from initial calculation through dispute resolution to final settlement.
Defines the risk-sharing arrangement for this period. One-sided models allow only shared savings; two-sided models expose the ACO to both savings and losses.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425; CMS Innovation Center VBC Models

description: Date on which the net settlement payment was made to or recovered from the ACO or provider entity. Null if settlement has not yet occurred.
Calculated dollar amount of shared losses owed by the ACO or provider entity for this performance period (in USD). Positive value only; zero if loss threshold not met or one-sided model.
The contractually agreed percentage of qualifying losses that the ACO or provider entity is liable to repay under two-sided risk arrangements. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.30 = 30%).
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.610; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Calculated dollar amount of shared savings earned by the ACO or provider entity for this performance period (in USD). Positive value only; zero if savings threshold not met.
The contractually agreed percentage of qualifying savings that is distributed to the ACO or provider entity. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.50 = 50%). Varies by risk track and quality performance.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.610; NCQA VBC Standards

description: CMS Star Rating assigned to the health plan or ACO for this performance period. Influences quality bonus payments, shared savings eligibility, and member-facing plan comparisons.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Star Ratings Program; 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart D

description: First date of the measurement period during which provider performance and expenditures are tracked against the benchmark target.
Timestamp when this performance period record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; NAIC Model Audit Rule

description: Dollar amount withheld from shared savings distribution pending quality performance validation or regulatory review. Released upon satisfaction of quality gate or other contractual conditions.
reference: CMS MSSP 42 CFR Part 425 §425.610; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the capitation arrangement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the parent provider contract under which this capitation arrangement is established. Links to the contract master record.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capitation arrangements are budgeted and tracked via a cost center to manage per‑member‑per‑month payments and related expenses.
Reference to the health plan or benefit plan product associated with this capitation arrangement. Determines the member population eligible for attribution.
reference: CMS Plan Benefit Package (PBP) Requirements; ACA Section 1301

description: Reference to the capitated provider or provider group receiving the Per Member Per Month (PMPM) payment under this arrangement.
reference: CMS NPI Registry; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 162

description: Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: CAPITATION_PAYMENT: Each capitation arrangement draws funding from a designated risk pool for compliance and financial reporting.

Indicates whether this capitation arrangement is part of an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) or integrated delivery network model, which may have additional shared savings or risk-sharing components.
reference: CMS Medicare Shared Savings Program 42 CFR Part 425; CMS Innovation Center ACO Models

description: Total dollar amount for the attributed member population above which the health plan assumes financial responsibility for claims costs. Protects the provider from population-level adverse risk. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act

description: Identifier of the most recent contract amendment that modified this capitation arrangement. Tracks the amendment history for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Contractually agreed annual percentage increase applied to the PMPM rate at each rate renewal period (e.g., 3.50 for 3.5% annual escalation). Used for multi-year capitation arrangements.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement was formally approved by the health plan's contracting authority. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: Name or employee identifier of the contracting authority who approved this capitation arrangement. Supports audit trail and delegation-of-authority compliance.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care Capitation - HMO Commercial 2024'), used in reporting and provider communications.

Externally-known business identifier for the capitation arrangement, used in provider communications, payment remittances, and reconciliation processes. Assigned by the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Current lifecycle status of the capitation arrangement. Controls whether PMPM payments are generated and whether members can be attributed to this arrangement.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Classification of the capitation arrangement by scope of covered services. Global capitation covers all services; primary care capitation covers PCP services only; specialty capitation covers specialist services; institutional covers facility-based care. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global_capitation|primary_care_capitation|specialty_capitation|institutional_capitation|behavioral_health_capitation|pharmacy_capitation — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Current number of members attributed to this capitation arrangement for the active payment period. Used to calculate total monthly capitation payment disbursement.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NCQA Attribution Methodology Standards

description: Methodology used to attribute members to this capitation arrangement. PCP assignment uses enrollment data; claims-based uses plurality of visits; voluntary selection allows member choice; geographic assigns by service area.
reference: CMS Medicare Shared Savings Program Attribution Rules; NCQA PCMH Standards

description: Indicates whether this capitation arrangement automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the contractually specified notice period.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; State DOI Contract Filing Requirements

description: Description of services explicitly excluded from the capitation scope and reimbursed separately (e.g., 'Mental Health, Substance Abuse, Transplants, Neonatal ICU'). Carve-outs are paid fee-for-service or under separate capitation arrangements.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312 Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement becomes binding and PMPM payments begin. Aligns with the contract effective period.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Description or code of the geographic service area (county, state, or region) within which this capitation arrangement applies. Members outside this area may not be attributed to this arrangement.
reference: CMS Service Area Requirements 42 CFR Part 422.2; State DOI Service Area Filings

description: Dollar amount per member per year above which the health plan assumes financial responsibility for claims costs, protecting the provider from catastrophic individual member costs. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act

description: Line of Business to which this capitation arrangement applies (e.g., Commercial HMO, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid Managed Care). Determines regulatory requirements and rate-setting methodology.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations 42 CFR Part 422; CMS Medicaid Managed Care 42 CFR Part 438; ACA Section 1301

description: Network tier classification of the provider under this capitation arrangement. Tier 1 providers typically receive higher capitation rates in exchange for meeting quality and cost-efficiency benchmarks.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards 42 CFR Part 438.68; NCQA Network Accreditation Standards

description: Frequency at which capitation payments are disbursed to the provider. Monthly is standard for most capitation arrangements; quarterly or annual may apply for certain specialty or institutional arrangements.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Specific payment calculation methodology applied to this capitation arrangement. Pure capitation pays flat PMPM; withhold arrangements retain a percentage pending performance; risk-adjusted capitation applies RAF multipliers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pure_capitation|capitation_with_withhold|capitation_with_bonus|risk_adjusted_capitation|tiered_capitation — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Risk Adjustment 42 CFR Part 422.308; NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications

description: Fixed dollar amount paid to the provider per attributed member per calendar month, regardless of services rendered. The core financial parameter of the capitation arrangement. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

business_glossary_term: Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate Currency
description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PMPM rate (typically USD for domestic health plans). Required for multi-currency reporting and regulatory submissions.

Description of the quality performance benchmarks or HEDIS/STAR measure thresholds that must be met for the provider to receive release of withheld capitation amounts. Defines the performance contract terms.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS Star Ratings Methodology; CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Program

description: Multiplier applied to the base PMPM rate to account for the relative health risk of the attributed member population. A RAF above 1.0 indicates a sicker-than-average population; below 1.0 indicates healthier-than-average.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS/EDPS Submission Requirements

description: Date on which the current PMPM rate became effective. Supports rate history tracking when rates are renegotiated mid-contract without creating a new arrangement record.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

description: Date on which the current PMPM rate expires and is subject to renegotiation or automatic escalation. Null if the rate is open-ended for the arrangement term.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

description: Indicates whether the PMPM rate is subject to risk adjustment based on the attributed member population's Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scores. Applicable primarily for Medicare Advantage and ACA Exchange arrangements.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment 42 CFR Part 422.308; HHS HCC Risk Adjustment Methodology

description: Indicates whether the provider participates in a shared risk pool under this capitation arrangement. Risk pool participants share in surpluses or deficits relative to the capitation budget.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Risk-Sharing Standards

description: Percentage of risk pool surplus or deficit allocated to the provider under this capitation arrangement. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Applicable only when risk_pool_participant is True.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Risk-Sharing Standards

description: Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the covered service categories included under this capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care, Preventive Services, Lab, Radiology'). Defines the scope of services the provider is financially responsible for under capitation.
reference: CMS Essential Health Benefits 45 CFR Part 156; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this capitation arrangement record was sourced (e.g., FACETS, QNXT for Core Administration Platform; CACTUS or PROVIDER_SOURCE for Provider Management System).
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; EDI HIPAA 834 Transaction Standards

description: Native record identifier from the operational source system (Facets, QNXT, or Provider Management System) for this capitation arrangement. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and back-referencing to the system of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164 Audit Controls

description: Type of stop-loss protection included in the capitation arrangement. Individual stop-loss limits exposure per member; aggregate stop-loss limits total population exposure; both applies both thresholds.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement ends and PMPM payments cease. Null for open-ended arrangements. Distinct from contract termination date when partial termination applies.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Number of calendar days advance notice required by either party to terminate this capitation arrangement without cause. Typically 90–180 days for managed care capitation contracts.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards; State DOI Requirements

description: Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312 Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether this capitation arrangement includes Value-Based Care (VBC) performance incentive components such as quality bonuses, shared savings, or outcome-based adjustments beyond the base PMPM.
reference: CMS Innovation Center VBC Models; NCQA HEDIS Quality Measures

description: Percentage of the PMPM rate withheld from each payment cycle pending performance or quality metric achievement. Withheld amounts are returned upon meeting contractual benchmarks. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Program; NCQA HEDIS Performance Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each capitation payment disbursement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ensures each capitation payment is tied to the employee who authorized it, supporting payment audit and regulatory compliance.
Reference to the specific capitation arrangement or rate schedule within the contract, identifying the applicable PMPM rate table, risk pool configuration, and attributed population definition.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Managed Care Plan Network Adequacy Act

description: Reference to the capitation contract under which this payment is made. Links to the provider contract master defining the capitation arrangement, PMPM rates, and risk-sharing terms.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Reference to the health plan or benefit product under which the attributed members are enrolled. Determines the applicable benefit structure and capitation rate tier.
reference: CMS Plan Benefit Package (PBP) Standards; ACA Section 1301 QHP Requirements

description: Reference to the original capitation payment record that this record adjusts, reverses, or supplements. Populated only when prior_period_adjustment_flag is True. Enables audit trail linking of adjustment chains.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954; Internal Audit Standards

description: Reference to the provider (PCP, specialist group, IPA, or ACO) receiving the capitation payment. Corresponds to the credentialed provider entity in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).
reference: NPI Registry; CMS Provider Enrollment; NAIC Provider Standards

description: Accounts Payable (AP) voucher or invoice number assigned in Oracle Financials when the capitation payment is posted as a payable. Links the capitation disbursement to the AP subledger for financial reporting and audit trail.
reference: GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107; Oracle Financials AP Module Standards

description: Total number of members attributed to the provider for the payment period. This is the primary driver of the gross capitation payment calculation (attributed_member_count × pmpm_rate = gross_payment_amount).
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NCQA Attribution Methodology Standards

description: Rate tier or member category applied to determine the PMPM rate for this payment (e.g., adult, pediatric, senior, ESRD, dual-eligible). Different member demographics may attract different PMPM rates under the same contract.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; CMS Medicare Advantage Rate Announcement

description: Check number or EFT/ACH trace number assigned by the financial institution for the actual disbursement of this capitation payment. Used for bank reconciliation in Oracle Financials (AP module) and provider payment dispute resolution.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation E; GAAP ASC 230

description: Timestamp when this capitation payment record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for payment processing cycles.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 2 Type II Audit Standards; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this capitation payment record. Typically USD for domestic health insurance operations. Required for multi-currency financial reconciliation and GAAP/SAP reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency; SAP Statement 23

description: Dollar adjustment to the gross payment resulting from retroactive enrollment changes (late enrollments, disenrollments, or eligibility corrections) identified after the initial payment calculation. Can be positive (additional payment) or negative (recoupment).
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; HIPAA 834 Transaction Standards

description: General Ledger (GL) cost center code in Oracle Financials to which this capitation payment expense is allocated. Supports financial reporting by LOB, product, and organizational unit per GAAP and SAP statutory reporting requirements.
reference: GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; Oracle Financials GL Standards

description: Total gross capitation payment amount before any adjustments, calculated as attributed_member_count multiplied by the pmpm_rate. Represents the base payment obligation for the period prior to retroactive enrollment changes, risk pool withholds, or stop-loss recoveries.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107

description: Line of Business (LOB) under which the capitation payment is made. Determines regulatory requirements, rate-setting methodology, and CMS reporting obligations applicable to the payment.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations 42 CFR Part 422; CMS Medicaid Managed Care 42 CFR Part 438; ACA Section 1301

description: Final net amount disbursed to the provider after all adjustments: gross_payment_amount + enrollment_adjustment_amount - risk_pool_withhold_amount + stop_loss_recovery_amount + other_adjustment_amount. This is the actual amount reflected in the 835 ERA remittance advice.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107

description: Catch-all adjustment amount for miscellaneous contractual adjustments not captured in enrollment, risk pool, or stop-loss categories. Includes quality bonus payments, performance incentive adjustments, or administrative fee offsets per contract terms.
reference: CMS Value-Based Care Program Standards; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards

description: Date on which the capitation payment was formally approved for disbursement by the authorized approver in the billing or finance system. Supports segregation of duties controls and audit trail requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 1 Type II Controls; GAAP Internal Control Standards

description: Actual date the capitation payment was disbursed to the provider via EFT, check, or ACH. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and provider remittance reporting.
reference: ERISA Section 503; CMS Prompt Payment Standards; NAIC Prompt Pay Model Act

description: Mechanism by which the capitation payment was or will be disbursed to the provider. EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) and ACH (Automated Clearing House) are most common for capitation arrangements.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; CMS Electronic Payment Standards

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this capitation payment disbursement, used in provider remittance advice (835 ERA), accounts payable records, and provider portal references. Format: CAP-YYYYMM-NNNNNN.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; CMS Remittance Advice Standards

description: Last day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the last day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards

description: First day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the first day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards

description: Identifier of the capitation payment run or batch cycle that generated this payment record. Used to group all payments processed in the same cycle for reconciliation, audit, and reprocessing purposes in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: Internal Payment Processing Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Current lifecycle state of the capitation payment disbursement. 'pending' = calculated but not yet approved; 'approved' = authorized for payment; 'paid' = disbursed to provider; 'voided' = cancelled before disbursement; 'reversed' = clawed back after disbursement; 'on_hold' = suspended pending investigation or contract dispute.
reference: NAIC Model Managed Care Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Classification of the capitation payment by its business purpose. 'standard' = routine monthly capitation; 'retroactive' = payment for prior periods due to enrollment changes; 'adjustment' = correction to a previously issued payment; 'stop_loss_recovery' = reimbursement under stop-loss provisions; 'risk_pool_settlement' = periodic risk pool distribution or withhold settlement.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Risk-Sharing Model Standards

description: Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate applied for this payment period, as defined in the contract rate schedule. This is the contractually negotiated amount paid per attributed member per month. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary contract pricing.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Plan Standards; ERISA Section 408

description: Indicates whether this capitation payment includes or represents an adjustment to a prior payment period. True when the payment corrects or supplements a previously issued capitation payment due to retroactive enrollment changes or rate corrections.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954 Prior Period Adjustments

description: Amount withheld from the capitation payment pending achievement of quality performance targets (e.g., HEDIS measures, STAR ratings thresholds). Released or forfeited at the end of the performance measurement period based on provider quality scores.
reference: CMS STAR Ratings Program; NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Payment Program

description: Reference number of the 835 Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) transaction sent to the provider for this capitation payment. Used to reconcile capitation disbursements with provider accounts receivable and the EDI clearinghouse (Availity/Change Healthcare) transaction log.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; CMS Remittance Advice Standards

business_glossary_term: Risk-Adjusted Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate
description: PMPM rate after application of the risk adjustment factor (RAF score) to the base contractual PMPM rate. Reflects the risk-adjusted capitation amount per member per month for Medicare Advantage or risk-adjusted commercial arrangements.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Methodology; 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart G; CMS Medicare Advantage Rate Announcement

description: Amount withheld from the gross capitation payment and placed into a shared risk pool per the contract terms. Risk pool withholds are held and distributed (or forfeited) at the end of the risk settlement period based on actual utilization versus targets.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Risk-Sharing Model Standards

description: Aggregate Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score for the attributed member population used to adjust the capitation rate for this payment period. Derived from Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) coding and CMS Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS/EDPS) submissions.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Methodology; HCC Model; RAPS/EDPS Submission Standards; 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart G

description: Operational system of record from which this capitation payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and reconciliation between the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT), Billing System (HealthEdge), and Oracle Financials.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; Internal Data Governance Standards; SOC 2 Type II

description: Native identifier of this capitation payment record in the originating operational system (Facets, QNXT, or HealthEdge). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver Layer and the system of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164

description: Amount recovered from or credited to the provider under stop-loss provisions of the capitation contract. Positive values indicate plan reimbursement to provider for catastrophic cases exceeding the stop-loss threshold; negative values indicate provider liability adjustments.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Stop-Loss Insurance Model Act

description: Timestamp when this capitation payment record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Tracks the most recent update for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and reconciliation purposes.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 2 Type II Audit Standards; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: Indicates whether this capitation payment is made under a Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) contract arrangement, as opposed to a standard fee-for-service capitation. Drives VBC performance reporting and quality incentive calculations.
reference: CMS Innovation Center VBC Models; CMS ACO Regulations 42 CFR Part 425; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Business reason code or description explaining why this capitation payment was voided or reversed. Populated only when payment_status is 'voided' or 'reversed'. Examples: contract_termination, duplicate_payment, enrollment_error, rate_correction.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards; Internal Audit Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the fee schedule record in the contract domain. Primary key for the fee_schedule data product.
Reference to the provider contract under which this fee schedule is established. Links the fee schedule to its governing contractual agreement.
Reference to the specific health plan or benefit plan to which this fee schedule is scoped. Enables plan-level fee schedule assignment in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.
reference: Internal Plan Configuration Standards; CMS Plan ID Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Fee schedules generate revenue entries that must be posted to a designated ledger for accurate financial statements.
FK to contract.provider_contract.provider_contract_id — Fee schedules must resolve to their governing provider contract for rate lookup during claim adjudication and contract compliance auditing.
Reference to the provider or provider group for whom this fee schedule defines reimbursement rates. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).
reference: NPI Registry; NPPES National Plan and Provider Enumeration System

description: Reference to the provider network (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS) to which this fee schedule applies. Determines in-network vs. out-of-network reimbursement applicability.
reference: Internal Network Management Standards; CMS Network Adequacy Standards

description: Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: RATE_DEVELOPMENT: Fee schedules are applied within a specific risk pool; linking enables actuarial rate calculations and regulatory filings.
Reference to the fee_schedule_id of the newer fee schedule that replaced this one when schedule_status is 'superseded'. Enables fee schedule version chain navigation and historical rate lookups.

Identifier for the contract amendment that introduced or modified this fee schedule version. Null for the original fee schedule. Enables traceability between fee schedule versions and contract amendments.
Indicates whether this fee schedule includes anesthesia service reimbursement, which uses base units plus time units multiplied by a conversion factor rather than standard CPT-based rates.
reference: AMA CPT Anesthesia Guidelines; CMS Anesthesia Fee Schedule

description: Dollar amount per anesthesia unit (base units + time units) used to calculate anesthesia reimbursement when anesthesia_applicable is True. Negotiated rate specific to this fee schedule.
reference: AMA CPT Anesthesia Guidelines; CMS Anesthesia Fee Schedule

description: Date on which the fee schedule was formally approved by the authorized contracting officer or governance body. Required for audit and regulatory compliance.
reference: Internal Contract Governance Policy; State DOI Filing Requirements

description: The CMS Medicare fee schedule calendar year used as the basis or benchmark for this fee schedule when fee_schedule_source is 'cms_medicare'. Enables tracking of which CMS annual update cycle the rates are derived from.
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Annual Updates; CMS IPPS Annual Rule

description: Dollar amount multiplied by the total Relative Value Units (RVUs) to calculate the reimbursement amount for RVU-based fee schedules. Applicable when payment_basis is 'rvu_based'. Sourced from CMS RBRVS conversion factor or negotiated contract rate.
reference: CMS RBRVS; AMA RVU Guidelines; CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule

description: Indicates whether Coordination of Benefits (COB) rules apply to claims adjudicated under this fee schedule. When True, COB logic determines primary vs. secondary payer responsibilities.
reference: NAIC COB Model Regulation; CMS COB Guidelines; HIPAA COB Standards

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range End
description: Ending CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_start to define the scope of applicable procedure codes.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set; CMS HCPCS Level II Codes

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range Start
description: Starting CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_end to define the scope of applicable procedure codes. Null if the fee schedule applies to all CPT codes or uses ICD/DRG-based scoping.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set; CMS HCPCS Level II Codes

description: Timestamp when this fee schedule record was first created in the system. Audit field capturing the initial record creation event in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Audit Policy; HIPAA Security Rule Audit Controls

description: Indicates whether this fee schedule uses Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based reimbursement for inpatient facility claims. When True, DRG weights and base rates govern payment rather than individual procedure codes.
reference: CMS DRG Classification System; CMS IPPS (Inpatient Prospective Payment System)

description: The negotiated base dollar rate per DRG weight unit used for inpatient facility reimbursement when drg_applicable is True. Multiplied by the DRG relative weight to calculate the total inpatient payment.
reference: CMS IPPS DRG Base Rate; CMS Medicare DRG Rates

description: Date on which the fee schedule becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication and reimbursement calculations. Inclusive start date of the fee schedule validity period.
reference: CMS Fee Schedule Effective Date Standards; HIPAA 837 Transaction Standards

description: Free-text narrative description of the fee schedule, including its purpose, scope, special provisions, or notes relevant to adjudication staff and contract analysts.

Identifies the origin or benchmark source of the fee schedule rates: cms_medicare (based on CMS Medicare fee schedule), cms_medicaid (based on state Medicaid fee schedule), negotiated (directly negotiated with provider), custom (health plan proprietary rates), rbp_benchmark (Reference Based Pricing benchmark).
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule; CMS Medicaid Fee Schedule; Internal Contracting Policy

description: Indicates whether geographic adjustment factors (e.g., CMS Geographic Practice Cost Indices - GPCI) are applied to rates under this fee schedule. True when locality-based adjustments are in effect; False for flat national rates.
reference: CMS Geographic Practice Cost Indices (GPCI); CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule

description: Indicates whether global surgery payment rules apply under this fee schedule, bundling pre-operative, intra-operative, and post-operative services into a single reimbursement amount.
reference: CMS Global Surgery Policy; AMA CPT Global Surgery Guidelines

description: Identifies the Line of Business (LOB) to which this fee schedule applies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace, COBRA, FEHB). Determines which member populations and plan types are subject to this fee schedule during adjudication.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations; ACA Marketplace Standards; NAIC LOB Classification

description: CMS Medicare locality code identifying the geographic pricing area applicable to this fee schedule when geographic_modifier_applicable is True. Used to apply GPCI adjustments to RVU-based rates.
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Locality Codes; CMS GPCI

description: Indicates whether this fee schedule applies to Medicare crossover claims where Medicare is the primary payer and the health plan is the secondary payer. Relevant for Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans.
reference: CMS Medicare Secondary Payer (MSP) Regulations; CMS Crossover Claim Guidelines

description: Indicates whether CPT or HCPCS modifiers affect reimbursement rates under this fee schedule (e.g., modifier 26 for professional component, modifier TC for technical component, modifier 50 for bilateral procedures).
reference: AMA CPT Modifier Guidelines; CMS HCPCS Modifier Codes

description: Dollar threshold above which a claim qualifies as a cost outlier, triggering additional reimbursement beyond the standard DRG or bundled payment. Applicable for institutional fee schedules with outlier payment provisions.
reference: CMS IPPS Outlier Payment Policy; CMS Medicare Outlier Regulations

description: The methodology used to calculate reimbursement amounts under this fee schedule.
